title: Fix InvarDB silent database loading failures
description: CRITICAL: InvarDB.jl lines 479, 508, 574 — read_invar_perms, read_invar_rules, and LoadInvarDB return empty Dict/struct with only @warn when DB files are missing. Downstream PermToInv/InvToPerm fail with confusing 'Case not found' errors. Fix: throw IOError or DomainError on missing DB files instead of returning empty. Include full path and remediation.
close_reason: read_invar_perms, read_invar_rules, and LoadInvarDB now throw on missing files/dirs instead of silent @warn. Tests updated.

title: Fix XTensor parser operator precedence bug (line 1351)
description: CRITICAL/ACTIVE BUG: In XTensor.jl line 1351, `pos > n || s[pos] != '[' && error(...)` has wrong operator precedence. When pos > n, it short-circuits to true without error, then next line accesses s[pos] causing BoundsError. Fix: wrap condition in parentheses `(pos > n || s[pos] != '[') && error(...)`.
close_reason: Fixed operator precedence bug: (pos > n || s[pos] != '[') now correctly parenthesized. Added 2 parser error tests.

title: EPIC: Tracer Bullet — End-to-end single-task validation
description: Thinnest possible vertical slice through the entire Elegua architecture. One TOML test case flows through: pyproject.toml scaffold → EleguaTask instantiation → WolframAdapter execution → Layer 1 identity comparison → pass/fail result. Proves the extraction architecture works before building out breadth. Success criteria: 'uv run pytest tests/test_tracer.py' passes with a single DefTensor+Contract round-trip validated against Wolfram oracle.
close_reason: Created in wrong repo, moving to elegua

title: Add textbook GR correctness regression suite for JOSS claims
description:
Source: /var/home/sasha/Downloads/xact-docs.md

Execution mode
- AFK

Problem
- The review recommended explicit, reviewer-grade benchmark problems (Bianchi identities, Schwarzschild, FLRW, independent-component counts).
- Existing issues cover some pieces (sxAct-i2er for second Bianchi, sxAct-96ry for property tests), but there is no single tracked task for a concrete textbook regression suite that substantiates README/docs/paper examples.

Traceability
- README.md trust-story and status claims
- docs/src/status.md
- docs/src/notebooks/carroll_schwarzschild_julia.md
- docs/src/notebooks/wald_cosmology_julia.md
- sxAct-3hc7 (JOSS paper)
- Related issues: sxAct-i2er, sxAct-96ry

Scope
- Add a focused regression suite for a small set of canonical textbook checks, such as:
  - algebraic Riemann symmetries / first Bianchi identity
  - Schwarzschild vacuum checks (for example Ricci = 0 where supported)
  - Schwarzschild Kretschmann scalar benchmark
  - one FLRW Ricci-tensor or scalar-curvature check
  - independent-component count or equivalent symmetry reduction sanity check
- Reuse existing notebook/example material where possible, but promote the checks into automated tests.
- Link or defer sub-parts already tracked elsewhere instead of duplicating them.

Start here
- Create one failing regression test first for a currently documented Schwarzschild or FLRW claim.
- Prefer a dedicated test entry point such as test/joss_gr_regressions.jl or the nearest existing XTensor/xCoba test file.
- Reuse notebook formulas from the Carroll/Wald docs pages where possible.

Files / systems
- test/
- README.md
- docs/src/status.md
- docs/src/notebooks/carroll_schwarzschild_julia.md
- docs/src/notebooks/wald_cosmology_julia.md
- src/XTensor.jl and/or xCoba-related implementation files as needed

Acceptance criteria
- [ ] At least 3 canonical GR correctness checks run in automated tests.
- [ ] The suite references textbook or upstream-xAct expectations in comments/docs.
- [ ] Overlap with sxAct-i2er and sxAct-96ry is resolved explicitly (reuse or close the gap without duplicate assertions).
- [ ] README/docs/paper can point to these tests as part of the trust story.

Verification
- julia --project=. test/runtests.jl
- julia --project=docs docs/make.jl

Workflow mandate
- Follow TDD and Tidy First: write failing tests first, then implement or tighten the required machinery.

title: Decide and document XAct.jl branding strategy relative to upstream xAct
description:
Source: /var/home/sasha/Downloads/xact-docs.md

Execution mode
- AFK unless the outcome requires a human naming/branding decision or external coordination

Problem
- External review flagged potential reviewer/user confusion because `XAct.jl` reuses the established upstream xAct name.
- The repo already acknowledges the upstream Wolfram project, but there is no explicit tracked decision about whether the current name remains, whether a qualifier/tagline is sufficient, or whether JOSS-facing copy needs stronger disambiguation.

Scope
- Review README.md, docs/src/index.md, docs/src/naming.md, docs/src/wolfram-migration.md, and paper/paper.md when it exists.
- Coordinate the outcome with sxAct-3hc7 so the JOSS paper uses the same positioning.
- Decide the project stance: keep the current name with explicit compatibility wording, or plan a rename/alias strategy.
- Record the rationale in user-facing docs and in the JOSS paper so reviewers do not mistake the project for the original Mathematica package.

Acceptance criteria
- [ ] A documented decision exists on naming/branding strategy.
- [ ] README and docs clearly distinguish the Julia reimplementation from upstream Wolfram xAct.
- [ ] paper.md includes the same positioning and cites upstream appropriately.
- [ ] If a rename or alias is required, a follow-up HITL issue is opened and linked.

Verification
- Inspect README/docs wording for consistent upstream disambiguation.
- Confirm the JOSS paper issue/reference reflects the same decision.

Workflow mandate
- Follow TDD and Tidy First where code/docs changes need tests or link checks.

title: Update oracle version pin from xAct 1.2.0 to 1.3.0 and regenerate snapshots
description:
After the 1.2.0→1.3.0 audit is complete: update the oracle VERSION file (oracle/VERSION) to pin xAct 1.3.0, rebuild oracle Docker service (docker compose build oracle), re-run the full sxact parity test suite against the new oracle, regenerate any snapshots that changed due to 1.3.0 behavior differences, and document any regressions. Update docs/src/status.md to reflect the new oracle baseline.

Ref: resources/xAct directory (xTensor 1.3.0, xPerm 1.2.4, xPert 1.0.6, etc.).
Breaking-change list from docs/migration/xact-1.3.0-analysis.md (output of sxAct-nd7v):

1. xPerm 1.2.4 changes RightTransversal behavior
   - bugfix adds missing SortCycles
   - expect canonical representative / ordering drift in some permutation outputs
   - rerun Butler examples and downstream canonicalization snapshots carefully

2. xTensor 1.3.0 adds InertHeadArgumentCount and changes inert-head behavior
   - expressions with inert heads may print, validate, or reconstruct differently
   - watch for snapshot drift in inert-head-heavy tests

3. xTensor 1.3.0 extends / fixes complex metrics on inner vbundles
   - previously unsupported or partially supported behavior can now differ legitimately
   - if we have no coverage yet, add focused oracle cases after the baseline migration

4. No removals detected in implemented-module public symbol sets
   - this looks like an additive / bugfix upgrade, not a large API deletion event
   - most migration effort is therefore expected to be snapshot drift analysis, not wrapper breakage

5. Ticket text discrepancy: upstream xAct_1.3.0.tgz still contains xPert 1.0.6, not 1.1.0
   - adjust migration expectations and documentation accordingly

Recommended rerun order: xPerm → xTensor → xCoba → xPert → xTras → Invar

title: Audit xAct 1.2.0 → 1.3.0 changelog and identify breaking changes
description:
Review the xAct 1.3.0 release notes and diff the 1.2.0 vs 1.3.0 API surface for the modules we have implemented (xCore, xPerm, xTensor, xCoba, xPert, xTras, Invar). Identify: (1) breaking changes to existing behavior, (2) new functions/options added, (3) new packages bundled in 1.3.0. Produce a short written summary to guide the oracle update and implementation roadmap.

Output: docs/migration/xact-1.3.0-analysis.md — paste the breaking-change list into sxAct-ltaa description before ltaa starts.

Note: This is a human-in-the-loop task (HITL). Human review of the summary is required before sxAct-ltaa can begin.

title: [epic] Migrate sxAct oracle and parity tests to xAct 1.3.0
description: xAct released version 1.3.0 on December 29, 2025. The current oracle/test infrastructure is pinned to xAct 1.2.0. This epic covers auditing the changes, updating the version pin, and ensuring all parity tests continue to pass against the new version. Also captures any new API surface from 1.3.0 that should inform the implementation roadmap. Supersedes sxAct-bsd. Ref: specs/2026-03-09-xact-migration-master-plan.md

title: Cut stable release tag (e.g. v0.1.0) for JOSS submission
description:
Registrator.jl requires a tagged semver release before it can register a package in the Julia General Registry. This is a human step.

Steps:
1. Ensure HEAD of main is in a stable state
2. git tag v0.1.0 && git push origin v0.1.0
3. Verify the tag appears on GitHub

Done when: a semver tag (v0.x.x) exists on the main branch.

title: Register XAct.jl in Julia General Registry
description: The package is not registered in the Julia General Registry, so users cannot install via ]add XAct. Need to use Registrator.jl to submit a registration PR. Prerequisite: a stable release tag must exist. This blocks discoverability and is required before JOSS submission (editors check installability).

title: Write paper.md and paper.bib for JOSS submission
description:
JOSS requires a ≤1000-word narrative paper (paper.md) and a bibliography (paper.bib). These do not exist yet and are mandatory for submission.

Target: paper/paper.md and paper/paper.bib (create paper/ subdirectory if needed).
Done when: JOSS whedon checker returns no errors and word count ≤ 1000.

The paper must include:
- Statement of need (open-source Julia alternative to proprietary Mathematica xAct)
- Summary of functionality (xPerm, xTensor, xCoba, xInvar)
- CRediT-style author contributions statement
- AI attribution disclosure per emerging JOSS norms

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Architecture tradeoff: string-based engine vs Symbolics.jl integration addressed (known limitation, future direction)
- [ ] Scope table: xPerm/xTensor/xCoba/xInvar (done) vs Spinors/xTerior/Harmonics (planned)
- [ ] License provenance: algorithms implemented from published papers (Martín-García 2008, Manssur-Portugal 2002), not from GPL source
- [ ] AI attribution disclosure included
- [ ] Word count ≤ 1000
- [ ] paper.bib includes: Martín-García 2008 (xPerm), Manssur-Portugal 2002, Peeters 2007 (cadabra2), Wald 1984, Carroll 2004, Carlsson & Ekre 2019 (Tensors.jl)

title: Fix just test to run Julia test extras correctly
description:
Context
- While verifying sxAct-eo57, `just test` failed before project tests ran.
- The current recipe invokes `julia --project=. test/runtests.jl`, which bypasses Julia test-target activation, so extras like Aqua/JET are unavailable unless manually installed into the main environment.

Problem
- Repository quality gate `just test` is unreliable in a fresh/dev environment.
- This creates false negatives unrelated to the working ticket and weakens the required verification path.

Suggested direction
- Update the Julia test recipe to use `Pkg.test()` or otherwise activate the test target so extras are available consistently.
- Verify Aqua/JET/Literate load correctly through the supported test entrypoint.

Discovered while working on sxAct-eo57 (Schwarzschild notebook trustworthiness).
close_reason: Completed

title: Create shared notebook smoke-test workflow for docs notebooks
description:
Context
- Source: notebook review performed on 2026-04-24.
- Parent program: notebook review and upgrade program.
- Repeated issue-review finding: many notebook tickets require smoke tests or automated execution checks, but the repository does not track a shared notebook-validation task.

Problem
- Notebook tickets repeatedly require execution coverage, docs build validation, or smoke tests.
- Without a shared validation path, each notebook ticket may reinvent execution helpers or omit regression protection.
- Current tests in test/julia/test_docs.jl cover Literate examples but not the Jupyter notebook suite.

Goals
- Create one shared notebook smoke-test workflow for docs notebooks.
- Make per-notebook tickets able to reference a common validation path instead of inventing bespoke scripts.
- Keep the initial implementation small and CI-friendly.

Files
- test/julia/test_docs.jl
- docs/make.jl
- notebooks/julia/*.ipynb
- notebooks/julia/*.qmd
- notebooks/python/basics.ipynb
- notebooks/python/basics.qmd
- supporting scripts/helpers as needed

Proposed improvements
- Decide the authoritative execution path for notebook smoke tests.
- Add a reusable helper that can extract/execute notebook code or otherwise validate notebook examples.
- Cover at least the Julia notebook suite first; document the Python validation approach explicitly.
- Provide one documented command that notebook tickets can use for verification.

Acceptance criteria
- [ ] A shared smoke-test workflow exists for notebook examples.
- [ ] The workflow has a documented command for local execution.
- [ ] At least one automated test path exercises notebook execution outside the current Literate examples.
- [ ] Julia notebook validation is covered; Python notebook validation strategy is explicitly documented.

Verification
- `julia --project=. test/runtests.jl`
- `julia --project=docs docs/make.jl`

Workflow mandate
- Follow TDD and Tidy First.
- Start by adding a failing test/scaffold for notebook execution before implementing helpers.
close_reason: Completed

title: Turn relativistic fluids notebook into a rigorous conservation-law example
description:
Context
- Source: notebook review performed on 2026-04-24.
- Parent program: sxAct-l4xp.
- Shared validation prerequisite: sxAct-0bua.
- Notebook: notebooks/julia/physics_fluids.ipynb
- Authoritative source to edit: notebooks/julia/physics_fluids.qmd
- Generated docs artifact: docs/src/notebooks/physics_fluids_julia.md
- Current assessment: topic is worth having, but the current notebook is not yet sufficiently rigorous or impactful.

Primary scope for this ticket
- Phase 1 only: make the notebook mathematically disciplined as a generic relativistic-fluid derivation.
- In scope: velocity normalization, sign conventions, projector-based split of `∇_a T^{ab} = 0`, and one validated derived relation.
- Out of scope for this ticket: FLRW specialization or broader cosmology integration.

Problem
- The notebook claims to verify conservation laws and derive Euler equations, but the current code does not fully support those claims.
- It does not enforce or discuss the normalization `u^a u_a = -1`.
- It does not clearly use the projection machinery needed to separate energy conservation from momentum conservation in a principled way.

Goals
- Make the fluid notebook mathematically disciplined and physically meaningful.
- Show how `∇_a T^{ab} = 0` splits into recognizable equations.
- Ensure the notebook ends with a concrete, validated symbolic takeaway.

Files
- notebooks/julia/physics_fluids.qmd
- notebooks/julia/physics_fluids.ipynb
- docs/make.jl
- docs/src/notebooks/physics_fluids_julia.md
- test/julia/test_docs.jl

Start here
- Use the shared notebook validation path from sxAct-0bua.
- Start with a failing validation for the intended normalization/projection result.

Proposed improvements
- Explicitly state and use the velocity normalization condition.
- Introduce the spatial projector `h_ab = g_ab + u_a u_b` if that matches the chosen sign convention.
- Separate the conservation law into energy and momentum projections in a way that mirrors textbook derivations.
- End with one validated derived relation rather than an unverified broader claim.

Acceptance criteria
- [ ] The notebook no longer claims derivations it does not actually perform.
- [ ] Velocity normalization and sign conventions are explicit.
- [ ] The split between energy and momentum conservation is presented in a mathematically coherent way.
- [ ] At least one derived relation is validated or clearly tied to a textbook equation.
- [ ] The notebook leaves the reader with a concrete symbolic takeaway, not just a template.
- [ ] The notebook is executed by the shared smoke-test path from sxAct-0bua.

Verification
- `julia --project=. test/runtests.jl`
- `julia --project=docs docs/make.jl`
- Run the shared notebook smoke-test command introduced in sxAct-0bua for `notebooks/julia/physics_fluids.qmd`

Workflow mandate
- Follow TDD and Tidy First.
- Tighten the mathematics before adding any additional narrative or visualization.
close_reason: Completed: fluid notebook now states the timelike normalization/sign convention explicitly, introduces the spatial projector, records the standard energy and momentum projection templates honestly, and ends with the projector/normalization orthogonality takeaway with refreshed docs/test coverage.

title: Turn curved-spacetime electromagnetism notebook into a verified example
description:
Context
- Source: notebook review performed on 2026-04-24.
- Parent program: sxAct-l4xp.
- Shared validation prerequisite: sxAct-0bua.
- Notebook: notebooks/julia/physics_em.ipynb
- Authoritative source to edit: notebooks/julia/physics_em.qmd
- Generated docs artifact: docs/src/notebooks/physics_em_julia.md
- Current assessment: topic is worth having, but the current notebook is not yet strong enough as a physics-validation example.

Primary scope for this ticket
- Phase 1 only: convert the notebook from a notation demo into a verified symbolic EM example by checking core identities.
- In scope for this ticket: Faraday antisymmetry and the homogeneous Maxwell/Bianchi identity.
- Out of scope unless it falls out naturally: full curved-spacetime wave-equation derivation and concrete physical specialization.

Problem
- The notebook currently reads more like a notation/setup demo than a true verification notebook.
- It defines the vector potential and Faraday tensor expression, but it does not convincingly verify key statements such as antisymmetry or the Bianchi identity.
- The prose claims stronger verification than the code currently provides.

Goals
- Turn the notebook into a trustworthy EM-in-curved-spacetime symbolic demonstration.
- Add explicit validation cells for the core field-theory identities.
- Make the narrative honest about what is symbolically shown versus what is physically interpreted.

Files
- notebooks/julia/physics_em.qmd
- notebooks/julia/physics_em.ipynb
- docs/make.jl
- docs/src/notebooks/physics_em_julia.md
- test/julia/test_docs.jl

Start here
- Use the shared notebook validation path from sxAct-0bua.
- Begin with failing tests/validation cells for antisymmetry and the Bianchi identity.

Proposed improvements
- Add a validation that `F_ab` is antisymmetric.
- Add a validation of the homogeneous Maxwell equation / Bianchi identity, e.g. `∇_[a F_bc] = 0`.
- Narrow the Lorenz-gauge wave-equation discussion to an explanatory note unless it is actually demonstrated by the code.

Acceptance criteria
- [ ] The notebook no longer overstates what has been verified.
- [ ] Faraday antisymmetry is explicitly checked in code.
- [ ] The homogeneous Maxwell/Bianchi identity is explicitly checked in code.
- [ ] Any discussion of the Lorenz-gauge wave equation is clearly labeled explanatory unless also derived.
- [ ] The distinction between symbolic derivation and illustrative narrative is clear throughout.
- [ ] The notebook is executed by the shared smoke-test path from sxAct-0bua.

Verification
- `julia --project=. test/runtests.jl`
- `julia --project=docs docs/make.jl`
- Run the shared notebook smoke-test command introduced in sxAct-0bua for `notebooks/julia/physics_em.qmd`

Workflow mandate
- Follow TDD and Tidy First.
- Reduce overclaiming prose before adding broader physical examples.
close_reason: Completed

title: Correct and verify 2-sphere foundations notebook
description:
Context
- Source: notebook review performed on 2026-04-24.
- Parent program: sxAct-l4xp.
- Shared validation prerequisite: sxAct-0bua.
- Notebook: notebooks/julia/foundations_sphere.ipynb
- Authoritative source to edit: notebooks/julia/foundations_sphere.qmd
- Generated docs artifact: docs/src/notebooks/foundations_sphere_julia.md
- Current assessment: worth keeping, but it currently overclaims curvature verification and includes at least one misleading conceptual statement.

Problem
- The notebook claims to verify the Ricci scalar of the 2-sphere, but the reviewed execution path left `RicciScalarCD[]` symbolic rather than numerically/explicitly confirming the textbook result.
- The section on the Einstein tensor / 2D geometry needs conceptual correction; in 2D the Einstein tensor vanishes identically, and the current prose risks confusing that with statements about the Ricci tensor.
- This undermines trust in one of the most conceptually important notebooks.

Goals
- Make the sphere notebook mathematically correct and visibly verified.
- Use it as a high-trust demonstration of intrinsic curvature.
- Remove or correct any misleading statements about 2D curvature identities.

Files
- notebooks/julia/foundations_sphere.qmd
- notebooks/julia/foundations_sphere.ipynb
- docs/make.jl
- docs/src/notebooks/foundations_sphere_julia.md
- test/julia/test_docs.jl

Start here
- Use the shared notebook validation path from sxAct-0bua.
- Add a failing validation for the corrected curvature claim before revising the narrative.

Proposed improvements
- Replace vague “expected 2.0” style output with an actual validation path for the sphere curvature claim.
- If full component evaluation is awkward, restructure the notebook so it explicitly distinguishes symbolic identities from component-level verification.
- Correct the 2D Einstein-tensor discussion and explain the relevant identity carefully.
- Consider adding Gaussian curvature `K = 1/R^2` as the most intuitive geometric quantity.

Acceptance criteria
- [ ] The notebook no longer claims a curvature verification it does not actually perform.
- [ ] The 2D curvature discussion is mathematically correct and pedagogically clear.
- [ ] At least one explicit validation cell checks a textbook result for the sphere.
- [ ] The notebook clearly separates symbolic algebra from component/numerical evaluation.
- [ ] Any references to Einstein, Ricci, and scalar curvature are internally consistent.
- [ ] The notebook is executed by the shared smoke-test path from sxAct-0bua.

Verification
- `julia --project=. test/runtests.jl`
- `julia --project=docs docs/make.jl`
- Run the shared notebook smoke-test command introduced in sxAct-0bua for `notebooks/julia/foundations_sphere.qmd`

Workflow mandate
- Follow TDD and Tidy First.
- Correct the conceptual statements before adding any new visual polish.
close_reason: Completed

title: Make Schwarzschild notebook a high-trust flagship example
description:
Context
- Source: notebook review performed on 2026-04-24.
- Parent program: sxAct-l4xp.
- Shared validation prerequisite: sxAct-0bua.
- Notebook: notebooks/julia/carroll_schwarzschild.ipynb
- Authoritative source to edit: notebooks/julia/carroll_schwarzschild.qmd
- Generated docs artifact: docs/src/notebooks/carroll_schwarzschild_julia.md
- Current assessment: absolutely worth keeping; topic is high-value and motivating; current notebook overclaims what it verifies.

Primary scope for this ticket
- Phase 1 only: make the notebook honest and high-trust by validating one concrete Schwarzschild quantity and tightening the effective-potential interpretation.
- Chosen validation target: compare the sampled Schwarzschild geometry against one textbook diagnostic quantity, preferably the Kretschmann scalar for `M=1`; if implementation constraints prevent that exact target, explicitly document the fallback and narrow the claim accordingly.

Problem
- The notebook samples metric components at one point and then speaks as if the vacuum solution has been verified globally.
- The “derived Einstein tensor” section does not currently establish the physical claim that Schwarzschild satisfies the vacuum equations outside the source.
- The effective potential visualization is useful, but the notebook needs a sharper separation between symbolic derivation, coordinate instantiation, and physical interpretation.

Goals
- Turn this into a high-trust flagship notebook.
- Validate one genuinely diagnostic Schwarzschild result.
- Make the narrative explicit about what is symbolic, what is coordinate-specific, and what is physical interpretation.

Files
- notebooks/julia/carroll_schwarzschild.qmd
- notebooks/julia/carroll_schwarzschild.ipynb
- docs/make.jl
- docs/src/notebooks/carroll_schwarzschild_julia.md
- test/julia/test_docs.jl

Start here
- Use the shared notebook validation path from sxAct-0bua.
- Add a failing validation for the chosen primary diagnostic quantity before revising the narrative.

Proposed improvements
- Remove or narrow any prose that implies a global vacuum proof from pointwise component assignment.
- Validate one textbook Schwarzschild quantity in code, preferably the Kretschmann scalar for `M=1`.
- Keep the effective potential section, but tie the plotted features directly to textbook claims: event horizon, photon sphere at `r=3M`, ISCO at `r=6M`.
- Add Carroll chapter/section/equation references where practical.

Acceptance criteria
- [ ] The notebook no longer implies a global vacuum verification from a pointwise component assignment alone.
- [ ] One textbook Schwarzschild quantity is explicitly validated in code.
- [ ] The effective potential section identifies and interprets physically meaningful radii correctly.
- [ ] The prose clearly distinguishes symbolic identity, coordinate representation, and physical interpretation.
- [ ] Reference to Carroll chapter/section/equation numbers is added where practical.
- [ ] The notebook is executed by the shared smoke-test path from sxAct-0bua.

Verification
- `julia --project=. test/runtests.jl`
- `julia --project=docs docs/make.jl`
- Run the shared notebook smoke-test command introduced in sxAct-0bua for `notebooks/julia/carroll_schwarzschild.qmd`

Workflow mandate
- Follow TDD and Tidy First.
- Clean up overclaiming prose before adding more examples or visuals.
close_reason: Completed: notebook now validates the Kretschmann scalar at the sampled radius, narrows Schwarzschild vacuum claims, and marks/interprets the horizon, photon sphere, and ISCO with refreshed docs/test coverage.

title: Make FLRW cosmology notebook rigorous and trustworthy
description:
Context
- Source: notebook review performed on 2026-04-24.
- Parent program: sxAct-l4xp.
- Shared validation prerequisite: sxAct-0bua.
- Notebook: notebooks/julia/wald_cosmology.ipynb
- Authoritative source to edit: notebooks/julia/wald_cosmology.qmd
- Generated docs artifact: docs/src/notebooks/wald_cosmology_julia.md
- Current assessment: worth keeping because FLRW cosmology is a core use case; however, the current notebook overstates derivation and relies on illustrative scale-factor curves that are not clearly presented as approximations.

Primary scope for this ticket
- Choose the symbolic path, not the numerical ODE path.
- Make this notebook a symbolic FLRW geometry notebook that connects curvature quantities to the Friedmann equations, rather than a mixed symbolic/numerical notebook.

Problem
- The notebook says it derives Friedmann equations, but the current code does not convincingly do so.
- The plotted scale-factor functions for `k = ±1` are simplified placeholders, yet the prose can be read as stronger physical validation than is warranted.
- The notebook currently blurs symbolic derivation and illustrative cosmology plotting.

Goals
- Make the notebook honest, rigorous, and impactful.
- Rework it around symbolic FLRW curvature/Einstein-tensor content.
- Remove or relabel any illustrative content that is not derived from the chosen symbolic path.

Files
- notebooks/julia/wald_cosmology.qmd
- notebooks/julia/wald_cosmology.ipynb
- docs/make.jl
- docs/src/notebooks/wald_cosmology_julia.md
- test/julia/test_docs.jl

Start here
- Use the shared notebook validation path from sxAct-0bua.
- Add a failing validation for one symbolic FLRW result before revising the notebook narrative.

Proposed improvements
- Derive or inspect FLRW curvature quantities and explicitly connect them to the Friedmann equations.
- Remove ad hoc scale-factor curves unless they are clearly labeled “illustrative only”; preferred outcome is to delete them in favor of symbolic results.
- Add textbook references to Wald chapter/section/equation numbers where feasible.

Acceptance criteria
- [ ] The notebook clearly uses symbolic derivation as its primary mode.
- [ ] The notebook no longer presents ad hoc curves as if they were validated physical solutions.
- [ ] At least one core FLRW or Friedmann-related symbolic result is explicitly computed and checked.
- [ ] Any remaining illustrative content is clearly labeled and contextually justified.
- [ ] The final notebook leaves the reader with a trustworthy understanding of what was actually shown.
- [ ] The notebook is executed by the shared smoke-test path from sxAct-0bua.

Verification
- `julia --project=. test/runtests.jl`
- `julia --project=docs docs/make.jl`
- Run the shared notebook smoke-test command introduced in sxAct-0bua for `notebooks/julia/wald_cosmology.qmd`

Workflow mandate
- Follow TDD and Tidy First.
- Reduce ambiguity first; only then add richer cosmology content.
close_reason: Completed: FLRW notebook now follows a symbolic geometry path, validates the spatial Ricci scalar 6k/a^2 at a sample scale factor, connects that curvature term to the first Friedmann equation, and removes misleading ad hoc evolution plots.

title: Parser Phase B.2: Update serializer, canonicalizer, and key-hashing for CovDFactor
description:
**Phase B.2 of openspec unify-expression-parser. Parent: sxAct-m0c6. Depends on Phase B.1.**

Update all consumers of the AST to handle the new CovDFactor type.

**Tasks:**
1. Update `_term_key_str` to serialize CovDFactor as `"CD[-a][V[-b]]"` format (recursive)
2. Update `_serialize` / `_serialize_terms` to emit CovD bracket notation
3. Update `_canonicalize_term` to recursively canonicalize the inner operand of CovDFactor
4. Add test: `ToCanonical("CD[-a][S[-c,-b]]")` → `"CD[-a][S[-b,-c]]"` (inner canonicalized)
5. Add test: round-trip idempotency on CovD expressions
6. Add test: mixed CovD+product `ToCanonical("CD[-a][V[-b]] S[-d,-c]")` → correct result
7. Add test: CovD of sum raises error
8. Add fuzz tests: random CovD expressions survive ToCanonical round-trip
9. Verify full test suite passes (zero regressions)
close_reason: Deployed in 17146a2. Tasks 1-7,9 were completed in Phase B.1 (87edfdc). Task 8 (fuzz tests) added: 4 CovD fuzz suites (400 trials), all 5351 fuzz tests pass. Phase B complete.

title: Parser Phase B.1: Introduce TensorFactor/CovDFactor/FactorNode AST types
description:
**Phase B.1 of openspec unify-expression-parser. Parent: sxAct-m0c6. Depends on Phase A.**

Introduce the unified AST type system and update the parser to produce it.

**Tasks:**
1. Rename `FactorAST` to `TensorFactor` across all files (~65 references in 5 files)
2. Define `CovDFactor(covd_name::Symbol, deriv_index::String, operand::Vector{FactorNode})`
3. Define `const FactorNode = Union{TensorFactor, CovDFactor}`
4. Update `TermAST` to use `Vector{FactorNode}`
5. Update `_parse_monomial` to recognize `Name[idx][operand]` as CovD when `CovDQ(Name)` is true
6. Factor out inner coefficient to outer TermAST (e.g., `CD[-a][3 V[-b]]` → coeff=3, operand=[V[-b]])
7. Reject CovD of sums with explicit error (e.g., `CD[-a][V[-b] + W[-b]]`)
8. Reject non-CovD double brackets with parse error

**Key design decisions (from design.md):**
- operand is a monomial (Vector{FactorNode}), coefficient lives at outer TermAST level
- Union{TensorFactor, CovDFactor} for zero-overhead dispatch
- CovDQ registration check distinguishes CovD application from syntax errors
close_reason: Deployed in 87edfdc. TensorFactor/CovDFactor/FactorNode types introduced. Parser produces CovDFactor for CD[-a][T[-b]] syntax. ToCanonical round-trips CovD expressions with recursive inner canonicalization. 6 new tests, zero regressions (573 xtensor + 156 xperm Julia, 52 adapter Python, 353 TOML regression all pass).

title: Parser Phase A: Add CovD bracket detection guard in _parse_monomial
description:
**Phase A of openspec unify-expression-parser. Parent: sxAct-m0c6.**

Eliminate silent data loss in ToCanonical by detecting CovD bracket syntax and raising an explicit error.

**Tasks:**
1. In `_parse_monomial` (Canonical.jl:170): after parsing `Name[indices]`, if next char is `[`, raise error: "CovD bracket syntax CD[...][...] is not supported by ToCanonical. Use SortCovDs/CommuteCovDs for covariant derivative expressions."
2. Add test: `ToCanonical("CD[-a][V[-b]]")` raises error (not silently drops)
3. Add test: `Contract("CD[-a][V[-b]]")` raises error
4. Verify existing CovD tests still pass (SortCovDs/CommuteCovDs use separate parser)
5. Verify full XTensor + fuzz test suites pass

**Scope:** ~10 lines of production code + 2 test cases. Zero risk — SortCovDs/CommuteCovDs are unaffected.
close_reason: Deployed in 567ab18. Guard raises error on CovD bracket syntax in ToCanonical/Contract. Verified by test_core.jl tests and CI.

title: [epic] Unify XTensor expression parsers (openspec: unify-expression-parser)
description:
Unify the three CovD code paths in Canonical.jl into a single AST. See openspec/changes/unify-expression-parser/ for full proposal, design, and spec.

**Three phases:**
- Phase A: Error guard — detect CovD brackets in ToCanonical and error instead of silent drop
- Phase B: Unified AST — TensorFactor + CovDFactor union, parser + serializer + canonicalizer updates
- Phase C: Retire string-based parser — rewrite SortCovDs/CommuteCovDs, migrate _preprocess_covd_reductions, remove dead code

Resolves sxAct-nl2r (ToCanonical silently drops CovD operands).

title: Bug: ToCanonical silently drops CovD operands — parser can't represent nested brackets
description:
**Found during fuzz test review (2026-03-31).**

`ToCanonical("CD[-a][V[-b]]")` returns `"CD[-a]"` — the operand `V[-b]` is silently dropped.

**Root cause:** The expression parser (`_parse_monomial` in Canonical.jl:170) uses `FactorAST(tensor_name, indices)` which is a flat structure. After parsing `CD[-a]`, it hits `[` which isn't a letter, so the factor loop breaks and `[V[-b]]` vanishes without error.

There are **two parallel parsers** in XTensor:
1. `_parse_expression` → `FactorAST` (flat `T[-a] V[-b]` products) — used by ToCanonical, Contract, Simplify
2. `_extract_covd_chain` (nested `CD[-a][CD[-b][T[-c]]]` brackets) — used by SortCovDs, CommuteCovDs

The first parser has no concept of 'operand application'. Expressions with CovD pass through ToCanonical silently corrupted.

**Impact:** High — any expression containing CovD that enters ToCanonical (including via Contract/Simplify) loses derivative information. The `_preprocess_covd_reductions` at the top of ToCanonical handles metric_compatibility and second_bianchi as STRING rewrites before parsing, but arbitrary CovD expressions are mangled.

**Fix options:**
1. **AST extension**: Add `CovDFactorAST(covd_name, deriv_index, operand::Vector{FactorAST})` to the AST and teach the parser to recognize `Name[idx][...]` as application syntax
2. **Unified parser**: Merge the two parsers into one that handles both flat products and nested CovD brackets
3. **Guard**: At minimum, detect and error on CovD bracket syntax in `_parse_monomial` instead of silently dropping it
close_reason: Superseded by epic sxAct-m0c6 (unify expression parsers). Phase A (sxAct-xh47) adds immediate error guard; Phases B+C do full fix.

title: Safety: Fork-safety runtime guard and documentation
description:
**Anti-persona risk from adversarial evaluation (2026-03-31):** juliacall is not fork-safe. Users who wrap sxAct in multiprocessing.Pool (natural for batch computation) risk segfaults or silently wrong results from corrupted Julia heap state. No documentation warns against this. The thread lock in _bridge.py protects against threading but not forking.

**What needs to be done:**
1. Add runtime detection in `_runtime.py`: on import, check if `os.getpid()` differs from the PID that initialized Julia — if so, raise RuntimeError with clear message
2. Add fork-safety warning to installation.md and api.py docstring
3. Document recommended alternative: use `concurrent.futures.ProcessPoolExecutor` with spawn (not fork) start method, or use threading
4. Add a test that verifies the guard fires on fork

**Why:** Silent wrong results from corrupted heap state are worse than crashes. A researcher's training loss or published calculation could be silently corrupted. The fix is cheap; the risk is catastrophic.
close_reason: Implemented fork-safety guard in _runtime.py with PID tracking. 7 tests passing.

title: Tests: Fuzz/generative testing for canonicalization
description:
**Tiger risk from adversarial evaluation (2026-03-31):** Snapshot-only oracle verification has blind spots — any symmetry combination not in the TOML corpus is unverified. A silent correctness bug in canonicalization would destroy the project's 'verified correctness' reputation.

**What needs to be done:**
1. Implement property-based fuzz testing: canonicalize(random_permutation(expr)) == canonicalize(expr) for thousands of random expressions
2. Generate random tensor expressions with varying symmetry types (Symmetric, Antisymmetric, RiemannSymmetry, YoungSymmetry, GradedSymmetric) and verify canonicalization idempotency
3. Test combinations of symmetry + covariant derivative commutation that are not in existing TOML test cases
4. Add round-trip invariant checks: expr != 0 implies canonicalize(expr) != 0 (guards against trivial zeroing)

**Why:** The verification framework is sxAct's core differentiator. Snapshot tests verify known cases; fuzz tests verify the algorithm itself. This directly addresses the evaluation's top kill risk.
close_reason: Created test_xtensor_fuzz.jl with 3350 property-based tests across 20 testsets covering idempotency, symmetry, anti-zeroing, linearity, contract, simplify, Riemann orbit, products, metric trace, Weyl tracelessness. All passing.

title: Session: Add session kwarg to ToCanonical/Contract/Simplify
description:
**Engineer critique from adversarial evaluation (2026-03-31):** The Session migration is half-finished. def_*! functions all accept `; session::Session` kwarg, but the three most-used reader functions — ToCanonical, Contract, Simplify — accept no session parameter. They read from module-level globals exclusively.

**What needs to be done:**
1. Add `; session::Session=_default_session[]` kwarg to ToCanonical, Contract, Simplify
2. Thread session through ~115 internal callsites (_parse_expression, _canonicalize_term, _contract_term, etc.)
3. Update CommuteCovDs, SortCovDs, IBP, VarD similarly
4. Add tests: create two isolated Sessions with different metrics, verify ToCanonical produces correct results for each
5. Update Python API to pass session through juliacall bridge

**Why:** Without this, two notebooks sharing a Julia kernel silently cross-contaminate. The Session struct exists but is useless for the operations users care about most. This is the #1 blocker for reliable notebook/multi-user deployment.

title: Split XTensor.jl into include() subfiles
description: XTensor.jl is a God Object: 5514 lines, 187 functions, 19+ module-level mutable containers, 9+ semantic subsystems. Decompose into 7 include()-based subfiles within the same Julia module (zero API breakage). Proposed split: Core.jl (~1200 lines: registries, def_*, accessors, Session), Canonicalization.jl (~1500 lines: ToCanonical, Simplify, TermAST/FactorAST), Contract.jl (~700 lines: metric contraction, trace rules, Einstein expansion), CovD.jl (~600 lines: CommuteCovDs, SortCovDs, IBP, TotalDerivativeQ), Perturbation.jl (~500 lines: perturb_curvature, VarD, Euler-Lagrange), Coba.jl (~500 lines: BasisChangeObj, CTensorObj, Christoffel, ToBasis/FromBasis), Identities.jl (~300 lines: Bianchi, RegisterIdentity!, _apply_identities!). All 567 existing tests must continue to pass. Priority score 27 (system-wide, high change amplification).
notes: Verification: julia --project=. test/julia/test_xtensor.jl (567 tests). All subfiles share module scope via include() so cross-references work without import changes. Strategy: extract bottom-up (Identities first, then Coba, Perturbation, CovD, Contract, Canonicalization, leaving Core as the residual).
close_reason: Split into src/XTensor/{Core,Def,Canonical,Contract,Pert,Coba,XTras}.jl. All 567 XTensor + 156 XPerm + 238 TExpr tests pass.

Covers: XTensor.jl God Object decomposition, adapter file splits, TExpr coupling reduction, and optional cleanup of translation/recognizer modules. Sequencing: break cycles first (none found), then God Object decomposition, then shotgun surgery consolidation, then interface extractions.
notes: Parallelism: x8ln, pgjn, ckzw are independent and can be worked in parallel. lriv depends on x8ln. gs7w and vaoj are independent backlog items.

title: Phase E: Dimension-aware InvarDB cache with keyed dispatch tables
description:
Fix _ensure_invar_db to cache per-dimension and key dispatch caches by (dim, case) so dim=3 and dim=4 rules don't collide.

**TDD: Write failing tests first:**
- Load dim=4 DB, then call InvSimplify with dim=3 → must use dim=3 step-5 rules (currently uses dim=4)
- After reset_state!(), cache must be fully cleared for all dims
- Two sequential calls with different dims must return different DB instances

**Implementation:**
1. Change _invar_db from Union{Nothing,InvarDB} to Dict{Int,InvarDB} keyed by dim
2. Change _perm_dispatch to Dict{Tuple{Int,Vector{Int}}, Dict{Vector{Int},Int}} keyed by (dim, case)
3. Change _dual_perm_dispatch similarly
4. Update _ensure_invar_db to check haskey(_invar_db_cache, dim)
5. Update _ensure_case_dispatch to include dim in dispatch key
6. Update _reset_invar_db!() to empty! all three caches
7. Thread dim parameter through InvSimplify → _ensure_invar_db call chain

**Files:** src/XInvar.jl (lines 1660-1665, 2080-2116)
**Ref:** plans/2026-03-20-architecture-hardening.md#phase-e
**Tickets resolved:** sxAct-9b1d
**Contributes to:** sxAct-2cr2
close_reason: Dimension-keyed InvarDB cache prevents dim collision

title: Phase D: Bracket-aware _swap_indices to fix substring corruption
description:
Replace naive replace() in _swap_indices with bracket-aware substitution that only swaps labels inside [...] groups, leaving tensor names untouched.

**TDD: Write failing tests first:**
- _swap_indices('Tab[-a,-ab]', 'a', 'b') must yield 'Tab[-b,-ab]' (not 'Tbb[-b,-bb]')
- _swap_indices('T[-a,-b]', 'a', 'b') must yield 'T[-b,-a]' (basic swap works)
- _swap_indices('Rab[-a,-b]', 'a', 'b') must yield 'Rab[-b,-a]' (tensor name Rab untouched)
- _swap_indices('V[a]', 'a', 'b') must yield 'V[b]' (label at end of bracket)

**Implementation:**
1. Parse expression into outside-bracket and inside-bracket segments
2. Only apply label replacement inside bracket groups
3. Use word-boundary-aware regex: labels bounded by [, ], comma, -, or whitespace
4. Use $ (end anchor) not \$ in regex

**Files:** src/XTensor.jl (lines 4889-4898)
**Ref:** plans/2026-03-20-architecture-hardening.md#phase-d
**Tickets resolved:** sxAct-qmng
**Contributes to:** sxAct-sf4t
close_reason: Bracket-aware _swap_indices prevents substring corruption

title: Phase C: Fix Einstein trace type and parameterize numeric structs
description:
Fix _trace_scalars Int→Rational{Int} and parameterize BasisChangeObj/CTensorObj to eliminate Matrix{Any} type instability.

**TDD: Write failing tests first:**
- def_metric! with dim=3, then Contract('EinsteinM3[a,b] * gM3[-a,-b]') must yield '-(1//2) * RicciScalarM3[]' (currently yields '0')
- BasisChangeObj with Float64 matrix must have eltype(obj.matrix) == Float64 (currently Any)
- CTensorObj with Int array must have eltype(obj.array) == Int (currently Any)

**Implementation:**
C1 — Fix Einstein trace:
- Change const _trace_scalars type from Dict{Symbol,Tuple{Symbol,Int}} to Dict{Symbol,Tuple{Symbol,Rational{Int}}}
- Change line 1198: coeff = 1 - n // 2 (was 1 - div(n, 2))
- Update _auto_create_curvature! and Contract trace substitution accordingly

C2 — Parameterize numeric types:
- BasisChangeObj{T<:Number} with Matrix{T} fields
- CTensorObj{T<:Number,N} with Array{T,N} field
- Update _basis_changes dict to Dict{Tuple{Symbol,Symbol}, BasisChangeObj{<:Number}}
- Update _ctensors dict to Dict{Vector{Symbol}, CTensorObj{<:Number}}

**Risk:** Changes struct definitions. No serialization layer exists so no backward compat concern. All constructors infer T automatically.

**Files:** src/XTensor.jl (lines 180-196, 245-246, 260, 1198, 2530-2544)
**Ref:** plans/2026-03-20-architecture-hardening.md#phase-c
**Tickets resolved:** sxAct-le8b, sxAct-kcci
**Contributes to:** sxAct-z1gp
close_reason: Einstein trace fixed (Rational coeff), BasisChangeObj/CTensorObj parameterized

title: Phase B: Safe Julia bridge with typed arg builders
description:
Replace raw seval string interpolation in both api.py and julia_stub.py with typed argument builders that make injection impossible by construction.

**TDD: Write failing tests first:**
- jl_sym('M; evil()') must raise ValueError
- jl_int('not_a_number') must raise TypeError
- jl_call with validated args must succeed
- jl_call wraps Julia errors in RuntimeError with context

**Implementation:**
1. Create packages/xact-py/src/xact/_bridge.py (~120 lines) with:
   - validate_ident(name, context) — same regex as Julia Phase A
   - jl_escape(s) — backslash + quote escaping (replaces existing _jl_escape)
   - jl_sym(name, context) — validated Symbol literal ':name'
   - jl_int(n) — type-checked integer literal
   - jl_str(s) — escaped string literal '"..."'
   - jl_sym_list(names, context) — validated Symbol vector '[:a, :b]'
   - jl_path(p) — escaped path string
   - jl_call(jl, func, *args) — locked seval with error wrapping
2. Migrate api.py (~20 call sites) to use jl_call + typed builders
3. Migrate julia_stub.py (~30 action handlers) to use jl_call + typed builders
4. Remove old _jl_escape from api.py (replaced by bridge module)

**Note:** Timeout (sxAct-iz1u) is NOT addressed here — it requires subprocess isolation, tracked separately.

**Files:** packages/xact-py/src/xact/_bridge.py (new), packages/xact-py/src/xact/api.py, packages/sxact/src/sxact/adapter/julia_stub.py
**Ref:** plans/2026-03-20-architecture-hardening.md#phase-b
**Tickets resolved:** sxAct-7m5w (Python side), sxAct-854k, sxAct-gzqq, sxAct-b6v1
close_reason: Safe bridge module with typed arg builders, 859 Python tests pass

title: Phase A: Add Validation.jl module for Julia-side input validation
description:
Create src/Validation.jl (~100 lines) with reusable validation functions called from all Julia entry points. This is the Julia-side defense layer; Python-side is Phase B.

**TDD: Write failing tests first:**
- validate_identifier(:"M; evil()") must throw ArgumentError
- validate_perm([1,1,3]) must throw ArgumentError (duplicate)
- validate_disjoint_cycles([[1,2],[2,3]]) must throw ArgumentError (overlap)
- validate_order(0) must throw ArgumentError
- validate_order(-1) must throw ArgumentError

**Implementation:**

   - validate_identifier(name; context) — ASCII regex ^[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*$
   - validate_perm(p; context) — range + no-duplicates check
   - validate_disjoint_cycles(cycles) — cross-cycle element uniqueness
   - validate_order(order; context) — order >= 1
2. Include in xAct.jl before other modules
3. Add calls at boundaries:
   - def_manifold!, def_metric!, def_tensor! → validate_identifier(name)
   - Cycles() → validate_disjoint_cycles()
   - inverse_perm() → validate_perm()
   - perturb() → validate_order()

**Files:** src/Validation.jl (new), src/xAct.jl, src/XPerm.jl, src/XTensor.jl
**Ref:** plans/2026-03-20-architecture-hardening.md#phase-a
**Tickets resolved:** sxAct-dlw6, sxAct-jf3w, sxAct-y1q3, sxAct-15uh, sxAct-e6d3
**Contributes to:** sxAct-7m5w (Julia side only)
close_reason: Implemented in Validation.jl with full TDD and integration

title: Epic: Architecture Hardening (6 phases)
description:
Structural refactorings to fix systemic issues found in red-team review. See plans/2026-03-20-architecture-hardening.md for full design.

6 phases:
- Phase A: Validation Layer (Julia side)
- Phase B: Safe Julia Bridge (Python side)
- Phase C: Type Fixes (Einstein trace, BasisChangeObj, CTensorObj)
- Phase D: Index-Aware String Operations (_swap_indices)
- Phase E: InvarDB Dimension-Aware Cache
- Phase F: Session Context (future, structural thread safety)

Phases A, C, D, E are independent and parallelizable.
Phase B benefits from A. Phase F depends on A + C.

Resolves or contributes to 21 tickets from the red-team review.
close_reason: All 5 phases (A-E) complete. Phase F (Session Context) tracked as P2 separately.

title: Julia code injection via unsanitized identifier names in seval calls
description:
User-supplied strings (tensor names, index labels, covd names, metric names) are interpolated directly into Julia seval() calls without sanitization, enabling arbitrary Julia code execution.

**Affected files:**
- packages/sxact/src/sxact/adapter/julia_stub.py: lines 349, 350, 352, 384, 413 — Main.eval(:(global {name} = :{name})) patterns
- packages/xact-py/src/xact/api.py: ~20 functions interpolate into Symbol position (def_manifold, def_metric, def_tensor, def_basis, def_chart, canonicalize, contract, simplify, commute_covds, sort_covds, perturb, ibp, var_d, riemann_simplify, etc.)

Note: _jl_escape() is used for quoted string arguments, but Symbol-position arguments are raw.

**Reproduction:**
```python
from xact import Manifold
# This name contains Julia code injection:
m = Manifold('M; run(`rm -rf /tmp/test`)', dim=4, indices='a b c d')
```

**Fix:** Validate all user-supplied identifiers match ^[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*$ before interpolation. Alternatively, use Julia's Symbol() constructor via juliacall instead of string interpolation.
close_reason: Safe bridge module with typed arg builders, 859 Python tests pass

title: XInvar: _ensure_invar_db ignores dim parameter on cached DB, always loads dim=4 rules
description:
_ensure_invar_db (src/XInvar.jl line 2090) loads the Invar database once with dim=4 (default) and caches it globally. Once cached, subsequent calls with different dim values silently return the cached dim=4 database. All internal callers (InvSimplify, RiemannSimplify) call _ensure_invar_db() without passing dim.

**Impact:** Dimension-dependent simplification (InvSimplify level >= 5) produces wrong results for non-4D manifolds. Step 5 loads rules from 5_4/ subdirectory instead of 5_3/ or 5_5/.

**Reproduction:**
```julia
using .xAct
reset_state!()
def_manifold!(:M3, 3, [:a,:b,:c])
def_metric!(:g3, :M3, 1)
# This uses dim=4 rules instead of dim=3:
result = RiemannSimplify("RiemannM3[a,b,c,d] * RiemannM3[-a,-b,-c,-d]", :g3; level=5)
```

**Fix options:**
1. Key cache by dim: Dict{Int, InvarDB}
2. Pass dim through to _ensure_invar_db from InvSimplify/RiemannSimplify
3. Reload DB when dim changes (simplest)
close_reason: Dimension-keyed InvarDB cache prevents dim collision

title: _swap_indices uses naive string replace causing substring corruption
description:
_swap_indices (src/XTensor.jl lines 4889-4898) uses Julia's replace() for substring replacement without word boundaries. If one index label is a prefix of another or of a tensor name, the substitution corrupts the expression.

**Affected callers:** SymmetryOf(), MakeTraceFree()

**Reproduction:**
```julia
using .xAct
# Index labels where one is a prefix of another:
_swap_indices("Tab[-a,-ab]", "a", "b")
# Expected: "Tbb[-b,-ab]"  (only swap free index 'a')
# Actual:   "Tbb[-b,-bb]"  (also corrupts 'ab' → 'bb')
```

**Fix:** Use regex with word boundaries: r"\b" * label * r"\b", or better, parse the expression into tokens (tensor names vs index labels in brackets) and only substitute inside bracket groups.
close_reason: Bracket-aware _swap_indices prevents substring corruption

title: Einstein trace coefficient wrong for odd-dimensional manifolds
description:
In src/XTensor.jl line 1198, _auto_create_curvature! computes Einstein trace coefficient:
  coeff_int = 1 - div(n, 2)

For dim=4: div(4,2)=2, coeff=1-2=-1 → CORRECT (tr(G)=-R in 4D)
For dim=3: div(3,2)=1, coeff=1-1=0 → WRONG (should be -1/2, i.e. tr(G)=-R/2 in 3D)
For dim=5: div(5,2)=2, coeff=1-2=-1 → WRONG (should be -3/2)

Root cause: _trace_scalars is typed Dict{Symbol,Tuple{Symbol,Int}} (line 260), so it CANNOT store Rational coefficients. The type needs to change to Tuple{Symbol,Rational{Int}}, and the computation should be 1 - n//2.

Files: src/XTensor.jl lines 260, 1198, 2530-2544
Verify: def_metric! with dim=3, then Contract('EinsteinBm3[a,b] * gBm3[-a,-b]') should yield -(1/2)*RicciScalarBm3[], not 0
notes:
Reproduction:
julia> using .xAct; reset_state!()
julia> def_manifold!(:M3, 3, [:a,:b,:c])
julia> def_metric!(:g3, :M3, 1)
julia> Contract("EinsteinM3[a,b] * g3M3[-a,-b]")
# Returns '0' (wrong). Should return '-(1//2) * RicciScalarM3[]'
close_reason: Einstein trace fixed (Rational coeff), BasisChangeObj/CTensorObj parameterized

title: seval string interpolation lacks input validation for Julia symbol injection
description: Many functions in api.py pass user-supplied strings directly into jl.seval() f-strings as Julia Symbol literals (e.g. :{covd}, :{name}, :{basis}). While _jl_escape() is used for string arguments inside quotes, the Symbol-position arguments are interpolated raw. A user passing covd='foo); run("rm -rf /"); #' could inject arbitrary Julia code. Affected functions include: commute_covds, sort_covds, def_basis, def_chart, set_basis_change, change_basis, get_jacobian, basis_change_q, get_components, component_value, ctensor_q, to_basis, from_basis, trace_basis_dummy, christoffel, all_contractions, make_trace_free, check_metric_consistency, perturb_curvature, perturbation_order, perturbation_at_order. All should validate that identifier-position strings match a safe pattern (e.g. ^[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*$) before interpolation, or use the Julia calling convention (mod.Function(args)) instead of seval.
close_reason: Duplicate of sxAct-7m5w (same Julia code injection vulnerability)

title: Fix Python adapter silent Julia error suppression
description: CRITICAL: julia_stub.py line 314 — `except Exception as exc: return Result(status='error', error=str(exc))` catches ALL exceptions, loses type info and stack traces. Users see raw Julia error text without context about which operation failed. Fix: preserve exception type, add operation context (action name, args), wrap in Python-friendly exception.
close_reason: Error results now include exception type, action name, args, and full traceback for debugging.

title: Fix XTensor ToBasis unchecked term_results access
description: CRITICAL: XTensor.jl line 4502 — `n_free = length(term_results[1][2])` accesses term_results[1] without checking if term_results is non-empty. BoundsError on empty results. Fix: add explicit emptiness check with clear error message.
close_reason: Added defensive emptiness check before term_results[1] access. Low-risk: upstream guard already prevents this, but now code is self-documenting.

title: Fix XInvar RiemannSimplify missing metric validation
description: CRITICAL: XInvar.jl line 1968 — RiemannSimplify() accepts metric symbol but never validates it exists. Invalid/undefined metrics propagate silently through the pipeline causing confusing failures. Fix: validate metric is registered before processing.
close_reason: Added covd name validation in RiemannSimplify + level range check (1..6). 3 new tests.

title: Fix XPerm _young_columns() empty partition crash
description: CRITICAL: XPerm.jl line 1948 — _young_columns() accesses tab.partition[1] without checking if partition is empty. Results in BoundsError. Fix: add `isempty(tab.partition) && error("_young_columns: empty partition")` guard.
close_reason: Added empty partition guard in _young_columns(). 1 new test.

title: Fix XPerm Cycles() missing bounds check on cycle elements
description: CRITICAL: XPerm.jl line 1134 — Cycles() does not validate cycle elements are in range 1..n. Passing Cycles([1,2,100]) with n=3 crashes with BoundsError instead of clear domain error. Fix: validate all cycle elements before building image array.

Goals: (1) all errors are clear about WHAT went wrong, WHERE, and HOW to fix, (2) no silent failures, (3) typed exceptions instead of bare error(), (4) Python-side validation before Julia calls, (5) test coverage for error paths. 7 critical, 30 major, 23 minor issues across XPerm.jl, XTensor.jl, XInvar.jl, InvarDB.jl, and Python API/adapter layers.

title: Python public API: transparent wrapper over juliacall
description:
Create a user-facing Python API in xact-py that hides all juliacall internals. Users should never see jl.seval, _b suffixes, jlvec, or Julia Symbols.

**Target API:**
```python
import xact

M = xact.Manifold("M", 4, ["a", "b", "c", "d", "e", "f"])
g = xact.Metric(M, "g", signature=-1, covd="CD")
T = xact.Tensor("T", ["-a", "-b"], M, symmetry="Symmetric")

xact.canonicalize("T[-b,-a] - T[-a,-b]")  # "0"
xact.contract("V[a] * g[-a,-b]")          # "V[-b]"
xact.simplify("RicciCD[-a,-b] * g[a,b]")

h = xact.Tensor("h", ["-a", "-b"], M, symmetry="Symmetric")
xact.perturbation(h, background=g, order=1)
xact.perturb("g[-a,-b]", order=1)
```

**Implementation:**
- Thin wrapper module in packages/xact-py/src/xact/ (~200 lines)
- Handles list→Julia Vector conversion, string→Symbol, !→_b naming internally
- Lazy Julia init via existing xact.xcore._runtime
- reset_state() on module-level or explicit session management
- Return strings from canonicalize/contract/simplify (same as Julia API)
- Raise Python exceptions (ValueError, RuntimeError), not JuliaError

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] import xact; xact.Manifold(...) works without any juliacall knowledge
- [ ] All notebook examples rewritten to use the new API
- [ ] Python notebook runs in JupyterLab end-to-end
- [ ] Docstrings on all public functions
- [ ] No juliacall types leak into return values
close_reason: Python public API complete: xact.api module with Manifold/Metric/Tensor/Perturbation classes and canonicalize/contract/simplify/perturb functions. 18 unit tests passing. Notebook, README, and docs updated. Committed in 50a0746.

title: Julia: Bundle subcomponents into xAct.jl meta-package

2. **Python (Wrapper):**

title: xPerm: Wire YoungProjector into ToCanonical
description: Young projectors (YoungTableau, young_projector, row_symmetry_sgs, col_antisymmetry_sgs) were implemented in XPerm.jl (sxAct-9gt) but are not yet called by ToCanonical in XTensor.jl. Need to: (1) allow def_tensor! to accept a Young tableau as the symmetry spec instead of just :Symmetric/:Antisymmetric/:RiemannSymmetric; (2) wire ToCanonical to call young_projector and apply the projector to the canonicalized result. BLOCKER for Invar.
notes:
Key files: XTensor.jl:82 (SymmetrySpec struct), XTensor.jl:1141 (ToCanonical entry point), XPerm.jl:2054-2055 (YoungTableau + young_projector exports), XTensor.jl:261 (_parse_symmetry — where new :YoungSymmetry branch goes).

Step 1: Extend SymmetrySpec to support YoungTableau. Options: (a) add tableau::Union{Nothing,YoungTableau} field, or (b) add :YoungSymmetry as a new type symbol and store the partition in slots. Option (b) is less invasive.

Step 2: Extend _parse_symmetry / def_tensor! to accept YoungTableau partition spec (e.g. 'Young[{2,1}]' string or a partition vector).

Step 3: In ToCanonical (XTensor.jl:1212 dispatch block), detect sym.type == :YoungSymmetry, call young_projector(standard_tableau(partition, slot_positions), n), then collect and serialize the multi-term sum (each term is a sign * permuted index list).

Acceptance criteria: def_tensor! accepts a Young[{2,1}] symmetry; ToCanonical on a 3-index hook-[2,1] tensor returns the correct two-term symmetrized expression string; existing Symmetric/Antisymmetric/Riemann tests unchanged.
close_reason: Implemented. YoungSymmetry added to SymmetrySpec, _parse_symmetry, canonicalize_slots. 19 Julia + 8 TOML tests all pass.

title: xPert: Implement perturbation expansion rules (perturb/Leibniz)
description: def_perturbation! currently only registers metadata (name, background tensor, order). The actual perturbation calculus is missing: need perturb(expr, order) that applies the Leibniz rule to expand perturbations of products/sums, and tracks perturbation order correctly across tensor operations. BLOCKER for xPert milestone parity.
notes:
Key files: XTensor.jl:1586-1619 (def_perturbation!, check_metric_consistency), XTensor.jl:105-127 (PerturbationObj, _perturbations dict).

Scope boundary vs. sxAct-hyy: THIS issue (gys) implements the core perturb() expansion function. sxAct-hyy layers the h@n indexing notation on top and depends on this.

API target:
  perturb(tensor_name::Symbol, order::Int) → String
  perturb(expr::AbstractString, order::Int) → String
Input/output: String→String, consistent with ToCanonical pipeline. 

Leibniz rule for tensors: for a product T_{a} * S_{b}, perturb at order 1 gives (δT_{a}) * S_{b} + T_{a} * (δS_{b}). For a sum, distribute linearly.

Expression representation: expressions are Strings (same as ToCanonical input). Parsing needed: tokenize at + boundary, identify tensor factors per term.

Acceptance criteria: perturb(:g, 1) on a defined metric with a registered first-order perturbation returns a String containing the perturbation tensor name; perturb on a sum of two tensors distributes correctly; order-2 expansion produces correct quadratic terms.

DISCOVERED FROM: sxAct-lfs (Epic: Migrate xPert to Julia/Python)
close_reason: perturb() implemented with Leibniz expansion, reviewed, fixed, and committed

title: Implement PythonAdapter: wire sxact.xcore wrapper to TestAdapter protocol
description:
## Why

The PythonAdapter (src/sxact/adapter/python_stub.py) is a stub that returns 'not implemented' for all execute() calls. This blocks the core migration validation goal: comparing Python wrapper output against the Wolfram oracle.

## What to Do

Replace python_stub.py with a real implementation that routes actions to the sxact.xcore Julia-backed Python wrapper.

### Action routing (src/sxact/adapter/python_stub.py → real PythonAdapter)

The TestAdapter.execute() receives actions from the vocabulary (DefManifold, DefMetric, DefTensor, Evaluate, ToCanonical, Simplify, Contract, Assert). Map each to sxact.xcore calls:

- **Evaluate**: call the appropriate xcore function by name (args['expr'])
- **Assert**: evaluate and compare result to args['expected']
- **DefManifold / DefMetric / DefTensor**: these are stateful xAct setup steps — need to determine if xcore exposes these or if they belong to an xTensor layer (research needed)

### Key files to read

- src/sxact/adapter/python_stub.py — current stub (50 lines)
- src/sxact/adapter/base.py — TestAdapter ABC
- src/sxact/xcore/__init__.py — 611 lines of public functions
- src/sxact/adapter/wolfram.py — reference implementation
- src/sxact/adapter/julia_stub.py — partial Julia implementation for reference

### normalize() and equals()

Wire to the existing normalize pipeline (src/sxact/normalize/pipeline.py) — same pipeline the WolframAdapter uses.

### get_properties()

Can start as {} — matches JuliaAdapter's current behavior.

## Success Criteria

- All 8 actions from supported_actions() return real Result objects (not 'not implemented')
- Passes tests/test_adapter_conformance.py
- Can run the xcore TOML tests (tests/xcore/*.toml) through PythonAdapter and get passing results
- PythonAdapter vs WolframAdapter comparison produces matching results for at least 50% of xcore test cases

## Research Needed

Before implementing DefManifold/DefMetric/DefTensor actions: check if sxact.xcore exposes manifold management functions or if these are xTensor-only operations. If xCore-only scope for now, return 'not implemented' for tensor setup actions with clear error message.

## Notes

- Keep python_stub.py for conformance testing — rename to python_adapter.py for real impl, or replace in-place
- Context is a simple Python object (no Julia kernel management needed — juliacall manages that)
close_reason: Closed

title: Oracle: Symbol context pollution breaks xAct tensor operations
description:
## Problem

When evaluating expressions via wolframclient, symbols are parsed in `Global`` context before xAct sees them. This causes:

1. Tensors like `S[-a,-b]` become `Global`S[Times[-1, Global`a], ...]` instead of xAct tensor expressions
2. `ToCanonical` and other xAct functions don't recognize the expressions as tensors
3. Curvature tensors like `RiemannCD` are created in `Global`` context

## Evidence

```
Context[RiemannCDZ]  → "Global`"  (should be xAct context)
TensorQ[RiemannCDZ]  → False       (should be True)
```

## Impact

3 of 11 integration tests fail:
- TestSymbolicEquality::test_symmetric_tensor_sum_equals_double
- TestAntisymmetricTensor::test_antisymmetric_tensor_swap_negates  
- TestBianchiIdentity::test_riemann_first_bianchi_structure

## Recommended Fix

Implement per-test context isolation:

1. Add `context_id` parameter to `/evaluate-with-init` endpoint
2. Server wraps evaluation in `Block[{$Context = ctx, $ContextPath = ...}, expr]`
3. Pytest fixture generates unique context ID per test
4. All evaluations within a test use the same context ID

## References

- docs/oracle-quirks.md - full documentation of the issue
- plans/2026-01-22-sprint1-derisk-equivalence.md - Phase 4
close_reason: Fixed by implementing context isolation in Oracle. All 12 integration tests now pass. See commit for details.

title: Implement TexAct: LaTeX and Unicode rich output (TExpr Stage 3)
description: Port the TexAct module from xAct, providing publication-quality LaTeX rendering of tensor expressions. In our architecture this is TExpr Stage 3: Unicode REPL output and LaTeX rendering for Jupyter/Pluto notebooks. Currently listed as 'not yet started' in README. Acceptance: T[-a,-b] displays as T_{ab} in the REPL and renders correctly in Jupyter via show(MIME("text/latex"), expr). Required for JOSS submission and researcher adoption. TExpr Stage 1/2 are already complete and provide scaffolding.\n\nTDD entry: create test/texact_test.jl with one failing @test asserting REPL display of T[-a,-b]. Implement in src/TexAct.jl.

title: Implement xTerior: exterior calculus (differential forms)
description:
Port the xTerior module from xAct. xTerior provides exterior calculus operations: differential p-forms, wedge product (∧), exterior derivative (d), Hodge dual (★), and interior product (ι). Required for: differential geometry on manifolds, de Rham cohomology, and integration theory. Currently listed as 'not yet started' in README. The Session/manifold infrastructure from XTensor provides the scaffolding; new index types for form degree are needed.

Acceptance: wedge_product(da, db) returns a 2-form matching the xAct oracle; d(d(f)) == 0 holds symbolically.

TDD entry: create test/xterior_test.jl with one failing @test for wedge_product. Implement in src/XTerior.jl. Parity: run oracle comparison for xterior operations.

title: Add license provenance statement to paper.md
description: The package uses GPL-3.0 matching xAct upstream. The paper.md must explicitly state that algorithms were implemented from published papers (Martín-García 2008, Manssur-Portugal 2002) rather than translating GPL source code. This documents the license justification and protects against IP concerns. JOSS reviewers check license compatibility explicitly.
close_reason: Folded into sxAct-3hc7 acceptance criteria (architecture tradeoff, scope table, license provenance sections)

title: Add scope statement to paper.md distinguishing implemented vs planned xAct modules
description: JOSS editors require a clear scope statement. The paper.md must explicitly document which xAct modules are implemented (xPerm ✓, xTensor ✓, xCoba ✓, xInvar ✓, xPert partial) vs. not yet started (Spinors, xTerior, Harmonics, TexAct). Vagueness about scope is a common JOSS rejection reason. Also compare to cadabra2 and Python alternatives.
close_reason: Folded into sxAct-3hc7 acceptance criteria (architecture tradeoff, scope table, license provenance sections)

title: Document string-based architecture tradeoff in paper.md
description: XAct.jl uses its own string-based symbolic engine (mirroring Wolfram xAct design) rather than integrating with Symbolics.jl. JOSS reviewers will flag this as a question. The paper.md should proactively address: why this design was chosen, what it enables (compatibility with xAct conventions), and what the future integration path with Symbolics.jl looks like. This is a known documented limitation in the README but needs to appear in the paper narrative.
close_reason: Folded into sxAct-3hc7 acceptance criteria (architecture tradeoff, scope table, license provenance sections)

title: Tighten gravitational waves notebook from perturbation output to physics story

- Readers may come away with a good picture of polarization but a weaker sense of what was actually derived versus summarized.

Goals
- Preserve the successful visual intuition.
- Strengthen the symbolic-to-physics bridge.
- Make the notebook explicit about gauge assumptions and trace-reversed variables if they are part of the story.

- docs/make.jl
- docs/src/notebooks/mtw_gravitational_waves_julia.md
- test/julia/test_docs.jl

Proposed improvements
- Add a compact explanation of perturbation order, gauge choice, and what `perturb_curvature` is providing.
- If feasible, define or discuss the trace-reversed perturbation `hbar_ab` and the Lorenz gauge explicitly.
- Clearly label the polarization plots as physical visualization of a known solution, distinct from the symbolic perturbation calculation.

Acceptance criteria
- [ ] The symbolic perturbation section explains what has actually been computed.
- [ ] Gauge assumptions and variable definitions needed for the wave equation are stated clearly.
- [ ] The polarization visualization remains, but is explicitly tied to the conceptual model it represents.
- [ ] The notebook distinguishes derived statements from explanatory summaries.
