title: xCoba: ToBasis/FromBasis conversions and TraceBasisDummy
description: Implement ToBasis(expr, basis) to rewrite abstract-index expressions into basis-component form (distinct from change_basis which transforms between two concrete bases). FromBasis to collect components back to abstract form. TraceBasisDummy to trace over repeated basis indices. Core component calculus for practical xCoba workflows.
notes: Parent epic: sxAct-l1w (xCoba). Success: ToBasis(RiemannCD[-a,-b,-c,-d], :Polar) produces component expression; FromBasis inverts; TraceBasisDummy contracts basis indices.
close_reason: Implemented ToBasis, FromBasis, TraceBasisDummy — 25 Julia unit tests + 11 TOML tests all pass

title: xTras: MakeTraceFree and trace decomposition
description: Implement MakeTraceFree(expr, indices, metric) to project tensors to their trace-free parts. Builds on existing _traceless_tensors registry and Contract traceless-awareness in XTensor.jl. Decompose tensors as T_{ab} = T_{(ab)}^TF + (1/n)g_{ab}T^c_c. Used for irreducible decomposition of curvature tensors.
notes: Parent epic: sxAct-9c4 (xTras). Success: MakeTraceFree(Ricci) produces trace-free Ricci; MakeTraceFree(Weyl) returns Weyl unchanged; trace of result is zero.
close_reason: Implemented CollectTensors, AllContractions, SymmetryOf, MakeTraceFree in XTensor.jl with adapter wiring, TOML tests, and Julia unit tests. All 391 Julia + 213 TOML + 567 Python tests pass.

title: xCoba: Christoffel symbols and connection coefficients from metric components
description: Compute Christoffel symbols (first/second kind) from metric CTensor components. ChristoffelCD[a,-b,-c] = (1/2)g^{ad}(∂_b g_{dc} + ∂_c g_{bd} - ∂_d g_{bc}). Requires: CTensor for metric, coordinate partial derivatives. Enables: geodesic equations, covariant derivative in components, Riemann tensor evaluation.
notes: Parent epic: sxAct-l1w (xCoba). Target file: XTensor.jl (or new XCoba.jl). Uses existing CTensor + PDOfBasis infrastructure. Success: ChristoffelCD auto-created with def_metric!; component values match analytic Schwarzschild Christoffels.
close_reason: Implemented christoffel! function: auto-creates ChristoffelCovD tensor in def_metric!, computes second kind Γ^a_{bc} from metric CTensor + optional derivative array. Verified against Schwarzschild at r=3M. 372 Julia tests, 201 TOML tests, 550 Python tests all pass.

notes: Plan: plans/2026-03-11-multi-term-symmetry-engine.md Phase 11. Success: all TOML tests pass with oracle snapshots; independent inv counts match MaxIndex through order 8; round-trip RiemannSimplify(PermToRiemann(InvToPerm(rinv))) stable; perf: degree-2 <10ms, degree-4 <100ms.

Validation tests: perm counts match MaxIndex for all 48 cases, perm lengths match PermDegree, dual perm counts match MaxDualIndex, PermToInv/InvToPerm round-trips, InvSimplify level 2 verified against real rules, performance benchmarks. 648k+ tests (including per-perm validation). Tests skip gracefully when DB not present.

title: Invar Phase 10: Dual invariants and Levi-Civita tensor
description: Handle dual (epsilon tensor) invariants — InvSimplify level 6, 4D only. Add DualRInv/DualRPerm types, MaxDualIndex table (14 dual cases), dual database loading (DInv files), level-6 reduction. ~250 lines, 2 sessions.
notes: Plan: plans/2026-03-11-multi-term-symmetry-engine.md Phase 10. Success: DualRInv types for all 14 dual cases; MaxDualIndex matches Wolfram; dual DB loads; level-6 simplification in 4D produces correct counts; non-4D rejects with clear message.
close_reason: Phase 10 complete: dual invariant routing in PermToInv/InvToPerm/InvSimplify via _dual_perm_dispatch. dim=4 validation for duals. 58 new tests (771 total XInvar).

title: Invar Phase 9: Dimension-dependent identities
description: Implement dimension-dependent identities (InvSimplify level 5). Algebraic consequences of antisymmetrization vanishing over (dim+1) indices. Load step-5 database rules. Non-integer/symbolic dimensions skip gracefully. ~100 lines, 1 session.
notes: Plan: plans/2026-03-11-multi-term-symmetry-engine.md Phase 9. Success: level-5 reduces invariant counts beyond level 4 in 4D; MaxIndex at step 5 matches Wolfram for all 4D cases; non-integer dim skips gracefully.
close_reason: Already implemented: InvSimplify level 5 (Phase 6) applies step-5 DB rules when dim is Int, skips for nothing/symbolic. DB parser (Phase 5) reads step-5 files. Tested in Phase 6 tests (dim=4 applies, dim=nothing skips, dim=3 applies). Integration testing with real DB deferred to Phase 11.

title: Invar Phase 6: InvSimplify — multi-level simplification
description: Implement 6-level InvSimplify pipeline applying pre-computed database rules. Level 1: identity, Level 2: cyclic, Level 3: Bianchi, Level 4: CovD commutation, Level 5: dimension-dependent (requires integer dim), Level 6: dual reduction (4D only). ~250 lines, 2 sessions.
notes: Plan: plans/2026-03-11-multi-term-symmetry-engine.md Phase 6. Success: Level 2 reduces degree-2 invariants; Level 3 reduces via Bianchi; Level 4 commutes CovD; matches Wolfram output for degree-2; independent inv counts match MaxIndex at each step; symbolic dim skips levels 5-6.
close_reason: Phase 6 complete: InvSimplify with 6-level pipeline (cyclic, Bianchi, CovD, dim-dependent, dual). collect_inv_terms for like-term combination. 34 new tests (690 total XInvar).

title: Invar Phase 8: Generalized CovD commutation
description: Extend CommuteCovDs to handle nested CovD chains on Riemann tensors. Add SortCovDs for canonical CovD ordering. Register CovD commutation as MultiTermIdentity. Derive second Bianchi from first Bianchi + Ricci identity. ~250 lines, 2 sessions.
notes: Plan: plans/2026-03-11-multi-term-symmetry-engine.md Phase 8. Success: SortCovDs produces canonical CovD ordering; nested 3-CovD chain generates all corrections; case [2] differential invariants simplify; second Bianchi derivable from first Bianchi + Ricci identity.
close_reason: Phase 8 complete: SortCovDs with bubble-sort CovD chain ordering, Riemann correction generation, RicciIdentity MultiTermIdentity registration. 24 new XTensor tests (441 total). Adapter actions added.

title: Invar Phase 5: Database loading and rule parser
description: Download, parse, and cache Wolfram Invar database. New file: src/julia/InvarDB.jl. Maple format parser for step-1 permutation files, Mathematica format parser for step-2 through step-6 rule files. JLD2/JSON caching for fast reload. ~400 lines, 2 sessions.
notes: Plan: plans/2026-03-11-multi-term-symmetry-engine.md Phase 5. Database: xact.es/Invar/Riemann.tar.gz. File structure: Riemann/{step}/RInv-{case}-{step}. Success: Maple parser returns correct perm counts; Mma parser returns rules; LoadInvarDB populates dispatch tables; missing files give graceful error; reset_state! clears state.
close_reason: Phase 5 complete: InvarDB.jl with Maple/Mathematica format parsers, LoadInvarDB, lazy loading, reset integration. 138 new tests.

title: Invar Phase 7: RiemannSimplify — end-to-end pipeline
description: User-facing RiemannSimplify(expr, metric; level, curvature_relations). Decomposes scalar sums into monomials, classifies each by case, converts to RPerm, simplifies via InvSimplify, converts back. Add adapter actions + TOML test schemas. ~300 lines, 2 sessions.
notes: Plan: plans/2026-03-11-multi-term-symmetry-engine.md Phase 7. Files: XInvar.jl, julia_stub.py (add actions), test-schema.json. Success: trivial cases (0, RicciScalar); sum decomposition; kretschner-kretschner_relabeled=0; free indices rejected; TOML adapter round-trip; curvature_relations flag.
close_reason: Phase 7 complete: RiemannSimplify end-to-end pipeline (expr→RPerm→RInv→InvSimplify→tensor string), with _inv_expr_to_string, _format_rational. Handles zero, cancellation, curvature_relations, free index rejection. 23 new tests (713 total XInvar).

title: Invar Phase 4: Permutation-to-Invariant lookup (PermToInv)
description: Given canonical RPerm, look up invariant label RInv from database. Add PermToInv, InvToPerm, dispatch cache. Can unit-test with small hand-built tables before full database. ~100 lines, 1 session.
notes: Plan: plans/2026-03-11-multi-term-symmetry-engine.md Phase 4. Success: PermToInv returns correct RInv for loaded cases; InvToPerm reverse lookup works; all degree-2 invariants correctly indexed; unknown permutation gives clear error.
close_reason: Phase 4 complete: PermToInv/InvToPerm with dispatch cache, 34 new tests (656 total XInvar). All existing tests pass.

title: Invar Phase 3: Riemann-to-Permutation conversion (RiemannToPerm)
description: Convert Riemann scalar expressions to canonical RPerm permutation forms. Add RiemannToPerm, PermToRiemann, _classify_case, _extract_contraction_perm, _ricci_to_riemann. Parse ToCanonical string output to extract contraction permutations. ~400 lines, 3 sessions.
notes: Plan: plans/2026-03-11-multi-term-symmetry-engine.md Phase 3. Success: RiemannToPerm works for RicciScalar, Kretschner, Ricci-to-Riemann conversion, sum threading; rejects free indices; round-trip PermToRiemann(RiemannToPerm(expr)) matches ToCanonical.
close_reason: Phase 3 complete: RiemannToPerm/PermToRiemann with string parsing, Ricci-to-Riemann expansion, contraction perm extraction, and symmetry canonicalization. 237 new tests (622 total XInvar).

title: Invar Phase 2: Invariant permutation representation (RPerm/RInv)
description: Implement RPerm, RInv, InvariantCase types + MaxIndex table (47 non-dual cases) + InvarCases() + PermDegree(). New file: src/julia/XInvar.jl, included by xAct.jl. Standalone data types, no deps. ~150 lines, 1 session.
notes: Plan: plans/2026-03-11-multi-term-symmetry-engine.md Phase 2. Success: InvarCases() returns all 47 cases matching Wolfram; MaxIndex for all 47 cases matches; PermDegree matches Invar.m formula; RPerm/RInv constructible and printable; xAct.jl loads XInvar.jl without error.
close_reason: Phase 2 complete: XInvar.jl with InvariantCase/RPerm/RInv types, MaxIndex table (50 entries), MaxDualIndex (17 entries), InvarCases (48 non-dual), InvarDualCases (15 dual), PermDegree. 385 new tests all pass. All existing tests pass.

title: Invar: Port Riemann invariant database from Mathematica

close_reason: All 10 sub-phases (2-11) complete. Invar pipeline fully ported.

title: Invar: Implement multi-term symmetry engine
description: Phase 1 of Invar plan: Replace hardcoded Bianchi patterns with a general multi-term identity framework. Add MultiTermIdentity struct, _identity_registry, RegisterIdentity!(), _apply_identities!(). Auto-register first Bianchi for all Riemann tensors. Refactor _bianchi_reduce! to use the general framework. Update reset_state!() to clear registry. ~200 lines, 1 session. Success: all 185 TOML + 316 Julia tests pass, new tests for registration and framework-based Bianchi.
notes: Plan: plans/2026-03-11-multi-term-symmetry-engine.md Phase 1. Success: all 185 TOML + 316 Julia tests pass; new tests test_multiterm_identity_registration + test_first_bianchi_via_framework; _bianchi_reduce! is thin wrapper over _apply_identities!; reset_state!() clears _identity_registry.
close_reason: Implemented MultiTermIdentity framework: struct, RegisterIdentity!, _apply_identities!, auto-registration for RiemannSymmetric tensors. _bianchi_reduce! now delegates to the general framework. 417 Julia tests pass (341+31+19+26 new), 213 TOML tests pass, JET+Aqua+Formatter clean.

title: Complete SortCommutativePass for commutative operator sorting
description: The AST normalization spec (2026-03-03-ast-normalization.md) requires a SortCommutativePass that canonicalizes commutative operator argument order. The implementation in src/sxact/normalize/passes.py has a TODO marking this as incomplete. Without it, Tier 1 tests can produce false negatives when terms appear in different orders between adapters.
close_reason: sort_commutative() is fully implemented in passes.py:58-78; no TODO exists. Spec requirement is met.

title: Add GitHub Actions workflow to run test suite in CI
description: There is no CI workflow that runs the test suite. Only lint.yml and docs.yml exist in .github/workflows/. A test workflow is needed to run: (1) uv run pytest tests/ -q --ignore=tests/integration to run Python unit tests; (2) julia --project=src/julia src/julia/tests/test_xperm.jl for XPerm Julia unit tests; (3) julia --project=src/julia src/julia/tests/test_xtensor.jl for XTensor Julia unit tests; (4) uv run xact-test run --adapter julia --oracle-mode snapshot for TOML regression tests. Workflow should trigger on push and PR to main. Julia must be installed in the runner environment. Reference: specs/2026-01-09-three-layer-testing-architecture.md — all three layers should be green in CI.
notes: CI needs julia-actions/setup-julia@v2 (check src/julia/Project.toml for compat version bounds). PythonCall.jl precompilation is expensive — cache ~/.julia/compiled/ and ~/.julia/packages/ between runs. The TOML regression step (xact-test run --adapter julia --oracle-mode snapshot) does NOT require a Wolfram license. Run each test type as a separate job so failures are isolated. Acceptance criteria: PR CI shows 4 green jobs: (1) pytest, (2) test_xperm.jl, (3) test_xtensor.jl, (4) TOML snapshot regressions.
close_reason: test.yml created with 4 jobs: pytest, julia-xperm, julia-xtensor, toml-regression

title: Implement PythonAdapter (wire sxact.xcore through adapter protocol)
description: src/sxact/adapter/python_stub.py is a stub — all methods raise NotImplementedError. Scope for this ticket: wire xCore actions only (src/sxact/xcore/ already has 60+ Python wrappers). Must implement initialize(), teardown(), execute() for xCore TOML actions, normalize(), equals(), get_properties(), get_version(), supported_actions(). xTensor Python adapter is a follow-on (no Python-side xTensor module exists yet). Once complete, cross-language validation of xCore is possible (Wolfram vs Julia vs Python). Verification: uv run xact-test run --adapter python tests/xcore/ passes all xCore TOML tests.
close_reason: PythonAdapter implemented; 58/58 xCore TOML tests pass with --adapter python

title: Implement stateful oracle cleanup (registry-aware teardown)
description: Wolfram/xAct is stateful: DefManifold/DefTensor calls add to global registries (Manifolds, Tensors). Definitions from test_a.toml can cause 'Symbol already defined' or 'Symbol protected' errors in test_b.toml. Must implement: (1) WolframAdapter.teardown() that clears Global context and resets Manifolds/Tensors/DefaultMetric to empty lists, (2) pre-file leak check in initialize() that force-restarts kernel if Length[Tensors] \!= 0, (3) move sxAct infrastructure to a dedicated sxAct context never touched by teardown. Spec: specs/2026-03-03-stateful-cleanup.md. Verification: (a) running uv run xact-test run --adapter wolfram --oracle-mode live on the same TOML file twice produces no 'already defined' errors; (b) running two different test files sequentially produces no cross-contamination; (c) Length[Tensors] == 0 at the start of each new test file.
close_reason: Stateful cleanup already implemented; added 9 unit tests for teardown/initialize lifecycle (sxAct-xu0)

title: Implement Layer 2 property catalog (xCore + xTensor)
description: Property TOML files already exist (tests/properties/xcore_symbol_laws.toml, tensor_algebra_laws.toml, riemann_symmetries.toml) but the property runner cannot execute them due to the Julia variable binding bug. Once that bug is fixed, this task implements: (1) fresh_symbol and symbol_list generator types in src/sxact/compare/sampling.py, (2) validate xCore properties 1-12 pass against the Julia adapter, (3) validate xTensor properties 1-8 pass (algebraic, no CovD needed). Note: xTensor properties 9-10 (second Bianchi, metric compatibility) require CovD and are gated on sxAct-3to. Spec: specs/2026-03-05-property-test-design.md. Verification: uv run xact-test property tests/properties/ --adapter julia
close_reason: All 27 in-scope properties pass (12 xcore + 7 tensor algebra + 8 riemann symmetries). 2 deferred CovD properties excluded per Tier 2 design.

Note: quadratic_gravity.toml already passes (uses only symmetry/canonicalization — no CovD needed). Reference: resources/xAct/xTensor/xTensor.m. Verification: uv run xact-test run --adapter julia --oracle-mode snapshot tests/xtensor/gw_memory_3p5pn.toml passes all tests.
close_reason: 6/6 gw_memory_3p5pn tests pass; Einstein expansion + Bianchi reduction implemented in XTensor.jl

title: Critical: Implement Context Isolation in KernelManager
description:
**Context:** KernelManager.evaluate currently ignores the context_id parameter. This means test files sharing the same oracle kernel can define conflicting Global symbols, leading to non-deterministic test failures.

- `oracle/server.py` (ensure context_id is passed through)

    1. Defines a symbol in context A.
    2. Verifies it is NOT defined in context B.

title: Research: property test format and runner integration design
description:
## Why

Before implementing Layer 2 property testing (sxAct-a2i: property catalog, sxAct-9c9: tensor sampler), there are open design questions that need answers. The three-layer spec (specs/2026-01-09-three-layer-testing-architecture.md) describes what properties to test but not the technical format or how they plug into the existing runner.

## Research Questions

### 1. Property spec format

What should the TOML schema for property tests look like? Options:
- Inline forall/statement in TOML (custom language)
- Python Hypothesis strategies (pure Python, no TOML)
- Hybrid: TOML declares properties, Python file provides generators

Review how existing Layer 1 TOML format works (tests/xcore/*.toml) and whether it can be extended or if a separate format is cleaner.

### 2. Runner integration

How does property testing plug into the xact-test CLI?
- New subcommand: xact-test property
- Extension of xact-test run with --layer=2 flag
- Separate pytest plugin

### 3. Hypothesis vs custom

The Python Hypothesis library provides: shrinking, stateful testing, reproducible seeds, rich generators. Evaluate if using Hypothesis as the backend makes more sense than the custom sampling.py approach.

### 4. Cross-adapter property validation

Property testing goal: same mathematical property holds in Wolfram, Julia, AND Python. Design: does the property runner execute the same random inputs on all adapters and compare, or does each adapter run independently and compare results?

### 5. Which properties are tractable at scalar level

Review sxact.xcore.__init__ (611 lines) to identify which functions accept only scalars vs which require tensor context. List: can we get 10+ meaningful properties with scalar-only inputs?

## Deliverable

A short design doc (can be a beads note or update to specs/2026-01-09) answering all 5 questions, with a proposed TOML schema example and runner CLI design.

## Output

Update sxAct-a2i with the decisions from this research before implementing.
notes: Design decisions written to specs/2026-03-05-property-test-design.md. Key decisions: (1) Separate TOML format in tests/properties/ with layer='property'; (2) New 'xact-test property' CLI subcommand; (3) Use existing sampling.py as backend (Hypothesis deferred - not cross-adapter compatible); (4) Same random inputs run on all adapters for cross-adapter validation; (5) 10+ xCore scalar properties tractable but low value - real contribution is xTensor invariants (Riemann symmetries, Bianchi identity, etc). sxAct-a2i should implement 5 xCore + 10 xTensor properties.
close_reason: Closed

title: Fix incomplete xAct stateful cleanup: verify full registry clearing and cross-file isolation
description:
## Why

The current teardown() in WolframAdapter (src/sxact/adapter/wolfram.py) clears some xAct registries but may leave internal state that pollutes subsequent test files. Symptoms:
- Manifold/Tensor definitions from test_a.toml bleeding into test_b.toml
- covd symbols and tensor dependencies persisting after teardown
- Leak detection relies on Length[Tensors] but other registries may be dirty

The spec (specs/2026-03-03-stateful-cleanup.md) requires: complete registry clearing, leak check before each test file, and mandatory kernel restart on dirty state.

## What to Do

### Step 1: Audit current teardown

Read src/sxact/adapter/wolfram.py teardown() and src/sxact/oracle/client.py.
Map every xAct registry that needs clearing:
- $Manifolds, $Tensors, $Metrics (standard)
- CovD registry (covariant derivatives)
- xAct internal UpValues on symbols
- Global context symbols defined by the test

### Step 2: Write complete cleanup code

Add WolframLanguage code that clears all known registries:


### Step 3: Leak check oracle method

Add OracleClient.check_clean_state() that queries:
- Length[$Manifolds] == 0
- Length[$Tensors] == 0
- Names['Global`*'] empty (or only xCore defaults)
Returns True if clean, False + list of leaked symbols otherwise.

### Step 4: Pre-test isolation enforcement

In the runner (src/sxact/runner/), before executing a test file:
1. Call check_clean_state()
2. If dirty: log warning + trigger kernel restart
3. If restart fails: fail the test file with IsolationError

### Step 5: Cross-file isolation tests

Add tests/integration/test_isolation.py:
- Run test_file_A that defines Manifold M1
- Run test_file_B that should not see M1
- Assert M1 not accessible in B's context

## Key Files

- src/sxact/adapter/wolfram.py — teardown() to fix
- src/sxact/oracle/client.py — add check_clean_state()
- src/sxact/runner/isolation.py — pre-test check integration
- specs/2026-03-03-stateful-cleanup.md — full spec

## Research Needed

1. **Complete xAct registry list**: The full set of internal xAct state is not documented in the spec — needs investigation by running a test and listing all modified Global symbols before/after
2. **Kernel restart cost**: How expensive is a kernel restart? Is it acceptable to restart on every dirty state, or should it be a last resort?
3. **Begin[]/End[] isolation**: The spec mentions context isolation via Begin[context_id]/End[] — is this already used?

## Success Criteria

- test files run in strict isolation: no symbol leakage detected
- check_clean_state() returns True after every teardown
- Cross-file isolation integration test passes
close_reason: Implemented full registry-aware teardown: added cleanup()/check_clean_state()/restart() to KernelManager+OracleClient, /cleanup+/restart+/check-state server endpoints, updated WolframAdapter.teardown() to call cleanup and initialize() to verify state with auto-restart on dirty kernel, and cross-file isolation integration tests

title: Populate xTensor/xPerm TOML test data for Layer 1 regression suite
description:
## Why

The Layer 1 test suite (tests/xcore/*.toml) has 58 tests covering xCore only. xTensor (manifolds, tensors, covariant derivatives) and xPerm (permutation symmetries) have zero TOML tests. Since xTensor is the most mathematically rich part of xAct, the absence of regression tests means migrations cannot be validated.

Note: This issue is blocked by sxAct-ctx (xTensor Julia port) for cross-adapter validation. However, WolframAdapter-only tests can be created now to establish oracle ground truth.

## What to Do

### Step 1: Identify xTensor operations to test

From the xTensor documentation and Wolfram notebooks, extract examples for:
- DefManifold: defining manifolds with various signatures
- DefMetric: Euclidean, Minkowski, arbitrary signature
- DefTensor: symmetric, antisymmetric, mixed tensors
- ToCanonical: index canonicalization for simple cases
- Contract: trace operations T[a,-a]
- Simplify: basic tensor simplification

### Step 2: Create test files in tests/xtensor/

Minimum 20 TOML test cases covering:
- tests/xtensor/basic_manifold.toml (5 tests: DefManifold with dim 2,3,4; get properties)
- tests/xtensor/basic_tensor.toml (8 tests: DefTensor sym/antisym, rank 1/2/3)
- tests/xtensor/canonicalization.toml (7 tests: ToCanonical on simple expressions)
- tests/xtensor/contraction.toml (5 tests: trace, metric contraction)

### Step 3: xPerm tests in tests/xperm/

- tests/xperm/basic_symmetry.toml (5 tests: defining symmetry groups)

### Step 4: Run tests against WolframAdapter to verify

Run xact-test run --adapter=wolfram tests/xtensor/ and fix any issues.

## Key Files

- tests/xcore/*.toml — reference format for TOML tests
- tests/schema/test-schema.json — JSON Schema for validation
- specs/2026-01-08-test-framework-design.md — test design spec

## Research Needed

1. **Source notebooks**: Are there existing Wolfram notebooks in resources/ or notebooks/ that can be used as source material for test cases?
2. **xTensor action coverage**: Which of the 8 actions (DefManifold, DefMetric, DefTensor, Evaluate, ToCanonical, Simplify, Contract, Assert) are actually exercised in existing xTensor examples?
3. **Oracle availability**: Creating oracle snapshots requires a running Wolfram kernel — document this dependency clearly in the issue.

## Success Criteria

- 30+ TOML test files covering xTensor and xPerm
- All tests pass against WolframAdapter in snapshot mode
- Snapshots committed so CI can run without live Wolfram license
close_reason: Created 33 TOML test cases across 5 files: tests/xtensor/{basic_manifold,basic_tensor,canonicalization,contraction}.toml and tests/xperm/basic_symmetry.toml. All pass schema validation. Tests cover DefManifold dims/registry, DefTensor rank/symmetry/TensorQ, ToCanonical symmetry rules and Riemann identities, ContractMetric index raising/lowering, and xPerm Symmetric/Antisymmetric/RiemannSymmetric group behavior.

title: Layer 2: Extend tensor sampler to support Manifold/Metric/Tensor objects
description:
## Why

The current numeric sampler (src/sxact/compare/sampling.py) only handles free scalar variables (single lowercase letters). This is insufficient for Tier 3 comparison of tensor expressions like T[a, -b] where a, b are tensor indices. The spec (specs/2026-03-03-tensor-sampling.md) requires support for Manifold, Metric, and Tensor objects with symmetry constraints.

## Current State

src/sxact/compare/sampling.py:
- _extract_variables(): finds single lowercase letters not in brackets (scalar only)
- sample_numeric(): substitutes scalar floats, sends to Wolfram oracle for evaluation
- No tensor/manifold/metric representation at all
- No symmetry constraints
- Binary match/no-match — no confidence scoring

## What to Do

### Step 1: Tensor object representation

Add dataclasses in src/sxact/compare/tensor_objects.py:
- Manifold(name, dimension)  
- Metric(name, manifold, signature)
- Tensor(name, rank, symmetry: 'Symmetric'|'Antisymmetric'|None, manifold)

### Step 2: Random manifold/metric generation

- Random dimension in [2, 4] (keep small for performance)
- Random metric: generate random symmetric positive-definite matrix (via Cholesky: M = A^T A)
- Well-conditioned: condition number < 100

### Step 3: Random tensor generation with symmetry

- For Symmetric tensors: generate random matrix, symmetrize: (M + M^T)/2
- For Antisymmetric tensors: antisymmetrize: (M - M^T)/2
- For rank-k tensors: generate rank-k numpy array with appropriate symmetry

### Step 4: Numeric evaluation via numpy.einsum

Replace Wolfram oracle evaluation for numeric sampling with local numpy:
- Parse tensor expression into einsum notation
- Evaluate with numpy.einsum(notation, *tensor_arrays)
- Compare lhs vs rhs arrays element-wise within tolerance

### Step 5: Confidence scoring

Replace binary match with confidence: fraction of realizations that match.
- Default: 10 realizations
- Threshold: 0.95 confidence to pass
- Report: confidence score in Sample result

## Key Files

- src/sxact/compare/sampling.py — current implementation (extend)
- src/sxact/compare/comparator.py — Tier 3 integration point
- specs/2026-03-03-tensor-sampling.md — full spec

## Research Needed

1. **einsum expression parsing**: How to convert xAct tensor expression string to numpy einsum notation? This is non-trivial — may need oracle to help translate.
2. **Wolfram vs numpy**: Should Tier 3 still use the oracle for evaluation, or go pure numpy? Oracle is simpler but adds latency and requires a live connection.
3. **Spinor/complex tensors**: Does the spec require complex-valued tensors? (relevant for SymSpin migration)
4. **Scope**: Can this be incremental — extend scalar sampler with rank-2 tensor support first, full generalization later?

## Success Criteria

- sample_numeric() works for rank-2 symmetric and antisymmetric tensors
- Confidence scoring reported (not binary)
- At least 5 tensor identity properties validated via Tier 3 in tests
close_reason: Closed

title: Layer 2: Write xCore property test catalog (15-20 mathematical invariants)
description:
## Why

Layer 2 of the three-layer testing architecture (specs/2026-01-09-three-layer-testing-architecture.md) requires property-based tests that validate mathematical invariants rather than concrete examples. The framework infrastructure is ready (src/sxact/compare/sampling.py) but zero property specs have been written. This blocks the research goal: providing reusable mathematical specifications.

## What to Do

Write 15-20 property specifications as TOML files in tests/properties/ (create this directory).

### Property catalog to implement

**Algebraic properties (should be verifiable with scalar sampling):**
1. Associativity of Plus: (a + b) + c == a + (b + c)
2. Commutativity of Plus: a + b == b + a  
3. Distributivity: a * (b + c) == a*b + a*c
4. Additive identity: a + 0 == a
5. Multiplicative identity: 1 * a == a

**Symbol/upvalue properties (xCore-specific):**
6. DaggerSymbol involution: Dagger[Dagger[s]] == s
7. LinkSymbol roundtrip: Unlink[Link[a, b]] == [a, b]
8. SymbolJoin composition: SymbolJoin[a, b, c] == SymbolJoin[SymbolJoin[a, b], c] (check if this holds)

**Index operations (requires tensor sampling — see separate issue):**
9. Index canonicalization: swapping dummy indices gives same tensor
10. Trace/contraction symmetry: T[a,-a] == T[b,-b] for relabeled dummy

### Property spec format (to define/decide)

Need to establish the TOML format for property specs. Likely:


### Runner integration

Extend the test runner (src/sxact/runner/) to:
- Load property specs from tests/properties/
- Generate random inputs per the forall types
- Run statement on each adapter and compare results

## Key Files

- src/sxact/compare/sampling.py — random scalar sampling (already works for scalars)
- src/sxact/runner/ — existing test runner to extend
- specs/2026-01-09-three-layer-testing-architecture.md — architecture spec
- tests/xcore/*.toml — Layer 1 test examples for reference

## Research Needed

1. **Property spec format**: Needs design decision — what does the TOML schema look like? Check if spec proposes one.
2. **Runner extension**: How does property testing plug into the existing runner? New command? Flag?
3. **Which xCore properties are testable at scalar level?** Review xcore/__init__.py (611 lines) for functions that accept scalars.
4. **Hypothesis library?** Consider using Hypothesis (Python property testing library) as the random generator rather than rolling custom.

## Success Criteria

- 15+ property specs written
- Properties run against WolframAdapter and pass
- At least 5 properties run cross-adapter (Wolfram vs Julia or Wolfram vs Python)
close_reason: Created 29 Layer 2 property specs across 3 files: tests/properties/xcore_symbol_laws.toml (12 xCore invariants: dagger involution, link/unlink roundtrip, deduplication, JustOne), riemann_symmetries.toml (10 Riemann curvature invariants: antisymmetry, pair exchange, Bianchi identities, metric compatibility), and tensor_algebra_laws.toml (7 general tensor algebra laws: commutativity, associativity, distributivity, identities). Format follows specs/2026-03-05-property-test-design.md.

title: AST normalization: replace regex pipeline with S-expression parser
description:
## Why

The current normalization pipeline (src/sxact/normalize/pipeline.py) uses regex and naive string splits. This produces false negatives (incorrect 'not equal') for:
- Nested brackets: CD[-a][CD[-b][T[c]]] 
- Nested sums: (A + B) + (C + D) — split(' + ') breaks on sub-expression additions
- Complex coefficient forms

The spec (specs/2026-03-03-ast-normalization.md) calls for a tree-based approach.

## What to Do

### Step 1: Configure oracle to emit FullForm

The OracleClient (src/sxact/oracle/client.py) should wrap expressions in ToString[expr, FullForm] so the parser only deals with unambiguous prefix notation: Plus[a, b] not a + b.

### Step 2: S-Expression Parser

Implement a recursive-descent parser in src/sxact/normalize/ast_parser.py:
- Input: FullForm string like Plus[Times[2, a], b]
- Output: Node(head='Plus', args=[Node('Times', [Leaf(2), Leaf('a')]), Leaf('b')])
- Handle: Head[arg1, arg2], Leaf atoms (symbols, integers, reals)

### Step 3: Normalization passes (operate on tree)

In src/sxact/normalize/passes.py:
- **CanonicalizeIndicesPass**: DFS traversal, rename first-seen index to $1, second to $2, etc. Preserves -/+ distinction.
- **SortCommutativePass**: For Plus/Times nodes, sort args list by strict serialization key
- **CoefficientFlatteningPass**: Collect numeric coefficients to standard position

### Step 4: Strict serializer

Output whitespace-free canonical string for Tier 1 comparison and sorting.

### Step 5: Update pipeline.py to use new parser

Keep old regex functions available but route through AST pipeline by default. Feature flag during transition.

## Key Files

- src/sxact/normalize/pipeline.py — current implementation (replace/extend)
- src/sxact/oracle/client.py — configure FullForm output
- specs/2026-03-03-ast-normalization.md — full spec

## Success Criteria

- normalize('A[a] + B[b]') == normalize('B[a] + A[b]') → True
- Handles 3+ levels of nested brackets without false negatives
- All existing normalize tests still pass
- Integration suite: zero failures caused by index naming differences

## Research Needed

- Check if OracleClient already has a FullForm mode or needs a new method
- Determine if JuliaAdapter output is also in FullForm or needs separate parser
- Assess whether changing oracle output format breaks existing snapshots
close_reason: Implemented AST-based normalization engine: ast_parser.py (recursive-descent FullForm parser), passes.py (sort_commutative, canonicalize_indices, flatten_coefficients), serializer.py (canonical serializer). Added ast_normalize() to pipeline.py with fallback to regex on parse failure. Existing normalize() unchanged (regex). 17 new tests cover parser, commutativity (FullForm inputs), nested brackets, coefficient simplification.

FoldedRule represents a chain of rule lists applied sequentially: applying FoldedRule(rules1, rules2) to expr means apply rules2 to the result of applying rules1. Also implement CollapseFoldedRule, DependentRules, IndependentRules, MapRuleR, MapRuleL. Source reference: resources/xAct/xCore/xCore.m lines 562-596. If the sxAct-0q8 analysis determines FoldedRule is not needed by downstream packages, close this ticket with a one-line rationale.

title: Implement extension mechanism (xTension/MakexTensions) in Julia
description: Implement the Julia equivalent of xCore extension mechanism (xTension and MakexTensions). Background: xTension lets packages register hooks that fire at the Beginning or End of def-commands like DefMetric, without modifying the original package source. In Julia this should be a hook registry: xtension(package, defcommand, moment, fn) registers fn, and make_xtensions(defcommand, moment, args...) fires all registered hooks for that command-moment combination. Source reference: resources/xAct/xCore/xCore.m line 606. Unit tests must verify hooks fire in registration order and at both Beginning and End moments.
close_reason: Implementation and tests already complete in XCore.jl and runtests.jl. All 131 tests pass including 6 dedicated xTension/MakexTensions tests plus L2 property tests.

title: Implement WolframAdapter wrapping oracle client
description: Implement WolframAdapter: a concrete TestAdapter (sxAct-hij) that wraps the existing OracleClient at src/sxact/oracle/client.py. Maps adapter execute() calls to oracle evaluate_with_xact(). Manages the context_id lifecycle per test file. Must pass the adapter conformance test suite from sxAct-hij. All existing integration tests in tests/integration/ must continue to pass.
close_reason: WolframAdapter implemented in src/sxact/adapter/wolfram.py; conformance suite in tests/test_wolfram_adapter.py; 152 tests passing

title: Implement symbol registry and ValidateSymbol equivalent in Julia
description: Implement the Julia equivalent of xCore symbol registry and ValidateSymbol. Source reference: resources/xAct/xCore/xCore.m lines 678-779. Responsibilities: (1) a module-level registry mapping symbol names to owning packages, (2) validate_symbol(name) that throws if the name collides with reserved names or is already registered by another package, (3) register_symbol(name, package) that adds to the registry, (4) Julia equivalents of the dollar-Names lists ($xCoreNames, $xTensorNames, etc.). Unit tests must cover all error branches from the original ValidateSymbol implementation.
close_reason: Fully implemented in src/julia/XCore.jl with tests in runtests.jl

title: Design Julia xCore module structure and API mapping
description: Design the Julia module structure for XCore.jl based on the function categorization from sxAct-0q8. Reference resources/xAct/xCore/xCore.m for the source. Output: (1) Julia package skeleton at src/julia/XCore.jl with submodule structure if needed, (2) API mapping table listing xCore function to Julia equivalent or new implementation plan, (3) explicit decision on whether FoldedRule is needed based on xTensor and xPerm usage. The design document lives as notes on this ticket. Implementation tickets sxAct-zaj, sxAct-j4d, sxAct-2nf must not start until this ticket is marked complete.
notes:
## FoldedRule decision: SKIP

FoldedRule, CollapseFoldedRule, DependentRules, IndependentRules are all Category C. Zero references in xTensor or xPerm. They are a Wolfram-specific rule-sequencing idiom with no downstream consumers in the packages being ported. Not implemented in XCore.jl.

---

## Julia module structure decision: FLAT (single module, no submodules)

34 must-implement symbols do not justify submodule overhead. XCore.jl is one module with 11 clearly labelled sections. If section 5 (xUpvalues) or section 8 (extensions) grow substantially during implementation they can be split into sub-files included via `include()`.

Package layout:
  src/julia/
  ├── Project.toml   (uuid 61ed89dc-cc38-478d-be53-e1ee1d7160f1, julia ≥ 1.9)
  └── XCore.jl       (module XCore, all 34 Category-A + 2 Category-B aliases)

---

## API mapping table (Category A)

| xCore symbol | Julia approach | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| SetNumberOfArguments | no-op shim | Julia arity checked at compile time via dispatch |
| AppendToUnevaluated | alias for push\! | Julia has no lazy OwnValues; push\! is equivalent |
| JustOne | custom function | 1-liner with length check |
| MapIfPlus | custom function | dispatch on AbstractVector/Tuple vs scalar |
| DeleteDuplicates | unique (Base) | Category B; order-preserving by definition |
| DuplicateFreeQ | allunique (Base) | Category B |
| SymbolJoin | Symbol(join(string.(args))) | 1-liner |
| NoPattern | identity | no Pattern wrappers in Julia |
| xUpSet | xUpSet\!(prop, tag, val) → Dict | _upvalue_store[tag][prop] = val |
| xUpSetDelayed | xUpSetDelayed\!(prop, tag, thunk) | stores 0-arg thunk |
| xUpAppendTo | xUpAppendTo\!(prop, tag, el) | push\! into Dict-stored vector |
| xUpDeleteCasesTo | xUpDeleteCasesTo\!(prop, tag, pred) | filter\! on Dict-stored vector |
| xTagSet | xTagSet\!(tag, key, val) | delegates to xUpSet\! with :tag_ prefix |
| xTagSetDelayed | xTagSetDelayed\!(tag, key, thunk) | delegates to xUpSetDelayed\! |
| SubHead | SubHead(e) → Symbol | dispatches on Symbol/Expr/other |
| xEvaluateAt | no-op shim | Julia evaluates eagerly; nothing to force |
| xHold | struct XHold{T} | wrapper with custom show; semantics match |
| CheckOptions | custom function | flattens and type-checks for Pair |
| TrueOrFalse | TrueOrFalse(::Bool)=true, else false | 2-method dispatch |
| xTension | xTension\!(pkg, cmd, moment, func) | _xtensions Dict stores hook vectors |
| MakexTensions | MakexTensions(cmd, moment, args...) | iterates and calls hooks |
| ValidateSymbol | custom function | checks xCore/xPerm/xTensor lists + Base exports |
| FindSymbols | custom function | recursive on Symbol/Expr/collection |
| HasDaggerCharacterQ | occursin(DaggerCharacter[], string(s)) | |
| MakeDaggerSymbol | custom function | toggle dagger before $ or at end |
| $DaggerCharacter | const DaggerCharacter = Ref{String}('†') | mutable ref |
| $xActDirectory | const xActDirectory = Ref{String}('') | populated at load time |
| $xActDocDirectory | const xActDocDirectory = Ref{String}('') | |
| $WarningFrom | const WarningFrom = Ref{String}('XCore') | |
| $xPermNames | const xPermNames = String[] | populated by xPerm package |
| $xTensorNames | const xTensorNames = String[] | populated by xTensor package |
| $xCoreNames | const xCoreNames = String[] | populated at load time |
| Disclaimer | custom function | println of GPL text |

---

## Output artifacts

- src/julia/Project.toml  — package manifest (uuid, julia ≥ 1.9, no dependencies)
- src/julia/XCore.jl      — module XCore with all 34 symbols; ready for implementation tickets
close_reason: Julia package skeleton created at src/julia/XCore.jl + Project.toml. API mapping table and FoldedRule=SKIP decision recorded in notes.

title: Documentation: Differential Geometry Primer for xActCore
description:
This task is to create a document that serves as a primer on the key concepts of differential geometry needed to understand the xActCore codebase. This document will be aimed at new contributors who may have a programming background but need a refresher on the relevant mathematics.

### Topics to Cover:

1.  **Manifolds:**
    *   What is a manifold?
    *   Charts, atlases, and coordinate systems.

2.  **Vectors and Tensors:**
    *   Tangent and cotangent spaces.
    *   Vectors as directional derivatives.
    *   Covectors (one-forms) as linear maps.
    *   Tensors as multilinear maps.

3.  **Tensor Algebra:**
    *   Tensor product.
    *   Tensor contraction.
    *   Abstract index notation vs. component-based notation.

4.  **Metric Tensor:**
    *   The role of the metric in defining geometry.
    *   Raising and lowering indices.
    *   Calculating lengths, angles, and volumes.

5.  **Differential Forms:**
    *   The wedge product.
    *   The exterior derivative.

6.  **Covariant Derivative:**
    *   The concept of a connection and parallel transport.
    *   The covariant derivative of a tensor field.
    *   Christoffel symbols.

7.  **Curvature:**
    *   The Riemann curvature tensor.
    *   The Ricci tensor and Ricci scalar.
    *   The Einstein tensor.

8.  **Symmetries of Tensors:**
    *   Symmetric and antisymmetric tensors.
    *   Symmetries of the Riemann tensor (e.g., Bianchi identities).
close_reason: Created comprehensive differential geometry primer covering all 8 requested topics: manifolds, tensors, tensor algebra, metric, differential forms, covariant derivative, curvature, and tensor symmetries

title: Epic: Migrate xCore to Julia/Python
description: This epic tracks the migration of the xCore module from Wolfram xAct to a new implementation in Julia with a Python wrapper. xCore is the programming infrastructure and utility layer that all other xAct packages (xTensor, xPerm, etc.) depend on. It provides: symbol registry and validation (ValidateSymbol, $-Names lists), an extension mechanism that lets packages augment Def commands (xTension/MakexTensions), a rule composition system (FoldedRule and utilities), upvalue management patterns (xUpSet, xTagSet, etc.), and assorted programming utilities (argument validation, list ops, option checking, symbol linking, dagger handling). The exact set of must-implement functions is determined by sxAct-0q8. NOT migrated: string formatting utilities (Underline, Overline, TildeString, etc. are Wolfram notebook display only) and the testing framework (xTest is replaced by pytest and Julia Test).

title: Phase 4: Integration & Validation
description:
## Status: Partially Complete

Write integration tests to validate the full pipeline with real xAct.

## Progress

### Done
- ✅ Created tests/integration/__init__.py
- ✅ Created tests/integration/test_xact_basics.py with 11 tests
- ✅ All tests marked @pytest.mark.oracle and @pytest.mark.slow
- ✅ Documented xAct quirks in docs/oracle-quirks.md

### Test Results: 8/11 Passing

**Passing (8):**
1. TestDefineManifold::test_define_manifold_returns_manifold_info
2. TestDefineManifold::test_manifold_dimension
3. TestDefineMetric::test_define_metric_with_signature
4. TestSymmetricTensor::test_symmetric_tensor_swap_indices
5. TestToCanonical::test_tocanonical_reorders_indices
6. TestMetricContraction::test_metric_contraction_raises_index
7. TestRiemannTensor::test_riemann_exists_after_metric_definition
8. TestNumericSampling::test_numeric_evaluation_of_scalar_expression

**Failing (3) - blocked by sxAct-7g6:**
1. TestSymbolicEquality::test_symmetric_tensor_sum_equals_double
2. TestAntisymmetricTensor::test_antisymmetric_tensor_swap_negates
3. TestBianchiIdentity::test_riemann_first_bianchi_structure

## Blocker

Issue sxAct-7g6: Symbol context pollution breaks xAct tensor operations.
wolframclient parses symbols into Global` context before xAct sees them.

## Remaining Work

1. Fix context pollution (sxAct-7g6)
2. Re-run all tests after fix
3. Verify all three comparison tiers exercised

## Key Learnings

- Use unique symbol names per test (M1, M2, etc.) to avoid protection errors
- Avoid reserved names: N, I, E, C, D, O
- Use SignDetOfMetric instead of SignatureOfMetric
- Multi-call tests fail due to context pollution between calls
close_reason: All 12/12 integration tests passing. Three-tier comparator exercised: Tier 1 (normalized string), Tier 2 (symbolic oracle), Tier 3 (numeric sampling). Context isolation fix from sxAct-7g6 resolved the remaining 3 failing tests.

title: Phase 3: Three-Tier Comparator
description:
Implement three-tier comparison strategy for expression equivalence.

## Comparator Module
- src/sxact/compare/__init__.py
- src/sxact/compare/comparator.py
  - EqualityMode enum: NORMALIZED, SYMBOLIC, NUMERIC
  - CompareResult dataclass (equal, tier, confidence, diff)
  - compare(lhs, rhs, oracle) function

## Numeric Sampling
- src/sxact/compare/sampling.py
  - sample_numeric(lhs, rhs, oracle, n=10, seed=42)

## Three Tiers
1. Tier 1: Normalized string comparison (pure Python)
2. Tier 2: Symbolic diff=0 via oracle Simplify
3. Tier 3: Numeric sampling fallback

## Tests (TDD)
- tests/compare/__init__.py
- tests/compare/test_comparator.py

## Success Criteria
- Tier 1 catches exact matches
- Tier 2 catches T[-a,-b] + T[-b,-a] == 2*T[-a,-b] for symmetric T
- uv run pytest tests/compare/ passes

Create Result dataclass and normalization pipeline.

## Normalization Pipeline
- src/sxact/normalize/__init__.py
- src/sxact/normalize/pipeline.py - normalize() function with:
  1. Whitespace normalization
  2. Dummy index canonicalization ($1, $2, ...)
  3. Term ordering (lexicographic)
  4. Coefficient normalization

## Tests (TDD)
- tests/normalize/__init__.py
- tests/normalize/test_pipeline.py

## Success Criteria
- T[-a, -b] → T[-$1, -$2]
- B + A → A + B
- 2*x → 2 x
- uv run pytest tests/normalize/ passes

Plan: plans/2026-01-22-sprint1-derisk-equivalence.md
Depends: sxAct-jmt
close_reason: Closed

title: Strengthen specs with missing design phases
description:
## Summary

Oracle review of specs against 6-phase design framework identified significant gaps in Phases 1-2 (problem definition) and Phase 5 (tactical design).

## Root Cause

Specs jumped to Phase 5 (Design) without rigorous Phase 1-2 (Describe/Diagnose). This leaves key architectural decisions unjustified and implementation-blocking details unspecified.

## Required Changes

### Phase 1-2: Add Problem Statement & Root Causes

Add section to specs with:
- **Concrete failure modes** (dummy index mismatch, stateful definitions, oracle version drift, symbolic vs string equality)
- **Root cause → consequence table**:
  | Root Cause | Design Consequence |
  |------------|-------------------|
  | Dummy indices differ across impls | Need alpha-equivalence comparator |
  | CAS definitions are stateful | Isolation strategy per-test/per-file |
  | Oracle outputs version-dependent | Pin versions + normalize before hash |
  | Symbolic ≠ textual equality | Three-tier comparison layer |

### Phase 3: Explicit Scope Boundaries

- Non-goals (pretty-printing, UI, packages beyond core trio)
- Oracle policy: ground truth vs math laws when they conflict
- CI/environment: Wolfram licensing, supported versions, local vs CI

### Phase 4: Missing Comparisons

Add alternatives-considered for:
1. **Equivalence strategy**: string vs AST/IR vs semantic (diff→0) vs numeric sampling
2. **Oracle strategy**: golden files vs live execution
3. **Isolation strategy**: per-test vs per-file vs shared session

### Phase 5: Tactical Gaps

1. **Action vocabulary**: canonical list with args schema per action
2. **Adapter contract**: WolframAdapter / JuliaAdapter / PythonAdapter interface
3. **Comparator module**: Normalization + Equivalence (three-tier)
4. **Isolation semantics**: state machine (setup→ops→capture→compare→report)
5. **Benchmark protocol**: warm-up, sample size, JIT separation, variance policy

### Phase 6: Reorder Roadmap

Attack unknowns first:
1. Oracle runner + output normalization
2. Equivalence checker (alpha-equiv, symbolic diff=0, numeric fallback)
3. Then scale extraction and property generators

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Root cause → consequence table added to spec
- [ ] Non-goals section added
- [ ] Alternatives-considered for equivalence/oracle/isolation
- [ ] Action vocabulary defined with schemas
- [ ] Adapter interface specified
- [ ] Three-tier comparison design documented
- [ ] Roadmap reordered to de-risk unknowns first
close_reason: Closed

title: Publish post-registration discoverability package for XAct.jl
description:
Source: /var/home/sasha/Downloads/xact-docs.md

Execution mode
- Mixed AFK + HITL
- AFK: prepare repo/docs/discoverability assets
- HITL: publish the Julia Discourse announcement and apply any account-level repository metadata changes that require a human session

Problem
- The external review highlighted weak discoverability beyond the repository itself.
- Registration in General Registry is already tracked by sxAct-kncc, but there is no issue for the follow-on launch/discovery work that helps users and JOSS reviewers actually find the project.

Blocked by
- sxAct-kncc (General Registry registration)
- sxAct-3db5 (branding/naming decision) when final copy depends on the outcome

Scope
- Add/verify GitHub topics and repository metadata.
- Prepare the announcement copy with install path, scope, limitations, and docs links.
- Publish a Julia Discourse announcement (ANN) once registration is live.
- Ensure README/docs installation instructions align with the registered install path.
- Link the announcement from README/docs if appropriate.

Files / systems
- README.md
- docs/src/index.md
- docs/src/installation.md
- docs/src/getting-started.md
- GitHub repository metadata/topics
- Julia Discourse announcement post

Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Repository metadata/topics are set for Julia, symbolic-computation, tensor-algebra, and relativity-adjacent discovery.
- [ ] Announcement copy exists and is ready before the HITL publishing step.
- [ ] A Julia Discourse announcement exists and is linked from the repository or docs.
- [ ] Install instructions no longer rely on unreleased/manual paths once registration lands.
- [ ] Human-only steps are called out explicitly in the issue or linked notes.

Verification

- Confirm the registered install path is documented consistently.

Workflow mandate

title: Implement SpinFrames: spinor frame formalism
description:
Port the SpinFrames module from xAct 1.3.0. SpinFrames extends the Spinors module with a full spinor frame/tetrad formalism, enabling component calculations in the spinor basis. This module is new in xAct 1.3.0 (not present in 1.2.0). Must be completed after Spinors (sxAct-3s3): Newman-Penrose and GHP equations, and requires the oracle to be on 1.3.0 (sxAct-ltaa) since there is no 1.2.0 reference to test against.

Acceptance: spin-frame basis construction and component extraction match xAct 1.3.0 SpinFrames oracle output.

TDD entry: create test/spinframes_test.jl with one failing @test for spin-frame basis construction. Implement in src/SpinFrames.jl.

title: Implement SymManipulator: symmetrized tensor expression tools
description:
Port the SymManipulator module from xAct. Provides tools for working with symmetrized tensor expressions: explicit Young symmetrizers, symmetric/antisymmetric projectors, and manipulation of totally (anti)symmetric sub-expressions. Note: partial overlap with XPerm's Young tableaux functionality — scope should be clarified during design (a scoping subtask should be opened before claiming this ticket).

Acceptance: symmetrize(T[a,b]) produces output matching the xAct SymManipulator oracle for standard cases; antisymmetrize(T[a,b,c]) similarly verified.

TDD entry: create test/symmanipulator_test.jl with one failing @test for symmetrize. Implement in src/SymManipulator.jl.

title: Implement xPrint: formatted tensor expression input
description:
Port the xPrint module from xAct, which provides a formatted/interactive input interface for tensor expressions — allowing researchers to enter expressions in a notation closer to standard index notation rather than the string DSL. Currently 'not yet started'. Should integrate with TExpr typed layer and the @indices macro infrastructure.

Acceptance example: @xprint T[a,b] + S[a,b] parses to the same internal expression as the programmatic construction T[a,b] + S[a,b]; round-trip identity holds.

TDD entry: create test/xprint_test.jl with one failing @test for the @xprint macro parse. Implement in src/XPrint.jl.

title: Implement Harmonics: tensor spherical harmonic decomposition
description:
Port the Harmonics module from xAct. Provides tensor spherical harmonic decomposition used in cosmological perturbation theory and gravitational wave data analysis. xPert (sxAct-hyy) is complete and provides the required perturbation infrastructure. Currently listed as 'not yet started' in README. Milestone: reproduce the standard scalar/vector/tensor decomposition of metric perturbations on S² as documented in the xAct Harmonics notebook.

Acceptance: scalar/vector/tensor SVT decomposition of a 2-tensor on S² matches xAct Harmonics oracle output.

TDD entry: create test/harmonics_test.jl with one failing @test for the SVT decomposition. Implement in src/Harmonics.jl.

title: Add CITATION.cff file
description:
GitHub's 'Cite this repository' button requires a CITATION.cff file. Expected for academic packages and JOSS submissions.

Create skeleton now; DOI field is TBD (HITL: populated after JOSS assigns a DOI post-submission).
Done when: CITATION.cff exists with all fields except doi, which is marked as TBD.

Should reference:
- The paper title, authors, and repository URL
- The xAct upstream packages being implemented
