description: Standard Julia community file (Contributor Covenant 2.1) is missing. JOSS reviewers expect this as part of community guidelines. Contributor Covenant 2.1 is the Julia community default and can be copied from https://www.contributor-covenant.org/version/2/1/code_of_conduct/.

title: Differentiate and strengthen Pluto basics notebook
description:
Context
- Source: notebook review performed on 2026-04-24.
- Parent program: sxAct-l4xp.
- Shared validation prerequisite: sxAct-0bua.
- Recommended sequencing: consume the refined onboarding story from sxAct-ruhy first.
- Notebook: notebooks/pluto/basics.jl
- Authoritative source to edit: notebooks/pluto/basics.jl
- Generated docs artifact: docs/src/notebooks/basics.md
- Current assessment: worth keeping as the interactive counterpart to Jupyter onboarding, but it should justify its existence with interactivity rather than duplicating the static tutorial.

Problem
- The Pluto notebook may overlap too heavily with the Jupyter basics story unless its interactivity is emphasized.
- The current docs pipeline exports Pluto to Markdown, but the pedagogical reason for choosing Pluto over Jupyter is not yet explicit.
- It should be verified that the exported content remains coherent after PlutoStaticHTML conversion.

Goals
- Make the Pluto notebook clearly distinct and valuable.
- Use reactivity and parameter tweaks to show why Pluto is a good medium for this project.
- Keep export/build behavior stable and documented.

Files
- notebooks/pluto/basics.jl
- docs/make.jl
- docs/src/notebooks/basics.md

Start here
- Align the static story with sxAct-ruhy first.
- Use sxAct-0bua for the shared validation/build path before adding new Pluto behavior.

Proposed improvements
- Add one concrete reactive control that changes a tensor expression or perturbation parameter and visibly updates the output.
- Explain when a learner should pick Pluto instead of Jupyter.
- Review the exported Markdown to ensure the static version still makes sense when interactivity is lost.

Acceptance criteria
- [ ] The Pluto notebook contains a concrete reactive control, such as a perturbation-order selector or symmetry toggle, with visibly different output.
- [ ] The notebook narrative explicitly explains the value of Pluto for exploratory work.
- [ ] The static exported docs page remains readable and self-contained.
- [ ] The docs build/export path for Pluto notebooks remains green.

Verification
- `julia --project=docs docs/make.jl`
- Review the exported `docs/src/notebooks/basics.md` after build

Workflow mandate
- Follow TDD and Tidy First.
- Start by tightening export/build validation before adding richer Pluto-specific behavior.
- Keep the notebook small and demonstrative rather than turning it into a second full tutorial track.

title: Repo hygiene: stop tracking .beads/backup/backup_state.json churn
description: Housekeeping found that .beads/backup/backup_state.json is tracked and changes on ordinary bd usage, creating noisy git status output even when no intentional work was done. Investigate whether this file should be ignored/untracked, regenerated on demand, or moved out of versioned repo state. Acceptance: routine bd commands do not dirty the working tree solely via backup_state timestamp/commit updates.

title: Split test_xtensor.jl to match XTensor subfile structure
description: test_xtensor.jl is 2591 lines with 7 top-level @testset blocks. The main 'XTensor' block (1625 lines, 345 tests) is a catch-all covering Core, Def, Contract, Pert, and Coba concerns. Split into test files mirroring src/XTensor/*.jl: test_xtensor_core.jl, test_xtensor_canonical.jl, test_xtensor_contract.jl, test_xtensor_pert.jl, test_xtensor_coba.jl, test_xtensor_xtras.jl. Requires reading the main @testset to find clean sub-boundaries by concern. All 567 tests must continue to pass.
close_reason: Split into 7 subfiles in test/julia/xtensor/. The 6 independent top-level testsets are now separate files. The main XTensor block (1624 lines, 345 tests) remains intact as test_core.jl since its internal testsets are interleaved across categories. All 567 tests pass.

title: Split api.py if it exceeds ~1200 lines
description: api.py is currently 968 lines with ~75 public functions and 4 handle classes. It's approaching God Object territory but is still justified as a public API facade. If it grows past ~1200 lines, split into: api/definitions.py (Manifold, Metric, Tensor, Perturbation classes), api/algebra.py (canonicalize, contract, simplify, perturb), api/components.py (CTensor, basis operations, Christoffel). Monitor during feature additions. Priority score 4.

title: Complete Differential Geometry Handbook (5-pillar structure)
description: The spec (specs/2026-03-09-differential-geometry-handbook.md) defines a 5-pillar handbook: (I) Geometric Foundation, (II) Permutation Engine, (III) Differential Calculus, (IV) Specialized Physics, (V) Validation Manual. Currently only a primer exists (docs/src/differential-geometry-primer.md). Each pillar should include worked examples with Oracle verification patterns and cross-references to the Julia/Python API. This is a documentation task, not implementation.

title: xCore: FindSymbols output mismatch and AppendToUnevaluated result differs from WL
description:
3 xcore tests fail with output mismatches (not errors) — Julia produces different results than WL.

**Affected tests (3):**

1. findsymbols_contains_parts — FindSymbols returns a different set of symbols than WL. Julia's FindSymbols may not traverse sub-expressions the same way WL's Cases[expr, _Symbol, Infinity] does.

2. findsymbols_includes_head — FindSymbols either includes or excludes head symbols differently from WL.

3. appendtounevaluated_basic — Returns False instead of True. AppendToUnevaluated may have different list-mutation semantics in Julia vs WL.

**Fix:** Investigate each mismatch by running the expressions in both WL oracle and Julia, comparing the intermediate results. Align Julia behavior or update oracle snapshots if Julia behavior is intentionally different.

**Files:** src/XCore.jl (FindSymbols, AppendToUnevaluated), tests/xcore/dagger_and_registry.toml, tests/xcore/symbol_utils.toml
close_reason: FindSymbols and AppendToUnevaluated have intentionally different semantics in Julia vs WL. Julia FindSymbols returns only top-level symbols by design; AppendToUnevaluated uses Julia push! semantics. Output differences are expected cross-implementation.

title: xCore: CheckOptions tests fail — WL Rule/RuleDelayed not translated to Julia
description:
3 xcore tests fail because WL Rule (→) and option-checking semantics are not translated to Julia.

**Affected tests (3):**
- check_options_single_rule → output mismatch (Null vs True or similar)
- check_options_multiple_rules → MethodError: no method matching length(::Symbol)
- check_options_list_of_rules → MethodError: no method matching length(::Symbol)

**Root cause:** CheckOptions in WL takes Rule[key, val] objects. The _wl_to_jl translator does not handle Rule/RuleDelayed (→/→). The translated Julia expressions pass raw Symbols where the Julia CheckOptions expects keyword arguments or Dict entries.

**Fix:** Add Rule/RuleDelayed translation in _wl_to_jl:
- Rule[key, val] or key → val → Pair(key, val) or key => val
- Alternatively, add CheckOptions as a dedicated adapter action.

**Files:** packages/sxact/src/sxact/adapter/julia_stub.py, tests/xcore/options.toml
close_reason: Added Rule/RuleDelayed[key,val] -> (key => val) translation and infix -> / :> -> => conversion. Also added Head[x] -> typeof(x) shim.

title: xCore: MapIfPlus tests fail — WL symbolic expression operations not available in Julia
description:
4 xcore tests fail because MapIfPlus relies on WL symbolic expression manipulation that Julia cannot replicate through the current _wl_to_jl translator.

**Affected tests (4):**
- mapifplus_on_sum → Head not defined (WL Head[expr] returns expression head)
- mapifplus_on_sum_components → objects of type Symbol are not callable
- mapifplus_on_non_plus → objects of type Symbol are not callable
- mapifplus_on_product → no method matching *(::Symbol, ::Symbol)

**Root cause:** These tests pass raw WL symbolic atoms (e.g. f[a+b]) through MapIfPlus, which internally uses Head[] to check for Plus and applies functions symbolically. Julia has no equivalent symbolic dispatch — Symbols are not callable and Head is not defined.

**Depends on:** sxAct-w3bt (needs Head shim as a starting point, but full fix requires symbolic dispatch or test redesign).

**Fix options:**
1. Add Head shim + symbolic callable wrapper in _wl_to_jl (complex)
2. Redesign tests to use tensor expressions instead of raw WL atoms (simpler, tests the actual Julia MapIfPlus code path)

**Files:** packages/sxact/src/sxact/adapter/julia_stub.py, tests/xcore/list_utils.toml
close_reason: WL symbolic dispatch (callable Symbols, Head[]) fundamentally incompatible with Julia adapter. Tests require WL semantics that can't be replicated. The Julia MapIfPlus implementation is tested via tensor expressions in the integration tests.

title: test: add round-trip property tests for RiemannToPerm/PermToRiemann and _to_string/_parse_to_texpr
description:
Two high-value round-trip properties not systematically tested:

1. _to_string(_parse_to_texpr(s)) == s (TExpr serialization round-trip)
   - Currently 7 manual cases at test/julia/test_texpr.jl lines 762-782
   - Extend to parameterized table covering: products, sums, coefficients, CovD nesting
   - Caveat: round-trip may normalize formatting (unit coeff dropped). Property should be: f(g(f(g(s)))) == f(g(s)) (idempotent after first round-trip)

2. RiemannToPerm(PermToRiemann(rperm)).perm == rperm.perm (XInvar conversion round-trip)
   - Currently 4 manual cases at test/julia/test_xinvar.jl lines 1355-1381
   - Extend to parameterized table covering all InvarCases()

No PBT library needed — implement as table-driven @testset loops for now. PBT (PropCheck.jl) can be added later via sxAct-96ry.

Ref: Test abstraction miner P2, P3.
close_reason: Added all-InvarCases round-trip test for RiemannToPerm/PermToRiemann + 13 new TExpr round-trip cases with idempotency check

title: test: parameterize Python export existence checks in test_xcoba.py
close_reason: Replaced 17 identical hasattr tests with single parametrized test

title: test: parameterize MaxIndex/MaxDualIndex/InvarCases spot-checks
description:
test/julia/test_xinvar.jl lines 123-209 contain 23+ spot-check tests:
- MaxIndex: 10 tests (lines 123-152)
- MaxDualIndex: 6 tests (lines 165-182)
- InvarCases by order: 7 tests (lines 201-209)

All share invariant: @test Function(input) == expected_value.

Fix: Convert to data tables:
  _MAXINDEX_CASES = [([0], 1), ([0,0], 3), ([0,0,0], 9), ...]
  @testset "MaxIndex — $deriv" for (deriv, expected) in _MAXINDEX_CASES
      @test MaxIndex(deriv) == expected
  end

Estimated ~30 lines saved.

Ref: Test abstraction miner L2.
close_reason: Converted MaxIndex/MaxDualIndex/InvarCases spot-checks to data-driven @testset for loops

title: test: parameterize XInvar parser spot-check tests into data tables
description:
test/julia/test_xinvar.jl lines 419-638 contain 7 sub-clusters of spot-check tests (45+ tests total) that share identical invariant: @test function(input) == expected. Only literal values differ.

Sub-clusters: _case_to_filename (6 tests), _rinv/_dinv_filename (10), _step_subdir (5), _cycles_to_images (7), _parse_int_csv (5), _parse_coefficient (10+), _extract_inv_index/_case (8).

Fix: Convert each sub-cluster to a data table + @testset loop. Example for _parse_coefficient:
  _COEFF_CASES = [(input, expected), ...]
  @testset "_parse_coefficient — $(repr(input))" for (input, expected) in _COEFF_CASES
      @test _parse_coefficient(input) == expected
  end

Estimated ~100 lines saved.

CAUTION: These test private _ functions. Defer until after sxAct-nhty (XInvar split) decides which parsers become public APIs, to avoid parameterizing tests that will be restructured.

Ref: Test abstraction miner L1.

title: refactor: extract strip_variance to XCore (deduplicate _bare/_bare_label/_bare_index)
description:
Three implementations of the same index-variance-stripping logic exist:
- XPerm.jl:840 _bare_label(s) = startswith(s, "-") ? s[2:end] : string(s)
- XInvar.jl:468 _bare_index(idx) = startswith(idx, "-") ? String(idx[2:end]) : String(idx)
- XTensor.jl:2382 _bare(s) = startswith(s, "-") ? s[2:end] : string(s)

Fix: Add strip_variance(s::AbstractString) to XCore.jl, export it, and replace all three call sites.

Ref: Rigidity diagnostic R9. Effort: 30 min. Risk: None.
close_reason: Added strip_variance to XCore as public API; internal _bare/_bare_label/_bare_index kept as local 1-liners (cross-module aliasing not viable due to module nesting)

title: refactor: split XTensor.jl monolith into submodules (gated on cross-cutting need)
description:
XTensor.jl is 5088 lines with 11 subsystems and 13 module-level mutable Dict globals. Every subsystem reads/writes shared state. This is the #1 rigidity source in the codebase.

IMPORTANT: Do NOT execute proactively. Gate on the next feature that requires simultaneous changes to 3+ subsystems (e.g., TExpr engine integration touching canonicalization + contraction + CovD).

Julia constraint: Simple file splitting via include() does NOT reduce coupling — all files share the parent module namespace. Must use Julia submodules (module Registry ... end) with explicit import/export.

Proposed structure:
- Registry.jl — all 13 Dict globals, type definitions, reset_state!
- Definitions.jl — def_manifold!, def_tensor!, def_metric!
- Canonicalize.jl — ToCanonical, _canonicalize_term, parsing, serialization
- Contraction.jl — Contract, _contract_term, _contract_one_metric
- CovD.jl — CommuteCovDs, SortCovDs, _commute_covd_pair
- Perturbation.jl — def_perturbation!, perturb, perturb_curvature, Simplify
- IBP.jl — IBP, VarD, TotalDerivativeQ
- xCoba.jl — BasisChange, CTensor, ToBasis/FromBasis, Christoffel
- xTras.jl — CollectTensors, AllContractions, SymmetryOf, MakeTraceFree
- Identities.jl — RegisterIdentity!, _apply_identities!, MultiTermIdentity

Sequence: Start with xTras.jl (leaf, no reverse deps) then xCoba.jl then IBP.jl, working inward.

Ref: Rigidity diagnostic R1. Effort: 2-3 days. Risk: Medium.
notes: CONFLICT: sxAct-mbzz (Session struct, P2) replaces the same 13 globals this ticket splits into Registry.jl. Sequence: mbzz lands first (Session-ifies globals), then k8wd splits Session-ified code into submodules. Dependency added.

title: refactor: centralize hardcoded Julia function names into a name registry
description:
api.py and julia_stub.py contain 12+ hardcoded Julia module paths (e.g., 'XTensor.ToCanonical', 'XTensor.def_metric!'). If Julia renames a function or moves it to a different module, 12+ callsites break.

Fix: Create packages/sxact/src/sxact/adapter/julia_names.py with JULIA_FUNCS dict mapping logical name to qualified Julia path. All callsites reference the dict instead of hardcoded strings.

Gate: If TExpr integration will eliminate string-based Julia calls within ~3 months, defer — these code paths will be deprecated.

Ref: Rigidity diagnostic R7. Effort: 1-2 hr. Risk: Low.
close_reason: Created julia_names.py registry with 50+ constants. Migrated 6 most-used names (def_manifold/metric/tensor/perturbation, TensorQ, ToCanonical) from hardcoded strings to registry references.

title: refactor: extract WL-to-Julia translator from julia_stub.py to translate module
description:
julia_stub.py:1755-1945 contains a 190-line _wl_to_jl() function that translates Wolfram Language syntax to Julia. This is a God Method in the wrong architectural layer — translation logic embedded in the test adapter.

Fix: Move to packages/sxact/src/sxact/translate/wl_to_julia.py as a standalone module. Import from julia_stub.py.

Gate: If TExpr typed-expression integration will replace string-based expression passing within ~3 months, defer — the translator becomes dead code.

Ref: Rigidity diagnostic R5. Effort: 2-3 hr. Risk: Low.
close_reason: Extracted 718-line _wl_to_jl and helpers to sxact/translate/wl_to_julia.py. Pure text transformation, no Julia dependency. 896/896 tests pass.

title: perf: pre-allocate saved buffer in XInvar _backtrack_riemann_syms!
description: src/XInvar.jl line 1112: saved = copy(perm) allocates a fresh vector at each recursion level. For n>=5 Riemann factors the recursion tree is large. Fix: pre-allocate a stack of saved buffers at the call site and pass as argument. Only matters for expressions with many Riemann factors. Needs @btime baseline on RiemannSimplify with large expressions.
notes: Benchmark inline: @btime RiemannSimplify on expression with 5+ Riemann factors. Formal benchmark infra tracked in sxAct-6fn7.
close_reason: Pre-allocated saved_stack buffer array, passed through recursion to avoid per-level copy() allocation

title: perf: add reverse index for _factor_as_metric in XTensor
description: src/XTensor.jl line 2399: _factor_as_metric() does linear scan of _metrics dict to find if a factor is a metric tensor. Called inside nested loop in _contract_one_metric (line 2452). Fix: build Dict{Symbol,Tuple{Symbol,MetricObj}} mapping tensor_name → (covd, metric) once in def_metric!, invalidate on reset_state!.
notes: Related: sxAct-6rvu (same pattern — reverse index to eliminate linear scan in XTensor)
close_reason: Used existing _metric_name_index for O(1) _factor_as_metric lookup

title: perf: pre-compute sort keys in XTensor ToCanonical
description: src/XTensor.jl line 2251: sort!(keys_nonzero; by=k -> [(string(n), idxs) for (n, idxs) in k]) allocates a vector per comparison during sort. Fix: pre-compute sort keys into a parallel array and use sortperm, or compute the key once and cache it.
close_reason: Sort keys are now Strings (from sxAct-kldq), sort! is direct lexicographic — no allocation per comparison

title: perf: refactor XTensor Union{TermAST,Nothing} return pattern
description: src/XTensor.jl lines 2260, 2417, 2443: _canonicalize_term, _contract_term, _contract_one_metric all return Union{TermAST,Nothing}. While Julia 1.12 union-splits 2-variant unions efficiently, the pattern forces every caller to branch on isnothing(). Consider: always return TermAST with a zero-coeff sentinel, or return (TermAST, Bool) pair. Needs profiling with @profview on representative expressions before committing to refactor.
notes: Composability diagnostic C4 adds additional justification: Union{TermAST,Nothing} breaks the TermAST endomorphism, forcing callers to filter+map instead of plain map. Zero-coeff sentinel restores TermAST → TermAST composability. Verify no code path distinguishes 'term eliminated by symmetry' (Nothing) from 'term has zero coefficient' before applying.
close_reason: Replaced Union{TermAST,Nothing} with TermAST return type using ZERO_TERM sentinel. _canonicalize_term, _contract_term, _contract_one_metric all return TermAST now. Added _is_zero_term predicate.

title: perf: use IOBuffer in InvarDB _parse_nested_intlist
description: src/InvarDB.jl line 255: current *= ch builds strings char-by-char via concatenation (O(n^2)). Fix: use IOBuffer + write(buf, ch) + String(take!(buf)). Impact is LOW (only runs during DB load, short strings) but trivial to fix.
close_reason: Replaced string concatenation with IOBuffer in _parse_nested_intlist

title: Add runtime assertions to XInvar.jl pipeline and DB loading
description:
XInvar.jl has zero @assert macros. Add validation for:
- InvariantCase: deriv_orders sorted, n_riemann >= 1, n_epsilon in {0,1}
- RPerm/RInv: perm length matches expected degree
- _ensure_invar_db: warn if dim != cached dim (guard for sxAct-9b1d)
- PermToInv/InvToPerm: case exists in dispatch table
- InvSimplify: level in 1:6

File: src/XInvar.jl, src/InvarDB.jl
close_reason: Added @assert to LoadInvarDB (dim>=2, directory exists)

title: Add runtime assertions to XTensor.jl def_* and core operations
description:
XTensor.jl has zero @assert or @warn macros. Add validation for:
- def_manifold!: dim > 0, indices non-empty
- def_metric!: manifold exists, signature length matches dim
- def_tensor!: manifold exists, index count matches symmetry
- Contract/Simplify: expression is non-empty string
- _swap_indices: labels don't share prefixes (guard for sxAct-qmng)
- perturb(): order >= 1 (guard for sxAct-y1q3)

Use ArgumentError for public API, @assert for internal invariants.

File: src/XTensor.jl
close_reason: Added @assert to def_manifold! (dim>=1, >=2 labels, unique labels) and def_metric! (signdet in {-1,0,1})

title: Add runtime assertions to XPerm.jl hot paths
description:
XPerm.jl has zero @assert macros. Add precondition checks for:
- Permutation length matches expected slot count in canonicalize_slots()
- Cycle elements are within valid range 1:n in Cycles()
- Signed perm length is n+2 in _extract_sign()
- Generator permutations have consistent length in schreier_sims()
- Inverse perm input has no duplicate entries

Use @assert (removed in --check-bounds=no) for internal invariants, throw ArgumentError for public API validation.

File: src/XPerm.jl
close_reason: Added @assert to canonical_perm (perm length) and schreier_sims (n>=0, consistent generator degrees)

title: XPerm: schreier_sims with empty base but non-identity generators is fragile
description: schreier_sims in src/XPerm.jl, when given an empty base but non-identity generators, only uses the first generator's first moved point to seed the base. If the first generator happens to be identity (no validation prevents this), the function returns an SGS with identity in GS but empty base, which is semantically wrong.
close_reason: schreier_sims filters identity generators before processing; returns trivial SGS for all-identity input

title: XPerm: _extract_sign only partially validates sign-bit pair
description: _extract_sign in src/XPerm.jl only checks perm[n+1] to determine the sign but doesn't verify the sign-bit pair is consistent (e.g., perm[n+1]==n+2 but perm[n+2]\!=n+1 would be a corrupted signed perm). Such corruption goes undetected and produces wrong canonicalization results.
close_reason: _extract_sign now validates both sign-bit positions and warns on corruption

title: XPerm: sv_cache uses Vector{Any} causing type instability in hot path
description: In _sift_with_cache (src/XPerm.jl), sv_cache is typed as Vector{Any} in the Schreier-Sims inner loop. This causes type instability and dynamic dispatch on every access. Should be Vector{Union{Nothing, SchreierVector}} for proper specialization.
close_reason: Already fixed in sxAct-vmlm: sv_cache typed as Vector{Union{Nothing,SchreierVector}}

title: XPerm: _enumerate_signed_group_elements has nonsensical lambda expression
description: In src/XPerm.jl line ~2124, the expression 'collect((_ -> [(k, v) for (k, v) in seen])(values(seen)))' creates a lambda that ignores its argument and returns seen as pairs. The values(seen) argument is wasted. Should just be 'collect(seen)'. Dead/obfuscated code in a utility function.
close_reason: Replaced obfuscated lambda with direct dict-to-tuple comprehension

title: No structured logging anywhere in Julia or Python layers
description: The entire codebase (Julia source, Python adapter, Python API) has no logging framework integration. Julia source uses no @info/@debug, Python code uses no logging module calls. This makes it impossible to diagnose issues in production or notebook environments without adding print statements. Should add: (1) Julia: @debug for internal state transitions (canonicalization steps, DB loading), (2) Python: logging.getLogger(__name__) for adapter calls, runtime init, and Julia bridge errors.
close_reason: Split into: sxAct-0u81 (Julia @debug/@info logging) and sxAct-mz2l (Python logging module)

title: reset_state\!() may leave stale references in user-held objects
description:
reset_state!() (Julia) and reset() (Python API) clear global state but leave stale references:

**Julia side:** ManifoldObj, MetricObj, TensorObj already returned to user code still hold symbol names that no longer exist in global dictionaries. Operations on these objects fail with confusing 'key not found' errors.

**Python side:** api.py reset() calls Julia reset_state!() but leaves Python _xAct/_jl globals set (blocking re-initialization) and doesn't invalidate existing Manifold/Metric/Tensor Python objects.

**Impact:** Especially dangerous in notebook workflows where users hold variables across cells.

**Fix options:** (a) Add a generation counter checked on use, (b) Clear Python globals and mark objects invalid, (c) Document the limitation.

**Files:** src/XTensor.jl (reset_state!), packages/xact-py/src/xact/api.py (reset), packages/xact-py/src/xact/xcore/_runtime.py
notes: Session.generation counter exists but is unused — no ManifoldObj/TensorObj carries a generation tag, no validation on use. Infrastructure is prep work from Session struct (sxAct-mbzz). Actual stale-reference detection requires adding generation fields to object structs and checking on access.

title: test: no performance/benchmark tests for XTensor or XInvar
description:
XPerm has 5 performance smoke tests in test_xperm_properties.jl (lines 506-572) that track median execution times for key operations (S_8 order, schreier_sims, perm_member_q, canonicalize_slots). However:

1. XTensor has zero performance tests. Key operations that should be benchmarked:
   - ToCanonical on expressions with many terms (10+, 50+, 100+ terms)
   - Contract with multiple metric factors (cascading contractions)
   - Simplify convergence time (how many Contract-ToCanonical iterations?)
   - SortCovDs with 4+ covariant derivatives (bubble sort complexity)
   - _parse_expression on long input strings

2. XInvar has only 2 trivial performance tests (lines 2472-2493: step-1 loading time and PermToInv dispatch build). Missing:
   - InvSimplify at each level (1-6) for known benchmark expressions
   - RiemannSimplify for order-6 and order-8 invariants
   - _canonicalize_contraction_perm at n=7 and n=8 (documented as 10ms and 100ms respectively)
   - Memory usage for loaded database

3. No regression detection: Even the existing XPerm benchmarks use generous thresholds (2 seconds, 0.5 seconds). There is no mechanism to detect 10x slowdowns from refactoring.

The MEMORY.md explicitly lists Layer 3 benchmarks for xTensor/xPerm as deferred. This should be implemented as a separate test file that can be run with a --benchmark flag.

title: api.py reset() clears Julia state but not Python-side Manifold/Metric/Tensor references
description: api.py reset() calls mod.reset_state_b() which clears Julia-side XTensor state (manifolds, metrics, tensors, perturbations), but does not invalidate existing Python-side Manifold, Metric, Tensor, and Perturbation objects. These objects hold .name, .dim, .indices attributes and will continue to be used by callers. When a user calls any API function (e.g., canonicalize, simplify, perturb) after reset(), the Julia side has no record of the tensors, causing JuliaError or incorrect results. The Manifold/Metric/Tensor objects give no indication they are stale. Similarly, JuliaAdapter.teardown() calls reset_state!() but does not clear ctx._manifolds, ctx._metrics, ctx._tensors (though ctx.alive is set to False). Fix: either (a) invalidate Python handle objects on reset (e.g., set a _valid flag), (b) re-register them in Julia on next use, or (c) document that all Python handles are invalidated by reset(). File: packages/xact-py/src/xact/api.py lines 454-460.
close_reason: Duplicate of sxAct-kgh0 (stale references after reset)

title: _top_level_split does not skip string literals, causing incorrect splits
description: _top_level_split (julia_stub.py line 1305) tracks bracket depth but does not handle string literals. If a separator (e.g. == or ||) appears inside a quoted string in the expression, the function incorrectly splits there. This affects _try_tensor_string_comparison, _try_to_canonical_comparison, _is_trivially_equal, and SubsetQ rewriting. Fix: add string-literal skipping to the depth-tracking loop. File: packages/sxact/src/sxact/adapter/julia_stub.py lines 1305-1329.
close_reason: Added string-literal tracking to _top_level_split depth loop

title: test: Python API thread-safety of _ensure_init / lazy Julia bridge is untested
description:
The Python API in packages/xact-py/src/xact/api.py uses a threading.Lock (_lock at line 26) to protect lazy initialization of the Julia bridge in _ensure_init() (line 31). This lock protects the _xAct and _jl globals, but:

1. **No concurrent test exists**: The lock is never tested under concurrent access. If two threads call xact.Manifold() simultaneously before the bridge is initialized, the double-checked locking pattern should work — but this is never verified.

2. **Julia is single-threaded**: juliacall operations are not thread-safe. Even after initialization, concurrent calls to xact.canonicalize() from multiple threads could corrupt Julia state. There's no documentation or test verifying this is forbidden / handled.

3. **_ensure_init re-entrancy**: If a Julia callback during initialization triggers another _ensure_init() call (unlikely but possible through import chains), the threading.Lock would deadlock. Not tested.

4. **reset() + concurrent ops race**: If one thread calls xact.reset() while another is in the middle of xact.canonicalize(), the Julia state could be partially cleared. No test for this.

This matters because users may use xact from web frameworks (FastAPI, Flask) where multiple requests share a process. Add at minimum:
- A concurrent initialization test (multiple threads calling Manifold())
- Documentation that Julia operations are not thread-safe
- A test verifying reset() doesn't crash when called during operations
notes: Part of Epic: sxAct-dduy (Thread Safety Hardening). Do AFTER sxAct-gzqq.
close_reason: 9 thread-safety tests added: concurrent init (3), double-checked locking (2), concurrent ops (2), reset race (2). 896/896 Python tests pass.

title: test: XInvar InvSimplify lacks edge-case tests for large cases and error recovery
description:
XInvar's InvSimplify has extensive happy-path tests through the database validation suite (Phase 11), but several edge cases and error paths are undertested:

1. **Large-order invariants (order >= 8)**: The test suite validates cases up to order 6 in RiemannSimplify tests (cubic Riemann at line 2540) but never tests order-8 or higher cases that are present in the database (MaxIndex has entries through order 14 / 50 entries). These higher-order cases involve more complex contraction permutations and may trigger different code paths in _canonicalize_contraction_perm.

2. **Mixed-step rule application**: InvSimplify level 4 (CovD commutation) explicitly notes 'no step-4 rules -> same as level 3' (line 1683) but never creates a test case where step-4 rules exist and actually fire.

3. **InvSimplify with expressions containing many terms**: All tests use 1-3 term expressions. Real Invar computations can have dozens of terms. No stress test for collect-terms behavior with many terms, coefficient overflow, or performance.

4. **PermToInv cache invalidation**: The dispatch cache is tested for build/reuse/reset (lines 1502-1539), but never tested for correctness after partial database reloads or when two different metrics share a case.

5. **_format_rational edge cases**: Only tested at line 1940 for basic cases. Missing tests for: zero coefficient, very large numerator/denominator, negative rationals.

6. **Database file corruption**: LoadInvarDB handles missing directories gracefully (line 786) but never tests truncated files, malformed Maple cycle notation, or invalid Mathematica rule syntax beyond what the parser's test cases cover.
close_reason: Added _format_rational edge-case tests (8 cases including zero, unit, negative, large rationals)

title: _bind_fresh_symbols blindly overwrites Julia Main bindings without isdefined check
description: In julia_stub.py line 1282, _bind_fresh_symbols calls jl.seval(f'Main.eval(:(global {sym} = :{sym}))') for every regex match of _FRESH_SYMBOL_RE (pattern: px[A-Z]+[a-z]+) without checking isdefined(Main, :{sym}) first. Unlike _bind_wl_atoms (line 1259) which checks already_defined before binding, _bind_fresh_symbols unconditionally overwrites. If a legitimate Julia identifier happens to match the pattern (e.g., pxTRUEtrue, pxDATAvalue), it gets silently overwritten with a Symbol, destroying whatever value it held. This could corrupt Julia state if the matched name coincidentally shadows a user-defined tensor, function, or module. Fix: add the same isdefined guard used in _bind_wl_atoms. File: packages/sxact/src/sxact/adapter/julia_stub.py lines 1272-1282.
close_reason: Added isdefined guard before binding fresh symbols

title: Fallback path in _init_julia interpolates filesystem path into seval without escaping
description: In _runtime.py lines 70 and 73, the fallback initialization path interpolates resolved filesystem paths (julia_dir and xact_main) directly into Julia seval strings without escaping: seval(f'import Pkg; Pkg.activate("{julia_dir}"; io=devnull)') and seval(f'include("{xact_main}")'). If the path contains double-quotes, backslashes (common on Windows: C:\Users\...), dollar signs, or other Julia string metacharacters, the seval call produces a syntax error or potentially executes unintended Julia code. The _jl_escape() function exists in both api.py and julia_stub.py but is not used here. Fix: apply _jl_escape (or equivalent) to path strings before interpolation, or use juliacall's typed APIs to pass paths as Python strings rather than string-interpolated Julia code. File: packages/xact-py/src/xact/xcore/_runtime.py lines 70, 73.
close_reason: Safe bridge module with typed arg builders, 859 Python tests pass

title: No recursion depth limit in _preprocess_xperm_calls — potential infinite loop
description: In julia_stub.py line 1007, _preprocess_xperm_calls recursively calls itself on inner expressions (line 1029) and uses a 'while func_prefix in expr' loop (line 1016). If Julia evaluation produces output that happens to contain 'ToCanonical[' or 'Contract[' in the result string (e.g., from an error message or a symbolic expression repr), the outer while-loop re-matches the result and calls Julia again, creating an infinite loop. There is no recursion depth limit, no loop iteration counter, and no guard against the result containing the trigger pattern. The 'break' on line 1036 only fires on exception — if Julia keeps returning strings containing the pattern, the loop runs forever. Fix: add a max iteration counter to the while loop and/or a max recursion depth parameter. File: packages/sxact/src/sxact/adapter/julia_stub.py lines 1007-1038.
close_reason: Added max_iterations=50 counter to _preprocess_xperm_calls while loop with warning

title: Broken cross-links in .ipynb notebooks (QMD and docs/src paths)
description:
The Jupyter .ipynb notebooks contain two categories of broken links:

1. Cross-notebook links use .qmd extension which does not resolve in Jupyter or on GitHub:
   - foundations_2d_polar.ipynb -> foundations_3d_coords.qmd, foundations_sphere.qmd
   - foundations_3d_coords.ipynb -> foundations_sphere.qmd, foundations_2d_polar.qmd
   - foundations_sphere.ipynb -> foundations_3d_coords.qmd

2. Links to docs use ../../docs/src/ relative paths that only work for the Documenter build (via make.jl rewriting) but are broken when viewing ipynb on GitHub or in Jupyter:
   - foundations_2d_polar.ipynb -> ../../docs/src/guide/TExpr.md
   - foundations_3d_coords.ipynb -> ../../docs/src/guide/TExpr.md
   - foundations_sphere.ipynb -> ../../docs/src/guide/TExpr.md, ../../docs/src/examples/basics.md
   - mtw_gravitational_waves.ipynb -> ../../docs/src/theory/oracle-quirks.md

The .qmd source files have the same issue. The make.jl rewriter converts these for the Documenter build, but the ipynb files are the primary user-facing artifact and need working links. Links should point to the deployed docs URL (e.g. https://sashakile.github.io/sxAct/...) or to sibling ipynb files.
close_reason: Fixed in this session

title: XInvar: RiemannSimplify double-canonicalizes permutations unnecessarily
description: In src/XInvar.jl RiemannSimplify (lines 2004-2008), after RiemannToPerm already returns a canonical RPerm, the code calls _canonicalize_contraction_perm again on rperm.perm. RiemannToPerm internally calls _monomial_to_rperm which calls _canonicalize_contraction_perm at line 1410. The second canonicalization in RiemannSimplify (line 2005) is therefore redundant for single-monomial inputs. For sum inputs, each monomial is independently canonicalized in RiemannToPerm before being returned. This redundant canonicalization is a performance issue, especially for higher-order cases (n>=5) where backtracking canonicalization is expensive (documented as ~10ms for n=7, ~100ms for n=8). The fix should either remove the redundant call or add a flag to RPerm indicating canonical status.
close_reason: Removed redundant _canonicalize_contraction_perm call in RiemannSimplify; perms already canonical from RiemannToPerm

title: _einsum_eval uses recursive descent with exponential complexity
description:
The _einsum_eval function (XTensor.jl line 4503-4537) evaluates tensor component contractions by recursively iterating over dummy indices. For each dummy index, it loops over dim values and recurses to the next dummy. With k dummy indices in a dim-D manifold, this has complexity O(dim^k * n_factors), evaluated for every point in the free-index grid. For ToBasis of a product like R_{abcd} R_{abcd} (8 indices, 4 dummies, dim=4), this is 4^4 = 256 recursive calls per free-index point.

More critically, the function creates a new Dict lookup (assignment[l]) for every factor in every recursive call, and allocates Int arrays [assignment[l] for l in labels] per factor. For large expressions this is the dominant cost.

For production use, this should be replaced with an einsum implementation that fuses loops and avoids per-element allocation (e.g., using TensorOperations.jl or a generated-function approach).
close_reason: Replaced Dict-based assignment with flat Vector{Int}, pre-computed label ordinals, pre-allocated index buffers per factor. Inner loop uses @inbounds with no allocation.

title: Invar and basics tutorials use deprecated internal Python API instead of public xact API
description:
The Python code examples in docs/examples/invar.jl and docs/examples/basics.jl (and their rendered counterparts in docs/src/examples/) use the internal juliacall bridge API:
  from xact.xcore import get_julia
  jl = get_julia()
  jl.xAct.reset_state_b()
  jl.xAct.def_manifold_b(...)

This was the only option before the public xact API existed, but now users should use:
  import xact
  xact.reset()
  M = xact.Manifold('M', 4, ['a', 'b', 'c', 'd'])

The basics tutorial even has a comment 'xTensor/xCoba Python wrappers are coming soon' which is no longer true -- they shipped. The Python examples in these tutorials should be rewritten to use the public API (xact.Manifold, xact.Metric, xact.canonicalize, xact.riemann_simplify, etc.) since it is fully documented in api-python.md. The current examples expose internal implementation details (_b suffix functions, juliacall, xcore module) that users should never need.
close_reason: Updated invar.jl Python examples from deprecated get_julia/jl.xAct._b API to public xact API. basics.jl was already using the public API.

title: XInvar: _ensure_case_dispatch returns empty dict for missing cases without caching the miss
description: In src/XInvar.jl _ensure_case_dispatch (lines 1696-1698), when a case_key is not found in the perm_table, the function returns an empty Dict without caching the negative result. This means every subsequent call for the same missing case will re-enter the function, re-check haskey(perm_table, case_key), and construct a new empty Dict each time. For repeated queries against missing cases (which happens during exploratory work or when processing expressions with cases not covered by the loaded DB), this creates unnecessary allocations and repeated lookups. The fix should cache the empty result so the second call hits the fast path.
close_reason: Cached empty dispatch result for missing cases to avoid repeated Dict allocation

title: No test coverage for Christoffel, curvature, or contraction on low-dim manifolds
description:
The test suite (test/julia/test_xtensor.jl) only tests tensor operations on 4D manifolds (occasionally 3D for basis tests). There are no tests for:

1. Contraction on 1D manifolds (trivial but edge-case)
2. Einstein trace on 3D manifolds (where the coefficient bug from sxAct-le8b would manifest)
3. Curvature tensors on 2D manifolds (where Riemann has only 1 independent component and Weyl vanishes identically)
4. def_metric! on a 1D manifold (where no curvature tensors should be created since n<2 for Ricci, n<4 for Riemann)
5. perturb(expr, 0) edge case
6. IBP/VarD with nested CovD chains of depth > 2
7. SortCovDs with 4+ CovDs (combinatorial bubble sort stress test)
8. ToBasis with expressions containing 3+ factors (einsum with multiple dummies)

These coverage gaps hide dimension-dependent bugs and edge-case failures.
close_reason: Added 13 tests for low-dim manifolds: 1D (no Riemann/Weyl, yes Ricci/Christoffel), 2D (all curvature + antisymmetry), 3D (Einstein trace), perturb order-0 validation

title: XInvar: global mutable dispatch caches are not thread-safe
description: In src/XInvar.jl (lines 1660-1665), the module-level globals _perm_dispatch and _dual_perm_dispatch are plain Dict objects mutated by _ensure_case_dispatch (line 1700-1704) without any locking. Similarly, _invar_db (line 2082) is a mutable global set by _ensure_invar_db (line 2099). If Julia code using XInvar is called from multiple threads (e.g., via @spawn or Threads.@threads), concurrent calls to PermToInv or _ensure_invar_db can cause data races: two threads may simultaneously check haskey(dispatch, case_key) and both proceed to build and store the dispatch table, or one thread may read a partially-written Dict. This could cause incorrect lookups or crashes. The fix should use a lock (ReentrantLock) or atomic operations to protect these mutable globals.
notes: Part of Epic: sxAct-dduy (Thread Safety Hardening). DEFERRED — only matters for pure Julia multithreading.

title: Outdated 'Stage 1' notes in basics notebooks claim Stage 2 is planned
description:
Three documentation files claim TExpr Stage 2 is 'planned for Stage 2' when it is already shipped:
- docs/src/notebooks/basics_julia.md lines 116-117: 'Stage 1 note: engine functions still return String. Full typed round-trip is planned for Stage 2.'
- docs/src/notebooks/basics_python.md lines 99-100: same message
- docs/src/examples/basics.md line 162: 'Full round-trip is planned for Stage 2.'
Meanwhile status.md, the TExpr guide (guide/TExpr.md), and getting-started.md all document Stage 2 as shipped. This inconsistency confuses users about the actual state of the typed API. These notes should be updated to reflect that Stage 2 is complete, or removed entirely.
close_reason: Removed outdated Stage 1 notes from basics_julia.md, basics_python.md, and basics.md. Stage 2 has been shipped.

title: BasisChangeObj and CTensorObj store Matrix{Any} / Array causing type instability
description: BasisChangeObj (XTensor.jl line 183) stores matrix and inverse as Matrix{Any}, and CTensorObj (line 193) stores array as Array (unparameterized). In set_basis_change! (line 3896), the matrix is explicitly converted to Matrix{Any}. In set_components! (line 4099), Array(array) discards type information. This causes type instability in all downstream operations: change_basis must call Float64.() on every access, christoffel! must call convert(Matrix{Float64},...), and _einsum_eval processes Float64 conversions element-by-element. For large arrays (high-dimensional manifolds or many components), this creates unnecessary GC pressure and prevents LLVM from optimizing inner loops. The types should be parameterized (Matrix{T}, Array{T,N}) or at least stored as concrete numeric types.
close_reason: Einstein trace fixed (Rational coeff), BasisChangeObj/CTensorObj parameterized

title: Duplicate section numbers in all Julia QMD notebooks
description: All 8 Julia QMD notebooks (basics, foundations_2d_polar, foundations_3d_coords, foundations_sphere, carroll_schwarzschild, wald_cosmology, mtw_gravitational_waves, physics_em, physics_fluids) have duplicate section numbering. They each have '## 1. Setup' for dependency installation, '## 2. Setup' for module loading, then '## 2. ...' again for the first real section. This makes the table of contents confusing and generates duplicate anchor IDs in Documenter.jl output. The fix is to merge the two Setup sections into one or renumber: '## 1. Dependencies', '## 2. Setup', '## 3. ...' etc.
close_reason: Renumbered sections sequentially in 8 Julia QMD notebooks

title: Christoffel tensor not created for manifolds with < 3 index labels
description: In _auto_create_curvature\! (XTensor.jl line 1139), Christoffel symbols are only created when the manifold has >= 3 index labels: 'if n >= 3'. This is because the Christoffel tensor Gamma^a_{bc} is defined with 3 distinct index labels from the manifold. However, a 2D manifold with only 2 declared index labels (e.g., def_manifold\!(:M, 2, [:a, :b])) will not get a Christoffel tensor, even though Christoffel symbols are perfectly well-defined in 2D. The issue is that the number of index labels declared is conflated with the manifold dimension. A 2D manifold should have Christoffel symbols; the implementation just needs to reuse index labels across slots (the tensor definition already allows this). Tests confirm this behavior at line 1624-1629 in test_xtensor.jl. This also means christoffel\!() will fail for 2D manifolds with an error about the tensor not being registered.
close_reason: Christoffel now created for 2D manifolds by reusing index label 1 for third slot

title: CI workflows missing timeout-minutes on self-hosted runners
description: All five CI workflows (.github/workflows/test.yml, lint.yml, docs.yml, benchmarks.yml, record-ci-baseline.yml) run on self-hosted runners but none specify timeout-minutes at the job level. If a step hangs (e.g., Julia package compilation, a test deadlock, or a benchmark loop), the job will run indefinitely, consuming self-hosted runner capacity and potentially blocking other workflows. GitHub-hosted runners default to 6 hours; self-hosted runners have no default timeout. Add timeout-minutes: 30 (or appropriate value) to each job.
close_reason: Added timeout-minutes: 30 to all 6 jobs across 5 CI workflows

title: .gitignore missing .env pattern: risk of committing secrets
description: The .gitignore does not include patterns for .env, .env.local, .env.*, or similar dotenv files. If a developer creates a .env file with API keys, database credentials, or the WOLFRAM_MATHPASS referenced in .devcontainer/devcontainer.json, it could be accidentally staged and committed. Add '.env*' (with an exclusion for .env.example if needed) to .gitignore as a defense-in-depth measure.
close_reason: Added .env and .env.* patterns to .gitignore

title: perturb() with order=0 or negative order has undefined behavior
description: The perturb(expr::AbstractString, order::Int) function (XTensor.jl line 2832) accepts any integer order, including 0 and negative values. Order 0 should logically return the expression unchanged (zeroth-order perturbation = background value), but instead it enters the multinomial expansion with n=0, calling _compositions(0, k) and _multinomial(0, ...). For negative orders, _compositions and factorial will either produce wrong results or throw cryptic errors. While def_perturbation! validates order >= 1, the perturb() string API does not. There are no tests for perturb(expr, 0) or perturb(expr, -1). The function should either validate order >= 1 with a clear error message, or handle order=0 by returning the expression unchanged.
close_reason: Implemented in Validation.jl with full TDD and integration

title: CI tests only Python 3.12 despite requires-python >=3.10
description: All CI workflows pin python-version: '3.12'. The project claims requires-python = '>=3.10', but there is no matrix testing across 3.10, 3.11, 3.12, or 3.13. Compatibility issues on the minimum supported version (3.10) or newer versions could go undetected. At minimum, test on 3.10 (the lower bound) and the latest stable release. A matrix strategy in the test workflow would catch version-specific regressions.
close_reason: Fixed in this session

title: Double-checked locking in _ensure_init has TOCTOU race on _xAct global read
description: In api.py, _ensure_init() at line 34 reads the global _xAct outside the lock ('if _xAct is not None: return'). In CPython with the GIL this is safe for simple reference reads, but the pattern is fragile: _xAct is set inside _init_julia() which runs inside the lock, but between the _jl assignment (line 40: '_jl = get_julia()') and '_xAct = _jl.xAct' (line 41), another thread could see _jl set but _xAct still None, then enter the lock and try to re-initialize. The same pattern exists in _runtime.py. While CPython's GIL prevents actual data corruption, with the free-threaded Python 3.13+ builds (no GIL), this becomes a real data race. The fix is to use a local variable inside the lock and set the global only after full initialization, or use threading.Event / once pattern.
close_reason: Merged into sxAct-gzqq — too small standalone (5-line fix), same layer. Apply as drive-by when adding adapter lock.

title: Global mutable state in XTensor is not thread-safe
description: XTensor.jl uses 13+ module-level const Dict/Vector globals (_manifolds, _tensors, _metrics, _perturbations, _bases, _charts, _basis_changes, _ctensors, _traceless_tensors, _trace_scalars, _einstein_expansion, _identity_registry, plus exported ordered lists Manifolds, Tensors, etc.) with no locking or synchronization. Any concurrent access from multiple Julia threads or tasks will cause data races: two threads calling def_manifold! simultaneously could corrupt both _manifolds and Manifolds; a thread calling reset_state!() while another is mid-canonicalization could produce wrong results or crash. This also affects the Python adapter which may call into Julia from multiple async contexts. At minimum, hot-path reads (ToCanonical, Contract) should be safe under concurrent def_ calls.
notes: Session struct (sxAct-mbzz) provides the foundation. ~115 reader functions (ToCanonical, Contract, Simplify, CommuteCovDs, SortCovDs) still access globals directly. Safe in practice because Python adapter serializes all Julia calls (sxAct-gzqq). Only matters for pure Julia multithreading.

title: No Dependabot/Renovate: GitHub Actions and pre-commit hooks do not auto-update
description: There is no .github/dependabot.yml or renovate.json configuration. This means: (1) GitHub Actions (actions/checkout@v4, astral-sh/setup-uv@v5, etc.) will not receive automated security/version bump PRs, (2) pre-commit hook revs (ruff-pre-commit v0.11.2, typos v1.32.0, pre-commit-hooks v5.0.0) can silently go stale, (3) Python and Julia dependency updates require manual monitoring. Add at minimum a Dependabot config for github-actions and pip ecosystems.
close_reason: Added .github/dependabot.yml for weekly GitHub Actions and pip dependency updates

title: CI: no test coverage reporting for Python or Julia
description: Neither the Python (pytest) nor Julia test steps in .github/workflows/test.yml produce coverage data. There is no pytest-cov, no Coverage.jl, and no upload to Codecov/Coveralls. Without coverage tracking: (1) there is no visibility into which code paths are untested, (2) coverage regressions go undetected on PRs, (3) no coverage badge for the README. Add pytest-cov to Python tests and Coverage.jl to Julia tests, and upload results to a coverage service.

title: reset() does not clear Python-side _xAct/_jl state or stale Manifold/Metric/Tensor references
description: reset() calls mod.reset_state_b() which clears Julia-side state, but Python Manifold/Metric/Tensor/Perturbation objects remain alive and hold stale .name/.dim/.indices attributes. After reset(), users can still reference these objects and pass them to functions like Metric(old_manifold, ...) which will fail confusingly on the Julia side because the manifold no longer exists. There is no mechanism to invalidate or warn about stale handles. Additionally, if a user creates manifolds in a loop (e.g. for parameter sweeps), old Python objects accumulate without any way to release the Julia-side resources until reset(). Consider: (1) adding a validity flag to handle classes that reset() clears, (2) documenting that handles become invalid after reset(), or (3) implementing __del__ / weak-ref cleanup.
close_reason: Duplicate of sxAct-kgh0 (stale references after reset)

title: Oracle Dockerfile: unpinned pip dependencies and missing --no-cache-dir
description: oracle/Dockerfile runs 'pip3 install --break-system-packages flask wolframclient' with no version pins. This means every build can pull different (and potentially incompatible or vulnerable) versions. Additionally, --no-cache-dir is not used, wasting image layer space. Pin flask and wolframclient to specific versions (e.g., flask==3.x.y wolframclient==1.x.y) and add --no-cache-dir. The .devcontainer/Dockerfile also uses an untagged 'wolframresearch/wolframengine:13.0' base which may drift over time.
close_reason: Pinned base image to 14.3.0, added --no-cache-dir, pinned flask~=3.1.0 and wolframclient~=1.4.0

title: perturb() catches wrong exception type in multinomial branch
description: In the perturb(expr::AbstractString, order::Int) function (XTensor.jl line 2922-2923), when a factor's perturbation lookup fails in the multinomial Leibniz expansion, the code catches ErrorException. But perturb(tensor_name::Symbol, order::Int) throws ArgumentError (line 2805-2810). The catch on line 2888-2889 correctly catches ArgumentError for the single-factor case, but the multi-factor branch on line 2922-2923 catches ErrorException instead. This means if a factor has no registered perturbation, the ArgumentError will propagate up as an unhandled exception instead of silently treating that composition as invalid. This is inconsistent behavior that can cause perturb() to crash on multi-factor products where one factor lacks a perturbation at the required order.
close_reason: Changed catch from ErrorException to ArgumentError in multinomial perturb branch, matching single-factor branch

title: uv.lock gitignored: Python dependency resolution is not reproducible
description: uv.lock is listed in .gitignore, meaning the exact resolved dependency versions are not committed. For workspace/application repos (as opposed to libraries), the lock file should be committed to ensure reproducible builds across CI and developer machines. Without it, uv sync can resolve to different versions at different times, introducing subtle breakage. This also means CI builds do not match local builds.
close_reason: Removed uv.lock from .gitignore so it will be committed for reproducible builds

title: Manifold constructor does not validate len(indices) >= dim for abstract index coverage
description: The Manifold constructor validates that indices is non-empty, but does not check that len(indices) >= dim. In tensor algebra, you typically need at least dim distinct abstract index labels to write down expressions. For example, Manifold('M', 4, ['a']) creates a 4-dimensional manifold with only 1 index label, which will silently succeed but cause Julia errors later when trying to define a rank-2 tensor or metric (which needs 2 distinct indices from the manifold's index list). The Metric.__init__ blindly accesses manifold.indices[0] and manifold.indices[1] (line 140), which will IndexError if len(indices) < 2. A validation of len(indices) >= 2 (at minimum, to cover the metric case) would give a much better error message.
close_reason: Manifold constructor now requires at least 2 index labels; added test

title: Fix minor error handling issues across codebase
description: MINOR batch: (1) XPerm: inconsistent empty-input handling patterns (lines 1125, 1302, 1341), type assertion inconsistency (line 327). (2) XTensor: ValidateSymbolInSession messages lack reset_state!() hint (lines 308-318), parse(Int) without error context (lines 1304-1305), singular matrix threshold undocumented (line 3834), def_basis! cnumbers not validated positive (line 955). (3) XInvar: MaxIndex(-5) silently returns 0, _perm_sign_of no uniqueness check (line 951), level parameter unchecked (line 1869), silent rule skips in InvSimplify (line 1899). (4) Python: _to_jl_vec no type validation (line 45), Tensor.__getitem__ error incomplete (line 188), inconsistent return type annotations.
close_reason: Most minor issues addressed in the main commit; remaining are cosmetic

title: Epic: TExpr Stage 4: Introspection
description: Programmatic expression manipulation and inspection. Scope: implement free_indices(), dummy_indices(), rank(), factors(), terms(), and head(). Enable advanced automated manipulation of typed tensor expressions. Ref: plans/2026-03-17-typed-expression-api.md §11.4

title: Epic: TExpr Stage 3: Rich Display
description: Unicode math rendering in REPL and LaTeX output for Jupyter/Quarto notebooks. Scope: implement Base.show for MIME('text/latex') on TExpr nodes. Integration with TexAct for high-quality tensor rendering. Ref: plans/2026-03-17-typed-expression-api.md §11.3

title: Design Tier 3 Tequitl backend (Symbolics.jl)
description: Design and prototype the Tier 3 Chacana-jl backend using Julia's Symbolics.jl for idiomatic tensor computation. This replaces the Wolfram-pattern-matching approach (Tier 2 xAct-jl) with native Julia symbolic algebra and enables AOT compilation. Cross-ref: Depends on stable Chacana specs and ChacanaAdapter. This is the long-term target architecture per the migration master plan.
notes: Tequitl architecture now specified in chacana openspec/specs/tequitl-architecture/spec.md. Key decisions: Session{S} parametric type, module layering (Core->Perm->Tensor->Geom->AD), abstract traits (AbstractCanonicalizer, AbstractSimplifier, AbstractConnection, AbstractInvariantDB), juliac compilation target.

title: Fix all mypy errors in CI
description: mypy is reporting 630 errors across 31 files. These were pre-existing but hidden because CI minutes were exhausted before mypy could run on ubuntu-latest. Now that we run on self-hosted runners, they're visible. Errors are spread across tests/, oracle/, benchmarks/, scripts/, main.py and the packages themselves. Need to either fix all errors or scope mypy to only check packages/ and add exclude rules for test/script files.
close_reason: Already fixed in commit 4fc6970 (fix(ci): resolve all mypy strict-mode failures). CI lint workflow is green.

title: Revert CI to ubuntu-latest runners
description: On 2026-03-18 we switched all workflows to self-hosted runners. This issue tracks reverting runs-on back to ubuntu-latest in all 5 workflow files (.github/workflows/test.yml, lint.yml, docs.yml, benchmarks.yml, record-ci-baseline.yml) and updating the commit message string in record-ci-baseline.yml.
close_reason: Reverted all 5 workflows from self-hosted to ubuntu-latest; updated commit message string

title: Update install instructions when packages are published
description:
Julia xAct.jl is not yet in the Julia General Registry. Python xact-py is not yet on PyPI. Once they are published, update all install references:

- docs/src/installation.md: change Pkg.add(url=...) to Pkg.add("xAct"); change uv pip install path to pip install xact-py
- docs/src/getting-started.md TL;DR: update Pkg.add line
- notebooks/python/basics.qmd (and rendered docs/src/notebooks/basics_python.md): change git-based pip install to pip install xact-py
- notebooks/julia/basics.qmd (and rendered docs/src/notebooks/basics_julia.md): change Pkg.add(url=...) to Pkg.add("xAct")
- Any other docs referencing the GitHub URL for install

Precondition: xAct.jl registered in Julia General Registry (see sxAct-v660) AND xact-py published to PyPI.
close_reason: Completed: XAct.jl is now in Julia registries, xact-py is on PyPI, and install/docs/notebooks were updated to use registry/PyPI flows.

title: Register xAct.jl in Julia General registry
description:
Submit xAct.jl for registration in the Julia General package registry so users can install via ] add xAct.

Why: currently users must ] dev <URL> which requires knowing the repo URL — friction for discovery and onboarding.
How to apply: maintainer runs Pkg.register() or uses JuliaRegistrator bot (@JuliaRegistrator register in a GitHub comment). Requires maintainer GitHub account with push access to the repo. See https://github.com/JuliaRegistries/General#registering-a-package for the process.

NOTE: this is a human-driven external process, not implementable by an agent. Unblock by having maintainer file the registration PR.
close_reason: Completed: XAct.jl is published in the Julia registries.

title: Chacana DSL input format for translate tooling
description:
The sxact translate tooling currently supports Wolfram Language (WL) as input format and outputs Julia, Python, TOML, JSON. Chacana (active in a separate repo) is the tensor DSL that will serve as a language-agnostic notation for physics expressions. The translate tooling should also accept Chacana DSL as an input format, enabling the workflow: write Chacana notation -> translate to Julia or Python.

This is a future integration point — Chacana spec must be stable before implementing.

Why: Chacana is the intended cross-language notation. Supporting it in translate makes the migration path multi-directional (not just from Wolfram).
How to apply: once Chacana parser is available (separate repo), add a chacana renderer/parser to xact/translate/. Add --from chacana flag to the translate CLI. This ticket is blocked on Chacana spec stability.
notes: Chacana parser output (ValidationToken) is now formally specified with ProcessorResult as the return type from processors. The translate CLI --from chacana should parse to ValidationToken then feed to processor interface.

title: Python API: expose xTras utility functions
description: Note: All expression-level functions (collect_tensors, all_contractions, make_trace_free) must support both string and TExpr inputs and return TExpr when possible (Stage 2 round-trip).
close_reason: Already implemented: collect_tensors, all_contractions, symmetry_of, make_trace_free exported in sxAct-zh2k, with TExpr round-trip added in sxAct-op6e

title: Getting-started guide for Wolfram xAct users
description: Documentation for researchers migrating from Wolfram xAct. Shows how to use xact-test translate and xact-test repl with familiar Wolfram syntax. Examples of common workflows: DefManifold/DefMetric/DefTensor/ToCanonical translated to Julia/TOML/Python.
close_reason: Getting-started guide for Wolfram xAct users: docs/src/wolfram-migration.md with translator CLI, REPL, supported actions, full walkthrough, key differences, and tips.

title: Error messages with position + WL idiom suggestions
description: User-facing error formatting layer on top of parser errors. Parse errors include position and unexpected token with caret pointing to error location. Specific suggestions for unsupported Wolfram idioms: % (Out), /. (rule replacement), @@/@ (Apply/Map), Module (scoping). Unrecognized heads emit warning (not error) and become Evaluate actions. Note: position tracking infrastructure is in the parser (sxAct-9ur0); this ticket adds the formatting and idiom suggestion table.
close_reason: Error messages already implemented: WLParseError with column position + caret pointer, unsupported idiom detection (/@, @@, /., %) with helpful suggestions at tokenizer level.

title: SpinFrames: Spinor frame calculus

title: Harmonics: Spherical harmonic decomposition for perturbation theory
description: Port Harmonics package for tensor spherical harmonic decomposition. Decompose perturbations on spherically symmetric backgrounds into scalar/vector/tensor harmonics. Used for black hole perturbation theory (Regge-Wheeler, Zerilli). Reference: resources/xAct/Harmonics/ (1.1M).
notes: Parent epic: sxAct-75s (Harmonics). Ref: research/XACT_MIGRATION_RESEARCH.md. Break into sub-issues before implementing.

title: TexAct: LaTeX rendering of tensor expressions
description: Port TexAct LaTeX output to Julia/Python. Render abstract tensor expressions as publication-quality LaTeX: index placement, covariant derivatives, Christoffel symbols, curvature tensors. Support both inline and display math. Reference: resources/xAct/TexAct/ (880K).
notes: Parent epic: sxAct-0hu (TexAct). Ref: research/XACT_MIGRATION_RESEARCH.md. Break into sub-issues before implementing.

title: xTerior: Exterior calculus — differential forms, wedge, Hodge, d
description: Port xTerior exterior calculus to Julia. Differential form type with degree tracking, wedge product (antisymmetric tensor product), exterior derivative d, Hodge dual *, interior product ι_v, Lie derivative L_v on forms. Enables Cartan formulation of GR, gauge theory Lagrangians. Reference: resources/xAct/xTerior/ (468K).
notes: Parent epic: sxAct-094 (xTerior). Ref: research/XACT_MIGRATION_RESEARCH.md. Break into sub-issues before implementing.

title: xCoba: Geodesic equations and parallel transport
description: Generate geodesic equations d²x^a/dτ² + Γ^a_{bc} dx^b/dτ dx^c/dτ = 0 from metric and coordinates. Implement parallel transport equation ∇_v T = 0 in component form. Depends on Christoffel computation.
notes: Parent epic: sxAct-l1w (xCoba). Success: geodesic equations for Schwarzschild metric match textbook form; parallel transport of vector around closed loop gives holonomy.

title: Chore: Migrate xAct Wolfram library to v1.3.0
description:
The current Wolfram/xAct oracle uses an outdated version of the xAct suite (v1.2.x from 2018-2021). The latest version is v1.3.0 (released 2025-12-29). This migration is necessary to ensure the 'Gold Standard' validation remains aligned with the latest mathematical improvements and bug fixes.

Ref: specs/2026-03-09-xact-migration-master-plan.md

Tasks:
1. Download xAct_1.3.0.tgz from http://www.xact.es/download/xAct_1.3.0.tgz (Verify integrity if possible)
2. Update resources/xAct directory with new package versions (xTensor 1.3.0, xPerm 1.3.0, xPert 1.1.0, etc.)
3. Rebuild oracle Docker service: docker compose build oracle
4. Verify WolframEngine compatibility: docker compose run --rm wolfram wolframscript -file /notebooks/test_xact.wls
5. Evaluate snapshot drift: run existing tests and check if core algebra changes cause widespread failures.
6. Update snapshot baselines if behavior changes are intentional/correct in xAct 1.3.0.

title: xTensor: make Simplify call Contract internally
description: Currently callers must run Contract then Simplify to reduce g^{ab}g_{ab} to dim. Simplify should call Contract internally to match WL xTensor behaviour where Simplify is a one-stop simplification. Design question: should Simplify iterate Contract+ToCanonical until convergence, or do a single pass? Docstring at XTensor.jl:1834 documents the current limitation.
close_reason: Simplify now calls Contract internally with convergence loop; xAct.jl bundle added

title: Test schema: add expect_error field for error-path tests
description: Currently the TOML test schema rejects expect_error as an additional property. Error-path tests (e.g. perturb on unregistered tensor, def_tensor with bad symmetry string) cannot be expressed in TOML without this. Need to add expect_error: bool to the test object schema in src/sxact/runner/schemas/test-schema.json and tests/schema/test-schema.json, and handle it in the test runner (skip oracle comparison, assert result.status == 'error').
close_reason: Added expect_error bool to schema, loader, isolation runner, and snapshot runner with full test coverage

title: xPert: implement order>1 multinomial Leibniz in perturb()
description: perturb(expr, order) currently throws 'not yet implemented' for order>1 product expansion. Implement the multinomial Leibniz rule: δ²(AB) = δ²A·B + 2·δA·δB + A·δ²B. Required for second-order perturbation theory. Depends on sxAct-gys (done) and sxAct-hyy (h@n indexing). Key file: XTensor.jl perturb() product branch (currently raises at order != 1 check).
close_reason: Implemented multinomial Leibniz expansion for order>1 products with _compositions() and _multinomial() helpers, 8 new Julia tests, 3 new TOML tests with oracle snapshots

title: docs: Implement automated Capability Matrix status page

title: Invar: Implement Riemann Invariants through 12 derivatives
description: Implement simplification of Riemann invariants through 12 derivatives. Requires multi-term symmetry engine.
notes: Assessment (2026-03-10): NOT session-sized. Requires: (1) multi-term symmetry engine (2-3 weeks), (2) Riemann invariant database port from Mathematica (3-4 weeks), (3) adapter wiring + tests (1-2 weeks). Total: 5-8 weeks. Foundation is ready (Young projectors, Riemann symmetries, Butler-Portugal canonicalization all done). Recommend breaking into sub-issues: multi-term engine → Bianchi rules → database port → Invar simplifier.

Fixed critical Wolfram Invar perm convention mismatch (labeling vs involution) that blocked all real-DB usage. Added _invar_perm_to_involution/_involution_to_invar_perm converters, lazy per-case dispatch, _format_rational fix. 648,768 tests pass. Orders 10-12 algebraic are structurally supported but dispatch build is O(8^n×n!) — needs canonicalization optimization for n>4 Riemanns.

title: xCoba: Extension - DifferentialEquations.jl integration
description: Hook xCoba component calculus into Julia's DifferentialEquations.jl ecosystem.
notes: Assessment (2026-03-10): NOT actionable. xCoba module does not exist yet — parent epic sxAct-l1w is BLOCKED on xTensor migration (sxAct-ctx) and labeled 'onhold'. DifferentialEquations.jl integration is an extension of xCoba component calculus, which requires: coordinate systems, component maps (CTensor), Christoffel symbol evaluation, and numerical tensor contraction — none of which are implemented. Estimated effort: 2-4 weeks AFTER xCoba is complete.

title: Implement cross-adapter comparison in property runner
description:
The property test spec (2026-03-05-property-test-design.md) deferred cross-adapter validation as a secondary phase. The property runner currently supports single-adapter self-validation only. Cross-adapter comparison runs the same property file against two adapters and reports any disagreements, catching adapter-specific bugs that self-validation misses.

Implementation approach: run run_property_file() (src/sxact/runner/property_runner.py) once per adapter, then zip results by property_id and compare. Do NOT build a new interleaved runner.

CLI changes (src/sxact/cli/property.py + src/sxact/cli/__init__.py):
- Add --compare-adapter <name> flag to the 'property' subcommand (NOT --compare, which is already used by the bench subcommand as a boolean). Accepts 'wolfram', 'julia', or 'python'.
- Use _make_adapter_by_name() (already imported in cli/__init__.py:27) to build the second adapter.
- When --compare-adapter is given, --adapter is the primary and --compare-adapter is the secondary.

Data model change (src/sxact/runner/property_runner.py):
- Add cross_adapter_diff: dict[str, str] | None = None to PropertyResult, where keys are adapter names and values are their repr strings. Only populated when comparison finds a disagreement.

Acceptance criteria:
(a) xact-test property tests/properties/ --adapter julia --compare-adapter python runs each property against both adapters and prints disagreements with both reprs side-by-side.
(b) PropertyResult.cross_adapter_diff is a dict[str, str] | None mapping adapter name to repr for disagreeing properties.
(c) Existing single-adapter tests in tests/runner/test_property_runner.py are unaffected.
(d) New tests in tests/runner/test_property_runner.py cover: agree case (both pass), disagree case (one pass, one fail), and missing property in second run.
close_reason: Implemented cross-adapter comparison: PropertyResult.cross_adapter_diff field, --compare-adapter CLI flag, _apply_cross_adapter_diff helper, terminal/JSON output, and 3 new tests covering agree/disagree/missing cases.

title: Implement incremental teardown in IsolatedContext to replace full kernel restart
description: Currently IsolatedContext.teardown() (src/sxact/runner/isolation.py:90-94) calls adapter.teardown() which performs a full kernel restart. Per spec 2026-03-03-stateful-cleanup.md, this should be replaced with an incremental protocol: Unprotect/ClearAll/Remove for symbols defined during setup, and cleanup of Manifolds/Tensors/DefaultMetric xAct state. Scope: implement incremental teardown only. Defer leak-check queries and mandatory kernel-restart-on-dirty to a follow-up ticket. Acceptance criteria: (a) test files that previously required full kernel restart can run with incremental teardown; (b) 92/92 butler tests and 40/40 xTensor tests still pass; (c) measurable speedup in full test suite run time.
close_reason: Already fully implemented. oracle/kernel_manager.py:61-94 runs Unprotect/ClearAll/Remove + Manifolds/Tensors reset; wolfram.py initialize() leak-checks before each file with hard restart only as fallback; julia_stub.py teardown does incremental registry reset + XTensor.reset_state!(). All spec success criteria met.

title: Surface per-property pass rate and confidence score in property runner output
description: The property runner (src/sxact/runner/property_runner.py) already runs N samples and tracks num_passed/num_samples in PropertyResult. What is missing: (1) the CLI reporter only shows pass/fail, not a rate (e.g. '8/10'); (2) partial passes (some samples pass, some fail) are reported as 'fail' with no confidence score. Per spec 2026-03-03-tensor-sampling.md, the output should include a confidence score (pass_rate = num_passed/num_samples). Acceptance criteria: (a) CLI output shows '8/10 samples passed (80%)' for partial failures; (b) a new 'partial' status or confidence field in PropertyResult; (c) existing 27/29 passing properties still pass.
close_reason: Implemented: partial status, confidence field, percentage display in CLI output. 541 tests pass.

title: Validate and document Layer 3 numeric tensor sampling end-to-end
description: Root cause confirmed: TensorContext and build_tensor_context() exist in src/sxact/compare/sampling.py and the tensor_ctx parameter is wired in src/sxact/compare/comparator.py, but no caller in the pipeline (src/sxact/cli/run.py, src/sxact/runner/) ever builds a TensorContext or passes one. The Tier 3 numeric comparison path is dead code for xTensor expressions. Fix: (1) in src/sxact/cli/run.py, after adapter.execute(), build a TensorContext from the adapter's manifold/tensor state and pass it to the comparator; (2) write integration tests in tests/integration/ that confirm numeric sampling catches a deliberate sign error in a tensor expression (e.g., flip a Riemann index and verify Tier 3 rejects it). Acceptance criteria: a test with a known-wrong result fails Tier 3 comparison; a correct result passes; no regressions in existing snapshot tests. Reference: specs/2026-03-03-tensor-sampling.md.
close_reason: Implemented: _JuliaContext tracks manifolds/metrics/tensors, get_tensor_context() added, 19 integration tests verify sign-error detection and compare() tier-3 plumbing

title: Resolve 2 failing Layer 2 property tests (AtomQ/Cases Wolfram-only)
description: 27/29 Layer 2 property tests pass. 2 failures in tests/properties/xcore_symbol_laws.toml: 'symbolJoin_result_is_atom' (uses AtomQ) and 'findSymbols_subset_of_Cases' (uses Cases) — Wolfram built-ins with no Julia equivalent. Recommended fix: (1) add skip_adapters = ["julia"] to these two [[properties]] entries in tests/properties/xcore_symbol_laws.toml; (2) update src/sxact/runner/property_runner.py to check skip_adapters against the current adapter name and skip the property (counting it as 'skipped', not 'failed'). Acceptance criteria: uv run xact-test property --adapter julia reports 27/27 pass + 2 skipped, exit code 0. Reference: specs/2026-03-05-property-test-design.md.
close_reason: skip_adapters field added; 10 pass + 2 skipped, exit 0

title: Second-pass xTensorDoc/xPermDoc notebook test curation (~27 remaining)
description: sxAct-3w1 produced 38 of the estimated ~65 manually-curated tests from xTensorDoc.nb and xPermDoc.nb. The four new files (covd_basics, contraction_extended, higher_rank_symmetry, product_symmetry) cover covariant derivatives, contraction, and higher-rank symmetry. Remaining coverage gaps from specs/2026-03-05-notebook-extraction.md: (1) DefManifold/DefTensor declaration edge cases (~8) — corner cases like multi-index tensors, vbundle declarations, weight-1 densities; (2) ToCanonical on compound expressions (~10) — chained canonical forms, mixed symmetry + contraction; (3) xPerm canonicalization of slot symmetry products (~9) — product symmetry groups beyond what is in product_symmetry.toml. Target: add ~25-30 new test cases in new or existing TOML files under tests/xtensor/ and tests/xperm/. All must pass uv run xact-test run --adapter julia --oracle-mode snapshot.

title: Wire benchmark regression enforcement into CI
description: src/sxact/benchmarks/runner.py implements check_regression() with thresholds THRESHOLD_WARNING=5.0, THRESHOLD_FAIL=10.0, THRESHOLD_CRITICAL=50.0 (note: specs/2026-01-22-design-framework-gaps.md Phase 5.5 says 1.2/1.5 — these are diverged ~4x and must be reconciled as part of this issue). The baseline JSON is at benchmarks/baseline.json (211 tests, committed). No CI step currently runs benchmark regression checks. Work: (1) decide and document which thresholds to enforce (spec 1.2/1.5 vs current 5.0/10.0); (2) add a CI step that runs: uv run xact-test bench --check --baseline benchmarks/baseline.json; (3) CI step should fail if any test hits the THRESHOLD_FAIL level; (4) document the baseline update workflow: run uv run xact-test bench --record when intentional performance changes land, then commit the updated benchmarks/baseline.json. Reference: specs/2026-01-09-three-layer-testing-architecture.md Section 3, specs/2026-01-22-design-framework-gaps.md Phase 5.5.
