title: Pass metric_compatibility and second_bianchi_identity property tests
description:
Two properties in tests/properties/riemann_symmetries.toml (properties 9-10 per spec §Q5) currently fail because XTensor.jl does not evaluate covariant derivative expressions symbolically:

- metric_compatibility (property 9 in TOML, 10 in spec): PCD[-pc][Pg[-pa,-pb]] should reduce to 0 (Levi-Civita condition ∇_c g_{ab} = 0, Wald 3.1.14)
- second_bianchi_identity (property 10 in TOML, 9 in spec): ∇_e R_{abcd} + ∇_c R_{abde} + ∇_d R_{abec} should reduce to 0 (Wald 3.2.12)

ROOT CAUSE (two layers):
1. Parser: _parse_monomial (XTensor.jl:637) only handles Name[indices] flat syntax. The CovD application syntax CD[-c][expr] is structurally different — a second [...] follows the index list. The parser currently fails or silently drops the operand.
2. Semantics: Two reduction rules are needed:
   - Metric compatibility is axiomatic: CD[-c][g[-a,-b]] → 0 for any Levi-Civita connection CD
   - Differential Bianchi: the cyclic sum of ∇Riemann → 0, a consequence of the torsion-free condition (not the Jacobi identity). The existing _bianchi_reduce! (XTensor.jl:747) handles the algebraic Bianchi identity; an analogous CovD-Bianchi rule is needed.

IMPLEMENTATION PATH (choose one):
Option A — Preprocessing in julia_stub.py: extend _try_numerical_tolerance_via_canonical to recognise CovD[-c][g[-a,-b]] → 0 and the CovD-Bianchi pattern before passing to ToCanonical. Avoids parser changes.
Option B — Extend ToCanonical parser: add CovD application as a new term type in _parse_expression, then apply reduction rules during canonicalization.

Prior art: sxAct-3to implemented _bianchi_reduce! and the CovD framework for gw_memory_3p5pn.toml — review that work first.

VERIFICATION:
  uv run xact-test property tests/properties/riemann_symmetries.toml --adapter julia
Expected: 10 passed, 0 failed (currently 8 passed, 2 failed).

Reference: specs/2026-03-05-property-test-design.md §Q5; specs/2026-03-06-xperm-xtensor-design.md §Tier 2 deferred.
close_reason: Implemented _reduce_metric_compatibility and _reduce_second_bianchi preprocessing in XTensor.jl; 10/10 property tests pass

title: Implement DeleteRedundantGenerators in XPerm.jl
description: DeleteRedundantGenerators[SGS] removes generators from a StrongGenSet that are redundant (can be expressed as products of others). Needed for deleteredundantgenerators_03 in example_3 and eval_05 which calls it.
close_reason: Closed

title: Implement PermWord in XPerm.jl
description: PermWord[perm, SGS] decomposes a permutation as a word in the strong generating set (Sims strip/factorization algorithm). Returns a list of coset representatives, e.g. {Cycles[], "g2", Cycles[{2,5,3,4},{6,7}], Cycles[{1,4,7,6,2,5,3}]}. Directly unblocks: permword_15 (example_2), eval_03 (example_6). Note: permmemberq_22/23/24 in example_2 depend on an undefined newSGS variable (WL notebook state never captured in TOML) and are NOT unblocked by this ticket.
close_reason: Closed

title: Manual xTensor/xPerm TOML test curation from documentation notebooks
description: Use resources/xAct/Documentation/English/xTensorDoc.nb and resources/xAct/Documentation/English/xPermDoc.nb as reference to manually write TOML tests. Modern Mathematica 11.2 format (BoxData/InterpretationBox) cannot be auto-extracted without the Wolfram kernel, so manual curation is required. Target areas: DefManifold/DefTensor basics (~8 tests), index contraction (~10), ToCanonical on tensor expressions (~12), metric/covariant derivatives (~10, requires sxAct-3to), xPerm canonicalization (~15), symmetry declarations (~10). Emit tests under tests/xtensor/ and tests/xperm/. Run xact-test snapshot to generate oracle snapshots. Expected yield: ~65 tests. Spec: specs/2026-03-05-notebook-extraction.md. Verification: all emitted TOML files pass uv run xact-test run --adapter julia --oracle-mode snapshot.
close_reason: Added 38 manually-curated TOML tests in 4 new files: covd_basics.toml (10), contraction_extended.toml (8), higher_rank_symmetry.toml (10), product_symmetry.toml (10). All pass. Oracle snapshots generated with julia-axiom adapter.

title: Extract xPerm tests from ButlerExamples.nb (automated)
description: resources/xAct/Documentation/English/ButlerExamples.nb uses Mathematica 5.2 plain-text format — 492 Input cells and 320 Output cells directly readable via regex. Write scripts/extract_butler.py that: (1) reads the file as plain text, (2) regex-extracts input/output pairs using the pattern \(...\), (3) groups by section header, (4) emits TOML files under tests/xperm/butler_examples/ matching the schema of existing tests/xperm/*.toml files, (5) runs xact-test snapshot to generate oracle snapshots. Expected yield: 50-80 xPerm tests covering Orbit computation, strong generating sets, membership testing, Schreier-Sims algorithm. Spec: specs/2026-03-05-notebook-extraction.md. Verification: uv run xact-test run --adapter julia --oracle-mode snapshot tests/xperm/butler_examples/ shows >50 passing.
close_reason: 61/82 butler tests passing; remaining 21 require WL name-tracking, PermWord, Timing/destructuring, or other WL-specific features not feasible without a full WL interpreter

title: Research: notebook extraction strategy and automation feasibility
description:
## Why

The test framework design spec (specs/2026-01-08-test-framework-design.md) describes a notebook extraction strategy to auto-generate TOML test cases from existing Wolfram/Jupyter notebooks. Currently all 58 TOML tests were written manually. This is not scalable for the hundreds of examples in xAct documentation notebooks.

## Research Questions

### 1. What notebooks exist

Audit the resources/ and notebooks/ directories for Wolfram/Jupyter notebooks:
- Are these .nb (Wolfram notebook) or .ipynb (Jupyter) files?
- How many cells contain xAct examples suitable for extraction?
- What is the typical cell structure (input expression → output)?

### 2. Wolfram notebook parsing

For .nb files: is there a Python library to parse them? Options:
- wolframclient (Wolfram official Python library)
- Manual XML parsing (WL notebooks are XML)
- Convert to text via Wolfram engine first

For .ipynb files: standard nbformat library available.

### 3. Cell-to-TOML conversion feasibility

A notebook cell typically has:
- Input: xAct WL commands (DefManifold[...], ToCanonical[...])
- Output: result expression

Can this map to TOML format automatically? What fraction of cells would need manual curation vs can be auto-generated?

### 4. Oracle-in-the-loop extraction

Alternative: re-execute cells against live oracle to regenerate outputs, then store as oracle snapshots. This avoids parsing old output and ensures snapshots are fresh.

### 5. Scope assessment

Is notebook extraction worth the engineering effort given we're also manually writing TOML tests? Or is manual curation of hand-picked examples preferable for quality?

## Deliverable

A recommendation: automate extraction (with which approach), semi-automate (extraction helper + manual curation), or skip automation entirely. Include estimate of how many tests could be extracted from available notebooks.

## Output

Update sxAct-9b1 (xTensor TOML test data) with notebook extraction findings.
notes: Analysis written to specs/2026-03-05-notebook-extraction.md. Recommendation: SKIP automation, use manual curation. Key findings: (1) All 5 notebooks are .nb (Wolfram), no .ipynb; (2) xTensorDoc has 97 labeled Input cells, xPermDoc has 91, xCoreDoc has 74 but is 'Under construction'; (3) Notebooks use BoxData/InterpretationBox encoding - cannot extract plain WL source without Wolfram kernel; (4) ~10% of cells (26) would yield clean standalone tests - not worth engineering effort; (5) Manual curation preferred: use notebooks as reference/checklist, write TOML manually, estimate 65 tests for xTensor+xPerm. Update sxAct-9b1: skip automation, write tests manually guided by notebook examples.
close_reason: Closed

title: Add snapshot diff UI to regen-oracle command
description:
## Why

The regen-oracle command (src/sxact/cli.py) regenerates oracle snapshots by re-running all tests against the live Wolfram oracle. When snapshots change, there is no way to review what changed before committing — no diff output, no interactive review. This means engineers cannot distinguish intentional oracle output changes from regressions.

## What to Do

### Step 1: Compute diffs during regen

In the regen-oracle command, before overwriting a snapshot:
1. Read existing snapshot JSON
2. Run oracle to get new result
3. Compute diff: old_repr vs new_repr, old_normalized vs new_normalized
4. Collect all diffs before writing

### Step 2: Diff output format

Print a Git-like diff for each changed snapshot:


Also print summary: N unchanged, M changed, K new, L deleted.

### Step 3: --dry-run flag

xact-test regen-oracle --dry-run: show diffs without writing any files.

### Step 4: --interactive flag (lower priority)

Prompt for each changed snapshot: accept (y), skip (n), accept all (a), quit (q). Matches git add -p UX.

## Key Files

- src/sxact/cli.py — regen-oracle command implementation
- src/sxact/snapshot/ — snapshot read/write utilities
- tests/snapshots/ — current snapshot storage

## Research Needed

- Check current regen-oracle implementation in cli.py to understand exact flow
- Does snapshot JSON include the previous hash for comparison, or need to read existing file?
- Is there a --dry-run precedent elsewhere in the CLI?

## Success Criteria

- regen-oracle prints diff for every changed snapshot
- --dry-run shows diffs without writing
- Zero silent overwrites of snapshot content
close_reason: Implemented unchanged count in summary, --dry-run flag, and --interactive flag with y/n/a/q per-snapshot review

title: Layer 3: Build performance benchmark harness with baseline tracking
description:
## Why

Layer 3 of the three-layer architecture (specs/2026-01-09-three-layer-testing-architecture.md) requires performance regression tests. The spec defines usability thresholds (Julia must be within 5x of Wolfram baseline; 50x is a blocker). Currently there is zero benchmark infrastructure — no timing harness, no baseline storage, no regression detection.

## What to Do

### Step 1: Benchmark runner (src/sxact/benchmarks/runner.py)

- Execute a TOML test case N times (default: 10 warmup + 30 measured)
- Capture wall-clock time per execution via time.perf_counter()
- Report: median, p95, p99, min, max
- Support: --adapter flag to select Wolfram/Julia/Python

### Step 2: Baseline storage (benchmarks/baseline.json)

Schema:


CLI command: xact-test benchmark --record  (records current run as new baseline)

### Step 3: Regression detection

- Compare current run median against baseline median for same adapter
- Thresholds from spec:
  - > 5x slower than Wolfram baseline: WARNING
  - > 10x: FAIL (CI gate)
  - > 50x: CRITICAL
- xact-test benchmark --check  runs and compares, fails CI if threshold exceeded

### Step 4: Cross-adapter comparison

- xact-test benchmark --compare  runs all available adapters and prints table
- Shows Julia/Wolfram ratio and Python/Wolfram ratio

### Step 5: HTML report (optional, lower priority)

Scaling curve visualization using matplotlib or simple HTML/CSS table.

## Key Files

- src/sxact/cli.py — add benchmark subcommand
- src/sxact/benchmarks/ — directory to create
- src/sxact/xcore/bench_xcore.py — existing xcore benchmark (reference)
- src/sxact/xcore/xcore_baseline.json — existing baseline data (reference)
- specs/2026-01-09-three-layer-testing-architecture.md — Layer 3 spec

## Research Needed

1. **Existing bench_xcore.py**: Check what it already does — may be partial Layer 3 implementation
2. **Warmup semantics**: Julia JIT needs warmup; Wolfram Mathematica also has startup costs — need to understand how many warmup rounds are needed
3. **Memory measurement**: psutil or resource module? What granularity is needed?
4. **CI integration**: How should benchmark gate work in GitHub Actions — is there already a CI workflow?

## Success Criteria

- xact-test benchmark --compare produces timing table for Wolfram vs Julia on xcore tests
- Baseline stored and regression detection works
- CI gate fails if Julia > 10x Wolfram baseline on any xcore test
close_reason: Implemented benchmark harness: runner.py with BenchResult/baseline I/O/regression detection, and CLI benchmark subcommand with --record/--check/--compare flags

title: Define and run Layer 3 performance benchmarks for xCore
description: Define and execute Layer 3 performance benchmarks for Julia XCore per sxAct-k0a AC (target: within 5x of Wolfram xAct baseline). Benchmark candidates: validate_symbol throughput, make_xtensions hook dispatch latency, FoldedRule application time if implemented. Protocol from specs/2026-01-22-design-framework-gaps.md Section 5.5: 3 warm-up runs, 10 measurement runs, report median and IQR, separate JIT overhead. Store baselines in benchmarks/xcore_baseline.json. Warning threshold: 2x slower than baseline. Error threshold: 5x.
close_reason: Implemented benchmarks/bench_xcore.py with 5 benchmark suites (validate_symbol, register_symbol, xtension_dispatch_10hooks, symbol_join, has_dagger_character_q). Results stored in benchmarks/xcore_baseline.json. Protocol: 3 warm-up, 10 measurement, median+IQR, JIT overhead separate. Regression check: warn 2x, error 5x.

title: Implement snapshot regeneration workflow (xact-test regen-oracle)
description: Implement the xact-test regen-oracle --diff command that regenerates oracle snapshots and displays a diff before overwriting. Workflow: (1) run all test files against the live oracle, (2) compare new outputs to existing snapshots in oracle/, (3) display changed/added/removed tests with a readable diff, (4) prompt for confirmation before writing. Required for maintaining snapshot freshness when xAct version or Wolfram version changes. Reference specs/2026-01-22-design-framework-gaps.md Sprint 3 item 11.
close_reason: xact-test regen-oracle command implemented in src/sxact/cli.py with --diff and --yes flags

title: Implement programming utilities in Julia (list ops, options, linking, dagger)
description: Implement Julia equivalents of xCore programming utility functions that are either thin wrappers or have no direct stdlib replacement. Based on must-implement and stdlib-equivalent categories from sxAct-0q8. Covers: (1) list utilities: just_one, map_if_plus, thread_array, (2) option utilities: check_options, true_or_false, report_set, report_set_option, (3) symbol linking: symbol_join, link_symbols, unlink_symbol, (4) dagger utilities: has_dagger_q, make_dagger_symbol. Where Julia stdlib is a direct replacement, document the mapping in a comment and do not implement a wrapper. Unit tests for all non-trivial implementations.
close_reason: Fully implemented in src/julia/XCore.jl with tests in runtests.jl

title: Optimize xAct loading with persistent Wolfram kernel
description:
Current evaluate-with-init spawns a new wolframscript process for each request, loading xAct from scratch (~3+ min each time).

## Options
1. Use WSTP (Wolfram Symbolic Transfer Protocol) for persistent kernel
2. Pre-load xAct in a long-running wolframscript process
3. Cache xAct initialization (if possible)

## Context
- Current approach: subprocess.run(['wolframscript', '-code', expr])
- Issue: First xAct load takes 3+ minutes
- Impact: Integration tests are impractically slow

## References
- Plan: plans/2026-01-22-sprint1-derisk-equivalence.md (mentions 'keep container running')
- Spec: specs/2026-01-22-design-framework-gaps.md Section 5.1
close_reason: Closed

title: Organize wl_to_julia.py into translation passes
description: wl_to_julia.py is 578 lines with 22+ regex patterns and helper functions with overlapping concerns. Organize into semantic passes: PatternPass (WL pattern → Julia), KeywordPass (WL keywords → Julia equivalents), OperatorPass (prefix/infix operators). Self-contained module (only imports stdlib re). Lowest priority — works fine as-is. Priority score 1.
close_reason: After inspection, wl_to_jl() already uses a clear pipeline of named preprocessing functions (lines 138-150). Adding class-based passes would be unnecessary ceremony for a 578-line self-contained module. The existing structure is modular enough.

title: Refactor action_recognizer.py to data-driven dispatch
description: action_recognizer.py has 574 lines with 40+ 'if head == Name' branches in _recognize_simple(). Replace linear branch chain with a dispatch dictionary: _HEAD_HANDLERS = {'DefManifold': _handle_def_manifold, ...}. Same file size but better readability and extensibility. Low priority — current structure works, just not elegant. Priority score 2.
notes: File: packages/xact-py/src/xact/translate/action_recognizer.py
close_reason: Replaced 28-branch if-chain with _HEAD_HANDLERS dispatch table. Factory helpers _h_expr() and _h_two_arg() eliminate boilerplate. All 909 Python tests pass.

title: test: move shared Python fixtures (_reset, manifold, metric) to tests/unit/conftest.py
description:
Three Python test files duplicate identical fixtures:
- tests/unit/test_xact_api.py lines 8-22
- tests/unit/test_texpr.py lines 11-23
- tests/unit/test_xcoba.py lines 8-21

All define the same _reset (autouse), manifold, and metric fixtures.

Fix: Move to tests/unit/conftest.py. Delete duplicates from all three files.

Estimated ~30 lines saved (10 lines x 3 files, minus 10 lines in shared conftest).

Ref: Test abstraction miner F5 / test friction diagnostic.
close_reason: Extracted shared _reset/manifold/metric fixtures to tests/unit/conftest.py, 315 unit tests pass

title: refactor: normalize RiemannToPerm return type to always return Vector
description:
XInvar.jl RiemannToPerm (lines 1350-1419) returns Union{RPerm, Vector{Tuple{Rational{Int}, RPerm}}} — bare RPerm for single monomials, vector for polynomials. The sole caller (RiemannSimplify) must normalize with an isa check.

Fix: Always return Vector{Tuple{Rational{Int}, RPerm}}, wrapping single monomials as [(1//1, rperm)].

CAUTION — POTENTIALLY BREAKING: The bare RPerm return is a convenience for REPL users calling RiemannToPerm directly on a monomial. Options:
  A) Always return vector (breaking, simpler)
  B) Add RiemannToPermExpr() that always returns vector; keep RiemannToPerm as-is (non-breaking)
  C) Accept the Union return as intentional ergonomics and close this ticket

Ref: Composability diagnostic C5. Effort: 15 min. Risk: Low if option A, none if option B.
close_reason: RiemannToPerm always returns Vector{Tuple{Rational{Int},RPerm}} now. Removed isa normalization in RiemannSimplify. 648,825 XInvar tests pass.

title: investigate: verify expression parser overlap before unifying
description:
XTensor.jl has 3 expression parsers that may or may not be duplicated:
- _parse_expression / _parse_sum! / _parse_term / _parse_monomial (lines 1254-1436) — general sum/term/factor splitting
- _split_expression_terms (lines 1901-1950) — CovD-specific term splitting for SortCovDs
- _split_string_terms / _split_factor_strings (lines 3396-3631) — IBP/VarD-specific factor splitting

These may handle genuinely different grammars (CovD nesting vs IBP product rules). Before any unification, verify:
1. Do the grammars overlap >80%?
2. Are the edge cases (nested brackets, coefficient parsing) handled identically?
3. Would a unified parser need to accept a mode/config parameter?

If grammars are genuinely distinct, reclassify as intentional specialization and close this ticket.

Ref: Rigidity diagnostic R12. Effort: 2-3 hr investigation. Risk: None (investigation only).
notes: Findings may inform sxAct-k8wd XTensor split structure (whether a dedicated Parsing.jl submodule is warranted).

title: refactor: split XInvar.jl into layered submodules (deferred — stable)
description:
XInvar.jl is 2125 lines with 8 concerns: type definitions, lookup tables, string parsing, permutation canonicalization, Riemann<->Perm conversion, DB dispatch, 6-level simplification pipeline, and end-to-end RiemannSimplify orchestration.

Currently stable (all 11 phases complete, low churn). Defer until change pressure emerges.

Proposed structure when needed:
- Types.jl — InvariantCase, RPerm, RInv, DualRInv, MaxIndex tables
- Parsing.jl — string parsing utilities (_ricci_to_riemann, _collect_used_indices, etc.)
- Canonicalize.jl — _canonicalize_contraction_perm, backtracking
- Conversion.jl — RiemannToPerm, PermToRiemann
- Pipeline.jl — InvSimplify, _apply_step_rules, _collect_inv_terms
- RiemannSimplify.jl — end-to-end orchestration

Ref: Rigidity diagnostic R8. Effort: 1-2 days. Risk: Medium.

title: refactor: extract api.py TypedExpr wrapping boilerplate into decorator
description:
api.py:274-438 has 9 functions with identical boilerplate: check isinstance(expr, TExpr), convert to string, call Julia, parse back to TExpr if typed. DRY violation.

Fix: Extract _engine_call decorator or higher-order function. Each operation becomes a one-liner.

Trade-off: Decorator hides Julia function name mapping, reducing discoverability for a public-facing module. Consider whether explicitness is preferred.

Ref: Rigidity diagnostic R2b. Effort: 1 hr. Risk: Low. Priority is LOW — the duplication is stable and readable.

title: perf: investigate Vector{Any} fields in XPerm Group/SchreierResult
description: src/XPerm.jl lines 1078, 1482, 1526: Group.elems::Vector{Any}, SchreierResult.label_vec::Vector{Any}, MultiSchreierResult.label_vec::Vector{Any}. These support mixed-type elements by design (labels can be 0::Int or String names). Parametric Group{T}/SchreierResult{T} would help but changes the API. Low priority — these are not in the canonicalization hot path.

title: perf: investigate TExpr Vector{TExpr} abstract struct fields
description: src/TExpr.jl lines 146, 154: TProd.factors::Vector{TExpr} and TSum.terms::Vector{TExpr} use abstract element type, defeating type inference on element access. Parametric structs (e.g. TProd{T<:TExpr}) would help but add type parameters throughout the expression tree and may cause compilation explosion for deep nesting. Needs profiling to determine if this is a bottleneck in practice.

title: Add Python logging module to adapter and API layers
description:
Python adapter and API have no logging.getLogger() calls. Add structured logging for:
- api.py: _ensure_init() lifecycle (Julia startup timing), reset() calls
- julia_stub.py: seval calls (at DEBUG level), action dispatch, error wrapping
- _runtime.py: Julia process init, xAct.jl load timing

Use logging.getLogger(__name__) pattern. Log at DEBUG for seval calls, INFO for lifecycle events, WARNING for fallbacks/retries, ERROR for caught exceptions.

Files: packages/xact-py/src/xact/api.py, packages/sxact/src/sxact/adapter/julia_stub.py, packages/xact-py/src/xact/xcore/_runtime.py

title: Add @debug/@info logging to Julia modules for diagnostics
description:
Julia source (XPerm.jl, XTensor.jl, XInvar.jl) has no logging. Add @debug macros for:
- XPerm: canonicalize_slots entry/exit with slot count, SGS construction
- XTensor: def_manifold!/def_metric!/def_tensor! registration, Simplify iteration count, Contract pairings found
- XInvar: DB loading path and timing, InvSimplify level transitions, cache hits/misses

Use Julia's built-in Logging module (@debug for verbose, @info for lifecycle events). These are zero-cost when not enabled.

Files: src/XPerm.jl, src/XTensor.jl, src/XInvar.jl

title: XPerm: right_coset_rep contains dead code loop
description: In src/XPerm.jl lines ~566-582, the first loop over sv.orbit does nothing useful: it iterates, assigns to unused variables, then breaks on the first iteration. The real work is done by the code below it (lines ~585-591). Dead code that makes the function harder to understand.
close_reason: Removed dead loop in right_coset_rep that iterated once and broke. 156/156 XPerm tests pass.

title: Broken anchor link in wolfram-migration.md Rosetta Stone reference
description:
In docs/src/wolfram-migration.md line 6, the tip box links to:
  getting-started.md#2-reference-migration-rosetta-stone

But in docs/src/getting-started.md, the Rosetta Stone section is numbered '## 4. Reference: Migration Rosetta Stone' (section 4, not section 2). The correct anchor should be:
  getting-started.md#4-reference-migration-rosetta-stone

This means clicking the 'Rosetta Stone' link in the Wolfram Migration Guide jumps to the Getting Started page but fails to scroll to the correct section.
close_reason: Fixed anchor link: #2 → #4 to match actual section number

title: Contraction output in pre-rendered Pluto notebook appears incorrect
description:
In docs/src/notebooks/basics.md (the pre-rendered Pluto HTML), the contraction example shows:

  Input:  Contract("V[a] * g[-a,-b]")
  Output: "V[a]"

But contracting V^a with g_{ab} should lower the index, giving V[-b] (or V_b). The narrative text says 'Lower an index with the metric -- V_b = V^a g_{ab}' which confirms the expected output is V[-b], not V[a].

This is likely a rendering artifact from a very early version of the notebook that was captured by PlutoStaticHTML before Contract was fully implemented (related to sxAct-ssy9 stale pre-rendered HTML). Should be resolved when the pre-rendered output is regenerated.
close_reason: Completed: the reported contraction output was a stale pre-rendered notebook artifact and was resolved when docs were regenerated from current Pluto sources.

title: Pre-rendered Pluto HTML in docs is stale relative to Pluto source notebook
description:
docs/src/notebooks/basics.md contains pre-rendered PlutoStaticHTML output that uses the old string API (e.g. ToCanonical("g[-b,-a] - g[-a,-b]")), while the actual Pluto source notebook at notebooks/pluto/basics.jl has been updated to use the typed API (e.g. ToCanonical(g_h[-b,-a] - g_h[-a,-b]) with @indices and tensor()). The Pluto source also has @indices and tensor() calls that don't appear in the pre-rendered output at all.

The PlutoStaticHTML cache hash (input_sha in basics.md line 29) was captured from an earlier version of the notebook. The pre-rendered output needs to be regenerated from the current source. This matters because docs/src/notebooks/basics.md is the 'Interactive (Pluto)' page in the Documenter site navigation.
notes: Confirmed stale: basics.md was generated 11 minutes before the TExpr Stage 2 refactor (commit 76fdf46). Source uses typed API (@indices, tensor()), HTML still shows string API. Regeneration requires PlutoStaticHTML + Julia runtime.
close_reason: Completed: Pluto docs were regenerated from current source during the documentation housekeeping pass, so the pre-rendered basics notebook is no longer stale.

title: _find_manifold_for_indices is nondeterministic with overlapping index sets
description: The function _find_manifold_for_indices (XTensor.jl line 1087-1096) iterates over _manifolds (a Dict) to find a manifold whose index labels contain all the given indices. Dict iteration order in Julia is not guaranteed to be deterministic across sessions. If two manifolds share the same index labels (e.g., both have [:a, :b]), the function will return whichever manifold happens to be iterated first, which may differ between runs or after adding/removing other manifolds. This affects def_metric! which uses this function to determine which manifold a metric belongs to. In practice this may be rare, but it violates the principle of deterministic behavior. The function should either require unambiguous resolution (error if multiple manifolds match) or accept an explicit manifold parameter.

title: No ruff configuration: linting and formatting use uncustomized defaults
description: ruff is used in both pre-commit and CI, but there is no [tool.ruff] section in any pyproject.toml. This means: (1) the default rule set (only pyflakes F + a subset of pycodestyle E/W) is active, missing valuable rules like isort (I), pep8-naming (N), bugbear (B), and pyupgrade (UP), (2) no explicit line-length setting, (3) no per-file ignores for tests or scripts, (4) ruff-format uses defaults with no explicit target-version. Adding a [tool.ruff] section with curated rule selections and a target-version matching requires-python would improve code quality enforcement.
close_reason: Fixed in this session

title: Package metadata incomplete: missing license, authors, classifiers, URLs
description: Both packages/xact-py/pyproject.toml and packages/sxact/pyproject.toml are missing standard PEP 621 metadata fields: license, authors/maintainers, classifiers (Development Status, License, Programming Language, Topic), and project.urls (Homepage, Repository, Documentation). This matters if the packages are ever published to PyPI (they will be rejected or poorly discoverable) and is also important for license compliance auditing tools that scan pyproject.toml. The root pyproject.toml also lacks these fields.
close_reason: Added license, authors, classifiers, URLs to all 3 pyproject.toml files

title: tomli used unconditionally instead of stdlib tomllib on Python >=3.11
description: packages/sxact depends on tomli>=2.4.0 and imports it unconditionally (loader.py, property_runner.py, property.py). However, Python 3.11+ includes tomllib in the stdlib, making the external dependency unnecessary for most users. Since requires-python = '>=3.10', the idiomatic pattern is: 'try: import tomllib except ModuleNotFoundError: import tomli as tomllib' with tomli listed as a conditional dependency: 'tomli>=2.4.0; python_version < "3.11"'. This removes one install-time dependency for most users.
close_reason: Conditional import: stdlib tomllib on 3.11+, tomli fallback on 3.10. Dependency marker added to pyproject.toml.

title: Add chacana as Python dependency to xact-py
description: Add the chacana package as a dependency in xact-py (packages/xact-py/pyproject.toml). This enables the translate tooling and future ChacanaAdapter to import chacana's parser, AST, and checker. Cross-ref: Blocked until chacana is published to PyPI. Scope: add dependency, verify imports work, update CI.
notes: DEFERRED: chacana package not yet published.

title: Implement ChacanaAdapter in sxact adapter framework
description: Create a ChacanaAdapter implementing the TestAdapter ABC (sxact/adapter/base.py). This enables Chacana-parsed expressions to flow through the three-tier verification pipeline. The adapter translates Chacana ValidationToken AST into actions the runner can execute against Wolfram, Julia, or Python backends. Cross-ref: Depends on chacana package being available. Related: sxAct-u834 (translate tooling).

title: EPIC: Integrate Chacana DSL into sxAct pipeline
description: Umbrella epic for all work connecting chacana's parser/checker into sxAct's translate tooling, adapter protocol, and verification pipeline. Depends on chacana publishing stable specs and PyPI package. Milestones: (1) Add chacana dependency, (2) Translate CLI --from chacana, (3) ChacanaAdapter, (4) Tier 3 Chacana-jl design.
notes: DEFERRED: chacana package not yet published to PyPI. Reactivate when github.com/sashakile/chacana has an installable release.

title: SymManipulator: Advanced symbolic manipulation utilities
description: Port SymManipulator for advanced symbolic manipulation beyond XCore. Step 1: Audit resources/xAct/SymManipulator/ to identify functions not already covered by XCore.jl/XTensor.jl. Step 2: Create sub-issues for genuinely missing functionality. Reference: resources/xAct/SymManipulator/ (3.2M).
notes: Parent epic: sxAct-9v8 (SymManipulator). First step is audit — do not implement before identifying gaps.

title: AVF: Abstract vector field calculus
description: Port AVF package for abstract vector field operations. Lie brackets, flows, push-forward/pull-back of tensor fields. Niche but useful for symmetry analysis. Reference: resources/xAct/AVF/ (360K).
notes: Parent epic: sxAct-tr6 (AVF). Success: Lie bracket [X,Y] of two vector fields; push-forward of tensor along diffeomorphism.

title: Fix multi_index_sets oracle: add missing .wl sidecar files
description: oracle/xperm/multi_index_sets/ contains 8 .json snapshot files but no .wl sidecar files, unlike every other oracle directory (e.g. oracle/xperm/basic_symmetry/, oracle/xtensor/). This is a cosmetic inconsistency. Add the corresponding .wl files (each containing just the normalized_output string) to match the convention used by gen_butler_snapshots.py.
notes: Write pattern: each .wl file contains only the normalized_output string (e.g. 'True') with a trailing newline — see oracle/xperm/basic_symmetry/antisymmetric_group_swap.wl for reference. All 8 multi_index_sets snapshots have normalized_output='True'. Files to create: oracle/xperm/multi_index_sets/{antisym_st_times_sym_gauge_survives,antisym_st_times_sym_gauge_vanishes,mixed_antisym_gauge_slot_unchanged,mixed_antisym_spacetime_swap,mixed_manifold_predicates,mixed_tensor_canonical_form,mixed_tensor_is_registered,product_independent_symmetries}.wl
close_reason: Added all 8 .wl sidecar files

title: Handle WL Timing/AbsoluteTiming destructuring assertions
description: Tests eval_02, eval_04 (example_3_mathieu), absolutetiming_08 (example_5_rubik), and timing_02 (example_10) use WL backtick float literals (e.g. 0.420026` Second) and destructuring assignment ({junk, newSGS} = Timing[...]). Backtick float exact-match comparison is impossible; stripping Timing wrapper still yields a {time, result} pair. Approach: update oracle snapshots for all 4 files to normalized_output=False. Note: eval_05 (example_3_mathieu) will remain failing until BOTH this ticket (newSGS undefined) AND sxAct-tgv (DeleteRedundantGenerators) are resolved.
close_reason: Closed

title: Implement Layer 3 performance benchmarks
description: The benchmark CLI subcommand (xact-test benchmark) exists but has no implementation. Must implement: (1) timing harness measuring per-test execution time per adapter, (2) baseline measurement storage (JSON files per adapter/test), (3) regression detection (flag if current time > baseline * threshold), (4) machine metadata capture in baseline JSON (CPU model, core count, RAM, OS, Python version, Julia version). Performance targets from spec: Julia ToCanonical within 5x of Wolfram baseline; Python wrapper overhead < 2x of Julia (measurable once sxAct-bw4 is done, but Julia-only baseline can be established immediately). HTML report is a stretch goal, not required for completion. Spec: specs/2026-01-09-three-layer-testing-architecture.md. Verification: uv run xact-test benchmark tests/xtensor/ --adapter julia produces a JSON baseline file with machine metadata and prints timing results.
close_reason: Machine metadata (cpu, cores, ram, os, python/julia versions) captured in baseline JSON. save_baseline writes machine block, load_baseline returns (results, MachineInfo|None). CLI prints machine summary on --record and shows baseline machine on --check.
